[opReplaceFace](#opReplaceFace-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

This is a direct editing operation that replaces the geometry one or
more faces with that of another face or multiple adjacent faces,
possibly with an offset.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `replaceFaces`    | [`Query`](#Query)     | The faces whose       |
|                       |                       | geometry to replace.  |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `templateFace`    | [`Query`](#Query)     | The face or multiple  |
|                       |                       | adjacent faces whose  |
|                       |                       | geometry to use as    |
|                       |                       | the replacement.      |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `offset`          | [`ValueWithUni        | *Optional*            |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) |                       |
|                       |                       | The positive or       |
|                       |                       | negative distance by  |
|                       |                       | which to offset the   |
|                       |                       | resulting face.       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `oppositeSense`   | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | If true, flip the     |
|                       |                       | surface normal of the |
|                       |                       | resulting face, which |
|                       |                       | may be necessary to   |
|                       |                       | match the surface     |
|                       |                       | normals of            |
|                       |                       | surrounding faces.    |
|                       |                       | Default is `false`.   |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[opRevolve](#opRevolve-Context-Id-map) (context is [Context](#Context),
id is [Id](#Id), definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Revolves edges and faces about an axis to produce sheet and solid
bodies. The edges and faces may abut, but not strictly intersect the
axis.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `entities`        | [`Query`](#Query)     | The edges and faces   |
|                       |                       | to revolve.           |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `axis`            | [`Line`](#Line)       | The axis around which |
|                       |                       | to revolve.           |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `line               |
|                       |                       | (vector(0, 0, 0) * in |
|                       |                       | ch, vector(0, 0, 1))` |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `angleForward`    | [`ValueWithUni        | The angle where the   |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | revolve ends relative |
|                       |                       | to `entities`.        |
|                       |                       | Normalized to the     |
|                       |                       | range \\\[0, 2 PI).   |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `30 * degree`       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `angleBack`       | [`ValueWithUni        | *Optional*            |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) |                       |
|                       |                       | The angle where the   |
|                       |                       | revolve starts        |
|                       |                       | relative to           |
|                       |                       | `entities`.           |
|                       |                       | Normalized to the     |
|                       |                       | range \\\[0, 2 PI).   |
|                       |                       | If                    |
|                       |                       | `angleF               |
|                       |                       | orward == angleBack`, |
|                       |                       | the revolve is a full |
|                       |                       | (360-degree) revolve. |
|                       |                       | Defaults to `0`.      |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[opRuledSurface](#opRuledSurface-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))
returns [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

Creates a ruled surface along a set of paths. Direction of ruled surface
is specified with either `ruledDirection` or `angleFromFaces`.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `path`            | [`Query`](#Query)     | The edges that form   |
|                       |                       | the paths for the     |
|                       |                       | ruled surface.        |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `cornerType`      | [`RuledSu             | *Optional*            |
|                       | rfaceCornerType`](#Ru |                       |
|                       | ledSurfaceCornerType) | How corners in the    |
|                       |                       | ruled surface are     |
|                       |                       | handled. Default is   |
|                       |                       | `RuledSur             |
|                       |                       | faceCornerType.SPUN`. |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `u                | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
| seCubicInterpolation` | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | Ruled surface will    |
|                       |                       | use a cubic           |
|                       |                       | interpolation if      |
|                       |                       | `true`. Otherwise, a  |
|                       |                       | linear interpolation  |
|                       |                       | will be used. Default |
|                       |                       | is `true`.            |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `width`           | [`ValueWithUni        | The width of the      |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | ruled surface.        |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`RuledSurfaceType    | Specifies how the     |
|    `ruledSurfaceType` | `](#RuledSurfaceType) | ruled surface is      |
|                       |                       | constructed.          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `ruledDirection`  | [`Vector`](#Vector)   | *Required if          |
|                       |                       | `ruledSurfaceType` is |
|                       |                       | `                     |
|                       |                       | ALIGNED_WITH_VECTOR`* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | Ruled surface will be |
|                       |                       | aligned with this     |
|                       |                       | vector.               |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `angle`           | [`ValueWithUni        | The angle at which    |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | the ruled surface     |
|                       |                       | meets reference faces |
|                       |                       | or `ruledDirection`.  |
|                       |                       | Default is `0`.       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `referenceFaces`  | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Required if          |
|                       |                       | `ruledSurfaceType` is |
|                       |                       | `ANGLE_FROM_FACE`*    |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | A set of faces from   |
|                       |                       | which to measure      |
|                       |                       | `angleFromFaces`.     |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `vertexOverrides` | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   -   `vertex`      | [`Query`](#Query)     | A vertex on the path  |
|                       |                       | where the override is |
|                       |                       | applied.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`Vector`](#Vector)   | If specified,         |
|  -   `ruledDirection` |                       | override will specify |
|                       |                       | local direction of    |
|                       |                       | ruled surface along   |
|                       |                       | this vector.          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   -   `width`       | [`ValueWithUni        | *Required if          |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | `ruledDirection` !=   |
|                       |                       | undefined or          |
|                       |                       | `angleFromFaces` !=   |
|                       |                       | undefined*            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | Width of ruled        |
|                       |                       | surface at this       |
|                       |                       | override.             |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`ValueWithUni        | If specified,         |
|  -   `angleFromFaces` | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | override will specify |
|                       |                       | direction as an angle |
|                       |                       | to reference faces.   |
|                       |                       | This is only          |
|                       |                       | applicable if         |
|                       |                       | angleFromFaces is     |
|                       |                       | also specified at the |
|                       |                       | top level.            |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   -   `upToEntity`  | [`Query`](#Query)     | If specified,         |
|                       |                       | override will specify |
|                       |                       | that ruled surface    |
|                       |                       | touches upToEntity at |
|                       |                       | override.             |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `                 | [`map`](/FsDo         | An array of positions |
| manipulatorPositions` | c/variables.html#map) | for which local       |
|                       |                       | [`CoordSy             |
|                       |                       | stem`](#CoordSystem)s |
|                       |                       | will be returned.     |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   -   `edge`        | [`Query`](#Query)     | One of the input      |
|                       |                       | edges.                |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   -   `parameter`   | [`number`](/FsDoc/v   | A parameter on the    |
|                       | ariables.html#number) | edge.                 |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

  Return type                              Description
  ---------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  [`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)   An array of local [`CoordSystem`](#CoordSystem)s at the requested positions.

[opShell](#opShell-Context-Id-map) (context is [Context](#Context),
id is [Id](#Id), definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Create a shell of a solid body with uniform thickness. The bodies that
are passed in are hollowed, omitting the walls on the `face` entities
passed in.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `entities`        | [`Query`](#Query)     | The faces to shell    |
|                       |                       | and solid bodies to   |
|                       |                       | hollow.               |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `thickness`       | [`ValueWithUni        | The distance by which |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | to shell. Positive    |
|                       |                       | means shell outward,  |
|                       |                       | and negative means    |
|                       |                       | shell inward.         |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[opSplitPart](#opSplitPart-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Split solid, sheet, and wire bodies with the given sheet body.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `targets`         | [`Query`](#Query)     | The solid, sheet, and |
|                       |                       | wire bodies to split. |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `tool`            |                       | A sheet body, a       |
|                       |                       | construction plane or |
|                       |                       | a face to cut with.   |
|                       |                       | Can be either a       |
|                       |                       | [`Query`](#Query) or  |
|                       |                       | a [`Plane`](#Plane).  |
|                       |                       | If a planar face is   |
|                       |                       | passed in, the split  |
|                       |                       | will extend the plane |
|                       |                       | infinitely unless     |
|                       |                       | `useTrimmed` is       |
|                       |                       | `true`.               |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `keepTools`       | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | If false, the tool is |
|                       |                       | deleted. Default is   |
|                       |                       | `false`.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `keepType`        | [`SplitOp             | *Optional*            |
|                       | erationKeepType`](#Sp |                       |
|                       | litOperationKeepType) | Controls which pieces |
|                       |                       | to keep. Default is   |
|                       |                       | `KEEP_ALL`.           |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `useTrimmed`      | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | If true, the trimmed  |
|                       |                       | face boundaries are   |
|                       |                       | used as the tool,     |
|                       |                       | rather than the       |
|                       |                       | underlying surface.   |
|                       |                       | Default is `false`.   |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[opSplitFace](#opSplitFace-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Split faces with the given edges or faces. Returns an array of edges
where the faces intersect. This includes already existing edges as well
as edges that were created by opSplitFace.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `faceTargets`     | [`Query`](#Query)     | The faces to split.   |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `edgeTools`       | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Optional*            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | The edges to cut      |
|                       |                       | with.                 |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `projectionType`  | [`ProjectionTy        | *Optional*            |
|                       | pe`](#ProjectionType) |                       |
|                       |                       | Edge projection       |
|                       |                       | method. Default is    |
|                       |                       | `ProjectionT          |
|                       |                       | ype.NORMAL_TO_TARGET` |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `direction`       | [`Vector`](#Vector)   | *Required if          |
|                       |                       | `edgeTools` are       |
|                       |                       | present and           |
|                       |                       | `projectionType` is   |
|                       |                       | `Projec               |
|                       |                       | tionType.DIRECTION`.* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | The projection        |
|                       |                       | direction.            |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `faceTools`       | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Optional*            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | The faces to cut      |
|                       |                       | with.                 |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `bodyTools`       | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Optional*            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | The sheet or wire     |
|                       |                       | bodies to cut with.   |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|    `keepToolSurfaces` | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | If `true`, the        |
|                       |                       | `bodyTools` do not    |
|                       |                       | get consumed by the   |
|                       |                       | operation. Default is |
|                       |                       | `true`.               |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `planeTools`      | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Optional*            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | These planar faces    |
|                       |                       | are treated as        |
|                       |                       | infinite, rather than |
|                       |                       | bounded to the face   |
|                       |                       | extents.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|  `extendToCompletion` | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | if `true`, imprinted  |
|                       |                       | edges are extended to |
|                       |                       | complete split of     |
|                       |                       | faces. Default is     |
|                       |                       | `false`.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `mutualImprint`   | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | if `true` and only    |
|                       |                       | `bodyTools` are       |
|                       |                       | supplied target faces |
|                       |                       | are used to split     |
|                       |                       | faces of `bodyTools`. |
|                       |                       | Default is `false`.   |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[SplitByIsoclineResult](#SplitByIsoclineResult) type

Map containing the results of splitting faces by their isoclines. Some
faces may have been split, others may have been left intact.

Value

Type

Description

`SplitByIsoclineResult`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `steepFaces`

[`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

An array of steep faces.

-   `nonSteepFaces`

[`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

An array of non-steep faces.

-   `boundaryEdges`

[`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

An array of edges at the transition from non-steep faces to steep faces.

[opSplitByIsocline](#opSplitByIsocline-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Split the given `faces` by isocline edges. Each isocline follows a path
along a face with a constant `angle` in the (-90, 90) degree range
(e.g., lines of latitude on a sphere). This `angle` is the face tangent
plane\'s angle with respect to the `direction` with its sign determined
by the dot product of `direction` and the face normal, and is analogous
to the angle used in draft analysis. Depending on the face geometry,
there may be zero, one, or multiple isoclines on each face. The isocline
edges are created as new edges which split the provided `faces`. The
resulting faces are either steep (i.e., the angle is less than the input
`angle`) or non-steep. To instead leave the original faces intact, you
can first extract the faces with opExtractSurface(), and create
isoclines on the resulting surfaces. The isocline edges can be queried
for with [`qCreatedBy`](#qCreatedBy-Id-EntityType). The split
orientation is consistent such that the non-steep faces are always in
\"front\" of the split, and can be reliably queried for with
[`qSplitBy`](#qSplitBy-Id--boolean):

EXAMPLE

> const isoclineEdges = qCreatedBy(id + "splitByIsocline1", EntityType.EDGE);
>     const steepFaces = qSplitBy(id + "splitByIsocline1", EntityType.FACE, true);
>     const nonSteepFaces = qSplitBy(id + "splitByIsocline1", EntityType.FACE, false);
>
> Note that [`qSplitBy`](#qSplitBy-Id--boolean) will return only those
> faces that were split, while the returned
> [`SplitByIsoclineResult`](#SplitByIsoclineResult) will include the
> intact faces as well.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `faces`           | [`Query`](#Query)     | The faces on which to |
|                       |                       | imprint isoclines.    |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `direction`       | [`Vector`](#Vector)   | A reference           |
|                       |                       | direction.            |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `angle`           | [`ValueWithUni        | The isocline angle    |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | with respect to the   |
|                       |                       | direction in the      |
|                       |                       | (-90, 90) degree      |
|                       |                       | range.                |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[SplitBySelfShadowResult](#SplitBySelfShadowResult) type

Map containing the results of splitting bodies by their shadow curves.
Some faces may have been split, others may have been left intact.

Value

Type

Description

`SplitBySelfShadowResult`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `visibleFaces`

[`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

An array of visible faces.

-   `invisibleFaces`

[`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

An array of invisible faces.

[opSplitBySelfShadow](#opSplitBySelfShadow-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Splits the faces of the given `bodies` into visible and invisible
regions with respect to the given `viewDirection`, creating shadow
curves as necessary. A shadow curve represents the transition of one
face from visible to invisible. Depending on the geometry, there may be
zero, one, or more shadow curves per face. The shadow curve edges are
created as new edges which split the faces of the provided `bodies`.
Each of the resulting faces is wholly visible or wholly invisible.
Edge-on faces are considered invisible. The shadow curve edges can be
queried for with [`qCreatedBy`](#qCreatedBy-Id-EntityType). The split
orientation is consistent such that the visible faces are always in
\"front\" of the split, and can be reliably queried for with
[`qSplitBy`](#qSplitBy-Id--boolean):

EXAMPLE

> const shadowEdges = qCreatedBy(id + "splitBySelfShadow1", EntityType.EDGE);
>     const invisibleFaces = qSplitBy(id + "splitBySelfShadow1", EntityType.FACE, true);
>     const visibleFaces = qSplitBy(id + "splitBySelfShadow1", EntityType.FACE, false);
>
> Note that [`qSplitBy`](#qSplitBy-Id--boolean) will return only those
> faces that were split, while the returned
> [`SplitBySelfShadowResult`](#SplitBySelfShadowResult) will include the
> intact faces as well.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `bodies`          | [`Query`](#Query)     | The bodies which cast |
|                       |                       | shadows and on which  |
|                       |                       | to imprint shadow     |
|                       |                       | curves.               |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `viewDirection`   | [`Vector`](#Vector)   | The viewing           |
|                       |                       | direction.            |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[opSweep](#opSweep-Context-Id-map) (context is [Context](#Context),
id is [Id](#Id), definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Sweep the given edges and faces along a path resulting in solid and/or
sheet bodies.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `profiles`        | [`Query`](#Query)     | Edges and faces to    |
|                       |                       | sweep.                |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `path`            | [`Query`](#Query)     | Edges that comprise   |
|                       |                       | the path along which  |
|                       |                       | to sweep. The edges   |
|                       |                       | can be in any order   |
|                       |                       | but must form a       |
|                       |                       | connected path.       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `ke               | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
| epProfileOrientation` | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | If `true`, the        |
|                       |                       | profile maintains its |
|                       |                       | original orientation  |
|                       |                       | as it is swept. If    |
|                       |                       | `false` (default),    |
|                       |                       | the profile rotates   |
|                       |                       | to remain normal to   |
|                       |                       | the path.             |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `lockFaces`       | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Optional*            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | Keep profile aligned  |
|                       |                       | to the normals of     |
|                       |                       | these faces.          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `lockDirection`   | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Optional*            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | Keep profile          |
|                       |                       | perpendicular to this |
|                       |                       | direction.            |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `profileControl`  | [                     | *Optional*            |
|                       | `ProfileControlMode`] |                       |
|                       | (#ProfileControlMode) | Default is NONE       |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `ProfileControlM    |
|                       |                       | ode.KEEP_ORIENTATION` |
|                       |                       | > the profile         |
|                       |                       | > maintains its       |
|                       |                       | > original            |
|                       |                       | > orientation as it   |
|                       |                       | > is swept.           |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `ProfileContro      |
|                       |                       | lMode.LOCK_DIRECTION` |
|                       |                       | > the profile is      |
|                       |                       | > perpendicular to    |
|                       |                       | > given direction.    |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `ProfileCo          |
|                       |                       | ntrolMode.LOCK_FACES` |
|                       |                       | > the profile is      |
|                       |                       | > aligned to the      |
|                       |                       | > normals of given    |
|                       |                       | > faces.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[opTessellatedLoft](#opTessellatedLoft-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Create a tessellated loft between two edge or vertex profiles.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | A two element array   |
|   `profileSubqueries` | variables.html#array) | containing edge or    |
|                       |                       | vertex profiles to    |
|                       |                       | loft between.         |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`ValueWithUni        | The maximum distance  |
|    `chordalTolerance` | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | a chord can deviate   |
|                       |                       | from the path.        |
|                       |                       | Default is            |
|                       |                       | `0.005 meters`        |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `connections`     | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | *Optional*            |
|                       | variables.html#array) |                       |
|                       |                       | An array of maps to   |
|                       |                       | define multiple       |
|                       |                       | profile alignments.   |
|                       |                       | Each connection map   |
|                       |                       | should contain:       |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | \(1\)                 |
|                       |                       | connectionEntities    |
|                       |                       | query describing an   |
|                       |                       | array of vertices or  |
|                       |                       | edges (one per        |
|                       |                       | profile),             |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | \(2\) connectionEdges |
|                       |                       | an array of           |
|                       |                       | individual queries    |
|                       |                       | for edges in          |
|                       |                       | connectionEntities.   |
|                       |                       | The order of          |
|                       |                       | individual edge       |
|                       |                       | queries should be     |
|                       |                       | synchronized with     |
|                       |                       | conn                  |
|                       |                       | ectionEdgeParameters. |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | \(3\)                 |
|                       |                       | con                   |
|                       |                       | nectionEdgeParameters |
|                       |                       | array - an ordered    |
|                       |                       | and synchronized      |
|                       |                       | array of parameters   |
|                       |                       | on edges in           |
|                       |                       | connectionEdgeQueries |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `modelParameters` | [`map`](/FsDo         | *Optional*            |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`ValueWithUni        | The front thickness   |
|  -   `frontThickness` | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | of the sheet metal.   |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`ValueWithUni        | The back thickness of |
|   -   `backThickness` | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | the sheet metal.      |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   -   `bendRadius`  | [`ValueWithUni        | The bend radius of    |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | the sheet metal.      |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[opThicken](#opThicken-Context-Id-map) (context is [Context](#Context),
id is [Id](#Id), definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Thicken sheet bodies and faces into solid bodies.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |

|                       |                       | faces to thicken.     |

| -   `thickness1`      | [`ValueWithUni        | The distance by which |

|                       |                       | direction along the   |
|                       |                       | normal.               |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `thickness2`      | [`ValueWithUni        | The distance by which |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | to thicken in the     |
|                       |                       | opposite direction.   |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `keepTools`       | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | Default is `true`. If |
|                       |                       | `false` the operation |
|                       |                       | will attempt to       |
|                       |                       | delete the            |
|                       |                       | `entities`. The       |
|                       |                       | operation will not    |
|                       |                       | delete sheet bodies   |
|                       |                       | unless the sheet body |
|                       |                       | or all faces of the   |
|                       |                       | sheet body are        |
|                       |                       | selected. The         |
|                       |                       | operation will not    |
|                       |                       | delete sketches or    |
|                       |                       | solid bodies.         |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[opTransform](#opTransform-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Applies a given transform to one or more bodies. To make transformed
copies of bodies, use [`opPattern`](#opPattern-Context-Id-map).

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `bodies`          | [`Query`](#Query)     | The bodies to         |
|                       |                       | transform.            |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `transform`       | [`Tr                  | The transform to      |
|                       | ansform`](#Transform) | apply.                |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `transform(vec      |
|                       |                       | tor(0, 0, 1) * inch)` |
|                       |                       | > will translate the  |
|                       |                       | > body 1 inch along   |
|                       |                       | > the world\'s        |
|                       |                       | > z-axis.             |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `rotationAround(    |
|                       |                       | myLine, 30 * degree)` |
|                       |                       | > will rotate around  |
|                       |                       | > a [`Line`](#Line)   |
|                       |                       | > object.             |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `sc                 |
|                       |                       | aleUniformly(factor)` |
|                       |                       | > will scale          |
|                       |                       | > uniformly about the |
|                       |                       | > origin.             |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `scaleUnif          |
|                       |                       | ormly(factor, point)` |
|                       |                       | > will scale          |
|                       |                       | > uniformly about a   |
|                       |                       | > given point.        |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `toWorld(cSys)`     |
|                       |                       | > will (somewhat      |
|                       |                       | > counterintuitively) |
|                       |                       | > perform a transform |
|                       |                       | > such that geometry  |
|                       |                       | > on the world\'s     |
|                       |                       | > origin and axes     |
|                       |                       | > will move to the    |
|                       |                       | > `cSys` origin and   |
|                       |                       | > axes.               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `fromWorld(cSys)`   |
|                       |                       | > will (somewhat      |
|                       |                       | > counterintuitively) |
|                       |                       | > perform a transform |
|                       |                       | > such that geometry  |
|                       |                       | > on the `cSys`       |
|                       |                       | > origin and axes     |
|                       |                       | > will move to the    |
|                       |                       | > world origin and    |
|                       |                       | > axes.               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `tra                |
|                       |                       | nsform2 * transform1` |
|                       |                       | > will perform        |
|                       |                       | > `transform1`,       |
|                       |                       | > followed by         |
|                       |                       | > `transform2`.       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[opWrap](#opWrap-Context-Id-map) (context is [Context](#Context),
id is [Id](#Id), definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Wraps or unwraps faces from one surface onto another. The location and
orientation of the wrapped faces on the destination surface are
controlled by the `anchorPoint` and `anchorDirection` of the `source`
and `destination` [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface)s. The `entities` of the
operation are not affected, the result of this operation is a new set of
surface bodies or imprinted edges representing the wrapped or unwrapped
faces. Faces that are topologically connected will remain topologically
connected in the result body for `WrapType.SIMPLE` and `WrapType.TRIM`.
This operation currently supports wrapping from a plane onto a cylinder
or a cone, and unwrapping from a cylinder or a cone onto a plane.

(Formerly `opRoll`)

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `wrapType`        | [`                    | The type of wrap to   |
|                       | WrapType`](#WrapType) | execute.              |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `WrapType.SIMPLE`   |
|                       |                       | > wraps `entities`    |
|                       |                       | > around the infinite |
|                       |                       | > definition of       |
|                       |                       | > `destination`.      |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `entities`        | [`Query`](#Query)     | Faces to wrap from    |
|                       |                       | `source` to           |
|                       |                       | `destination`.        |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `source`          | [`WrapSu              | The surface to wrap   |
|                       | rface`](#WrapSurface) | from. All `entities`  |
|                       |                       | must lie on this      |
|                       |                       | surface.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `destination`     | [`WrapSu              | The surface to wrap   |
|                       | rface`](#WrapSurface) | onto. Must be defined |
|                       |                       | using the `face`      |
|                       |                       | field for             |
|                       |                       | `WrapType.TRIM` or    |
|                       |                       | `WrapType.IMPRINT`.   |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `o                | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
| rientWithDestination` | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | If true (default),    |
|                       |                       | the normals of the    |
|                       |                       | resulting surface     |
|                       |                       | will point in the     |
|                       |                       | same direction as the |
|                       |                       | `destination`. If     |
|                       |                       | false, the normals of |
|                       |                       | the resulting surface |
|                       |                       | will point in the     |
|                       |                       | opposite direction.   |
|                       |                       | For the purpose of    |
|                       |                       | this parameter, the   |
|                       |                       | normals of a          |
|                       |                       | [`WrapSu              |
|                       |                       | rface`](#WrapSurface) |
|                       |                       | defined by an         |
|                       |                       | infinite              |
|                       |                       | [`                    |
|                       |                       | Cylinder`](#Cylinder) |
|                       |                       | are always pointing   |
|                       |                       | outwards.             |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
