[evMaxPathDeviation](#evMaxPathDeviation-Context-map)
(context is [Context](#Context),
arg is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))
returns [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

Returns the max deviation between two paths.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `arg`                 | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `side1`           | [`Query`](#Query)     | Bodies and/or edges   |
|                       |                       | forming a single      |
|                       |                       | continuous path.      |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `side2`           | [`Query`](#Query)     | Bodies and/or edges   |
|                       |                       | forming a single      |
|                       |                       | continuous path.      |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `showDeviation`   | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | If true, will display |
|                       |                       | a magenta comb for    |
|                       |                       | each deviation sample |
|                       |                       | and a red line with a |
|                       |                       | star for the maximum  |
|                       |                       | deviation. Default is |
|                       |                       | `false`.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Return                | Type                  | Description           |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
|                       | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `deviation`       | [`ValueWithUni        | value of the max      |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | deviation between     |
|                       |                       | `side1` and `side2`.  |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `side1Point`      | [`Vector`](#Vector)   | position on `side1`   |
|                       |                       | where `side1` is      |
|                       |                       | `deviation` away from |
|                       |                       | `side2`.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `side2Point`      | [`Vector`](#Vector)   | position on `side2`   |
|                       |                       | where `side2` is      |
|                       |                       | `deviation` away from |
|                       |                       | `side1`.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[evPointsDeviation](#evPointsDeviation-Context-map)
(context is [Context](#Context),
arg is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))
returns [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

Returns the deviation between the input points and the input topologies.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `arg`                 | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `points`          | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | Points to measure the |
|                       | variables.html#array) | deviation from.       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `topologies`      | [`Query`](#Query)     | Bodies                |
|                       |                       | /faces/edges/vertices |
|                       |                       | to measure the        |
|                       |                       | deviation to. The     |
|                       |                       | deviation will be     |
|                       |                       | between each point    |
|                       |                       | and the closest       |
|                       |                       | element of            |
|                       |                       | `topologies`          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `allDeviations`   | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | If true, will return  |
|                       |                       | a deviation value for |
|                       |                       | each point in         |
|                       |                       | `points`. If false,   |
|                       |                       | will only return the  |
|                       |                       | maximum deviation.    |
|                       |                       | Default is `false`.   |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `showDeviation`   | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | If true, show all     |
|                       |                       | deviations/the max    |
|                       |                       | deviation depending   |
|                       |                       | on the value of       |
|                       |                       | `allDeviations`.      |
|                       |                       | Default is `false`.   |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `limit`           | [`ValueWithUni        | *Optional*            |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) |                       |
|                       |                       | Do not show           |
|                       |                       | deviations less than  |
|                       |                       | this value. No effect |
|                       |                       | if `showDeviation` is |
|                       |                       | false. Default is     |
|                       |                       | `0 meter`.            |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

  Return type                              Description
  ---------------------------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  [`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)   If `allDeviations` is true, an array of maps, with one map per element in `points`; if it is false an array of a single map. Each map has a: `deviation` (ValueWithUnits) : The deviation between the given point and the elements of `topologies`. `pointPoint` (Vector) : The position of the given point. `topologyPoint` (Vector) : The closest position in `topologies`.

[evMeshPoints](#evMeshPoints-Context-map)
(context is [Context](#Context),
arg is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))
returns [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

Returns the positions of all the points of the input mesh bodies/faces.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `arg`                 | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `meshes`          | [`Query`](#Query)     | Mesh faces and bodies |
|                       |                       | to evaluate.          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

  Return type                              Description
  ---------------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------------------
  [`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)   An array of positions of all the vertices of the given meshes

## Math

## [approximationUtils](#module-approximationUtils.fs) {#module-approximationUtils.fs .file-header}

[APPROXIMATION_SAMPLES](#APPROXIMATION_SAMPLES) const

Number of sample taken on the curve to perform the approximation.

[MAX_CONTROL_POINTS](#MAX_CONTROL_POINTS) const

Maximum number of control points to approximate the curve with.

[MAX_DEGREE](#MAX_DEGREE) const

Maximum degree of the curve.

[TOLERANCE_BOUND](#TOLERANCE_BOUND) const

A `LengthBoundSpec` for approximation tolerance.

[DEGREE_BOUND](#DEGREE_BOUND) const

An `IntegerBoundSpec` for curve degree.

[curveApproximationPredicate](#curveApproximationPredicate-map)
(definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)) predicate

A predicate to add curve approximation parameters to a feature.

[checkApproximationParameters](#checkApproximationParameters-map-Path)
(definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map), path is [Path](#Path))

Checks approximation options to see if the approximation would succeed
and highlights the issue if not.

[approximateResults](#approximateResults-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Approximates the curves created by the feature id. This meant to be used
along with `curveApproximationPredicate` and it expects `definition` to
have the parameters defined in the predicate.

## [box](#module-box.fs) {#module-box.fs .file-header}

This module refers to 3D bounding boxes, e.g. the result of a call to
`evBox3d`.

This is not to be confused with the [box](/FsDoc/variables.html#box)
standard type used for references.

[Box3d](#Box3d) type

A three-dimensional bounding box.

Value

Type

Description

`Box3d`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `minCorner`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

A 3D position representing the corner with the smallest x, y, and z
coordinates.

-   `maxCorner`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

A 3D position representing the corner with the largest x, y, and z
coordinates.

[canBeBox3d](#canBeBox3d-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for [`Box3d`](#Box3d)

[box3d](#box3d-Vector-Vector) (minCorner is [Vector](#Vector),
maxCorner is [Vector](#Vector)) returns [Box3d](#Box3d)

Construct a bounding box from two opposite corners.

[box3d](#box3d-array)
(pointArray is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array))
returns [Box3d](#Box3d)

Construct a bounding box containing all points in pointArray

[transformBox3d](#transformBox3d-Box3d-Transform)
(boxIn is [Box3d](#Box3d), transformation is [Transform](#Transform))
returns [Box3d](#Box3d)

Return a box aligned with transformed coordinate system containing the
input box

[extendBox3d](#extendBox3d-Box3d-ValueWithUnits-number)
(bBox is [Box3d](#Box3d),
absoluteValue is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits),
factor is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))
returns [Box3d](#Box3d)

Return an enlarged bounding box. The box is scaled by `1 + factor`
around its midpoint, and then each face is moved outward by
`absoluteValue` (inward if `absoluteValue` is negative).

  Parameter         Type                                       Additional Info
  ----------------- ------------------------------------------ ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `absoluteValue`   [`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits)        The absolute distance to move each face of the box. The corners move `sqrt(3)` times as far.
  `factor`          [`number`](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)   The relative amount to expand the box, with `0` leaving it unchanged.

[box3dCenter](#box3dCenter-Box3d) (bBox is [Box3d](#Box3d))
returns [Vector](#Vector)

Return the center of the bounding box.

[box3dDiagonalLength](#box3dDiagonalLength-Box3d)
(bBox is [Box3d](#Box3d)) returns [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits)

Return the length of the diagonal from the `minCorner` to the
`maxCorner` of the bounding box.

[insideBox3d](#insideBox3d-Vector-Box3d) (point is [Vector](#Vector),
bBox is [Box3d](#Box3d)) predicate

Whether the specified point is within the bounding box.

[box3dAllCorners](#box3dAllCorners-Box3d) (bBox is [Box3d](#Box3d))
returns [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

Returns all 8 corners of a Box3d as an array of Vectors.

## [coordSystem](#module-coordSystem.fs) {#module-coordSystem.fs .file-header}

[WORLD_ORIGIN](#WORLD_ORIGIN) const

Position of the world origin, equivalent to `vector(0, 0, 0) * meter`

[X_DIRECTION](#X_DIRECTION) const

Direction parallel to the X axis, equivalent to `vector(1, 0, 0)`

[Y_DIRECTION](#Y_DIRECTION) const

Direction parallel to the Y axis, equivalent to `vector(0, 1, 0)`

[Z_DIRECTION](#Z_DIRECTION) const

Direction parallel to the Z axis, equivalent to `vector(0, 0, 1)`

[WORLD_COORD_SYSTEM](#WORLD_COORD_SYSTEM) const

The world coordinate system, equivalent to
`coordSystem(vector(0, 0, 0) * meter, vector(1, 0, 0), vector(0, 0, 1))`

[CoordSystem](#CoordSystem) type

A right-handed Cartesian coordinate system. Used for converting points
and geometry between different reference frames, or for creating planes
and mate connectors.

The y-axis of a coordinate system is not stored, but it can be obtained
by calling the [yAxis](#yAxis-CoordSystem) function, which simply
performs a cross product.

See also

[`toWorld(CoordSystem)`](#toWorld-CoordSystem)

[`fromWorld(CoordSystem)`](#fromWorld-CoordSystem)

[`coordSystem(Plane)`](#coordSystem-Plane)

[`plane(CoordSystem)`](#plane-CoordSystem)

[`opMateConnector`](#opMateConnector-Context-Id-map)

Value

Type

Description

`CoordSystem`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `origin`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

A 3D point, in world space, representing the origin of the coordinate
system.

-   `xAxis`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

A 3D unit vector, in world space, representing the x-axis of the
coordinate system.

-   `zAxis`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

A 3D unit vector, in world space, representing the z-axis of the
coordinate system. Must be perpendicular to the `xAxis`.

[coordSystem](#coordSystem-Vector-Vector-Vector)
(origin is [Vector](#Vector), xAxis is [Vector](#Vector),
zAxis is [Vector](#Vector)) returns [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem)

Creates a Cartesian coordinate system.

See also

[`CoordSystem`](#CoordSystem)

  Parameter   Type                  Additional Info
  ----------- --------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `origin`    [`Vector`](#Vector)   A 3D point in world space.
  `xAxis`     [`Vector`](#Vector)   A 3D vector in world space. Need not be normalized.
  `zAxis`     [`Vector`](#Vector)   A 3D vector in world space. Need not be normalized but must be orthogonal to xAxis.

[tolerantEquals](#tolerantEquals-CoordSystem-CoordSystem)
(cSys1 is [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem),
cSys2 is [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem)) predicate

Check that two [`CoordSystem`](#CoordSystem)s are the same up to
tolerance.

[toWorld](#toWorld-CoordSystem-Vector)
(cSys is [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem), pointInCSys is [Vector](#Vector))
returns [Vector](#Vector)

Convert a specified point from a specified coordinate system into world
space.

EXAMPLE

> `toWorld(cSys, vector(0, 0, 0))` equals `cSys.origin`

  Parameter       Type                  Additional Info
  --------------- --------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------
  `pointInCSys`   [`Vector`](#Vector)   A 3D vector, measured with respect to the `cSys` provided.

  Return type           Description
  --------------------- -----------------------------
  [`Vector`](#Vector)   A 3D vector in world space.

[toWorld](#toWorld-CoordSystem) (cSys is [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem))
returns [Transform](#Transform)

Returns a [`Transform`](#Transform) which will transform coordinates
measured in `cSys` into world coordinates.

EXAMPLE

> `toWorld(cSys) * vector(0, 0, 0)` equals `cSys.origin`

When used in operations which place or move parts (like
[`opTransform`](#opTransform-Context-Id-map),
[`opPattern`](#opPattern-Context-Id-map), or
[`addInstance`](#addInstance-Instantiator-function-map)), this transform
will (somewhat counterintuitively) move parts from the world origin and
orientation to the `cSys` origin and orientation.

[fromWorld](#fromWorld-CoordSystem-Vector)
(cSys is [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem), worldPoint is [Vector](#Vector))
returns [Vector](#Vector)

Convert a specified point from world space into a specified coordinate
system.

EXAMPLE

> `fromWorld(cSys, cSys.origin)` equals `vector(0, 0, 0)`

  Parameter      Type                  Additional Info
  -------------- --------------------- ---------------------------------------
  `worldPoint`   [`Vector`](#Vector)   A 3D vector, measured in world space.

  Return type           Description
  --------------------- --------------------------------
  [`Vector`](#Vector)   A 3D vector measured in `cSys`

[fromWorld](#fromWorld-CoordSystem)
(cSys is [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem)) returns [Transform](#Transform)

Returns a [`Transform`](#Transform) which will transform coordinates
measured in world space into `cSys` coordinates.

EXAMPLE

> `fromWorld(cSys) * cSys.origin` equals `vector(0, 0, 0)`

When used in operations which place or move parts (like
[`opTransform`](#opTransform-Context-Id-map),
[`opPattern`](#opPattern-Context-Id-map), or
[`addInstance`](#addInstance-Instantiator-function-map)), this transform
will (somewhat counterintuitively) move parts from the `cSys` origin and
orientation to the world origin and orientation.

[scaleNonuniformly](#scaleNonuniformly-number-number-number-CoordSystem)
(xScale is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
yScale is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
zScale is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
cSys is [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem)) returns [Transform](#Transform)

Returns a [`Transform`](#Transform) that represents 3 independent
scalings along the X, Y, and Z axes of a particular `cSys`, centered
around `cSys.origin`.

[yAxis](#yAxis-CoordSystem) (cSys is [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem))
returns [Vector](#Vector)

Returns the y-axis of a coordinate system

  Return type           Description
  --------------------- -----------------------------
  [`Vector`](#Vector)   A 3D vector in world space.

[toString](#toString-CoordSystem) (cSys is [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

Returns a representation of the coordinate system as a string.

## [curveGeometry](#module-curveGeometry.fs) {#module-curveGeometry.fs .file-header}

[X_AXIS](#X_AXIS) const

The global X axis, equivalent to
`line(vector(0, 0, 0) * meter, vector(1, 0, 0))`

[Y_AXIS](#Y_AXIS) const

The global Y axis, equivalent to
`line(vector(0, 0, 0) * meter, vector(0, 1, 0))`

[Z_AXIS](#Z_AXIS) const

The global Z axis, equivalent to
`line(vector(0, 0, 0) * meter, vector(0, 0, 1))`

[Line](#Line) type

Represents a parameterized line in 3D space.

Value

Type

Description

`Line`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `origin`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

A point on the line, as a 3D Vector with length units.

-   `direction`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

A unitless normalized 3D Vector.

[canBeLine](#canBeLine-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for [`Line`](#Line)

[line](#line-Vector-Vector) (origin is [Vector](#Vector),
direction is [Vector](#Vector)) returns [Line](#Line)

Creates a line from a point and a direction.

  Parameter     Type                  Additional Info
  ------------- --------------------- -------------------------------------------------
  `direction`   [`Vector`](#Vector)   The direction gets normalized by this function.

[tolerantEquals](#tolerantEquals-Line-Line) (line1 is [Line](#Line),
line2 is [Line](#Line)) predicate

Check that two [`Line`](#Line)s are the same up to tolerance, including
checking that they have the same origin.

To check if two [`Line`](#Line)s are equivalent (rather than equal), use
[`collinearLines`](#collinearLines-Line-Line).

[collinearLines](#collinearLines-Line-Line) (line1 is [Line](#Line),
line2 is [Line](#Line)) returns [boolean](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)

Returns `true` if the two lines are collinear.

[transform](#transform-Line-Line) (from is [Line](#Line),
to is [Line](#Line)) returns [Transform](#Transform)

Returns the transformation that transforms the line `from` to the line
`to` (including the origin) using the minimum rotation angle.

[project](#project-Line-Vector) (line is [Line](#Line),
point is [Vector](#Vector)) returns [Vector](#Vector)

Returns the projection of the point onto the line. See also other
overloads of `project`.

[rotationAround](#rotationAround-Line-ValueWithUnits)
(line is [Line](#Line), angle is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits))
returns [Transform](#Transform)

Returns a [`Transform`](#Transform) that represents the rotation around
the given line by the given angle. The rotation is counterclockwise
looking against the line direction.

[LineLineIntersection](#LineLineIntersection) type

Represents an intersection between two lines. Depending on the lines,
this intersection may be a point, a line, or nothing.

Value

Type

Description

`LineLineIntersection`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `dim`

[`number`](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

Integer representing the dimension of the intersection.

EXAMPLE

> `0` indicates that `intersection` is a 3D length [`Vector`](#Vector).

EXAMPLE

> `1` indicates that `intersection` is a [`Line`](#Line). (i.e. the
> lines are collinear)

EXAMPLE

> `-1` indicates that the intersection does not exist.

-   `intersection`

`undefined` or [`Vector`](#Vector) or [`Line`](#Line) (depending on
`dim`) that represents the intersection.

[canBeLineLineIntersection](#canBeLineLineIntersection-) (value)
predicate

Typecheck for [`LineLineIntersection`](#LineLineIntersection)

[intersection](#intersection-Line-Line) (line1 is [Line](#Line),
line2 is [Line](#Line))
returns [LineLineIntersection](#LineLineIntersection)

Returns a [`LineLineIntersection`](#LineLineIntersection) representing
the intersection between two lines. If the lines are collinear, `line1`
will be stored in the `intersection` field of that
`LineLineIntersection`.

[isPointOnLine](#isPointOnLine-Vector-Line) (point is [Vector](#Vector),
line is [Line](#Line)) returns [boolean](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)

Returns true if the point lies on the line.

[toString](#toString-Line) (value is [Line](#Line))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

[Circle](#Circle) type

Represents a circle in 3D space.

Value

Type

Description

`Circle`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `coordSystem`

[`CoordSystem`](#CoordSystem)

The circle lies in the xy plane of this coordinate system and the origin
of its parameterization is the x axis.

-   `radius`

[`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits)

The radius of the circle.

[canBeCircle](#canBeCircle-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for [`Circle`](#Circle)

[circle](#circle-CoordSystem-ValueWithUnits)
(cSys is [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem),
radius is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits)) returns [Circle](#Circle)

Returns a new `Circle` in the given coordinate system `cSys`.

[circle](#circle-Vector-Vector-Vector-ValueWithUnits)
(center is [Vector](#Vector), xDirection is [Vector](#Vector),
normal is [Vector](#Vector),
radius is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits)) returns [Circle](#Circle)

Returns a new `Circle` with the given parameters. `xDirection` and
`normal` must be perpendicular.

[tolerantEquals](#tolerantEquals-Circle-Circle)
(circle1 is [Circle](#Circle), circle2 is [Circle](#Circle)) predicate

Check that two `Circle`s are the same up to tolerance, including the
coordinate system.

[toString](#toString-Circle) (value is [Circle](#Circle))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

[Ellipse](#Ellipse) type

Represents an ellipse in 3D space.

Value

Type

Description

`Ellipse`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `coordSystem`

[`CoordSystem`](#CoordSystem)

The ellipse lies in the xy plane of this coordinate system and the x
axis corresponds to the major radius.

-   `majorRadius`

[`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits)

The larger of the two radii.

-   `minorRadius`

[`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits)

The smaller of the two radii.

[canBeEllipse](#canBeEllipse-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for [`Ellipse`](#Ellipse)

[ellipse](#ellipse-CoordSystem-ValueWithUnits-ValueWithUnits)
(cSys is [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem),
majorRadius is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits),
minorRadius is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits))
returns [Ellipse](#Ellipse)

Returns a new `Ellipse` with the given parameters.

[ellipse](#ellipse-Vector-Vector-Vector-ValueWithUnits-ValueWithUnits)
(center is [Vector](#Vector), xDirection is [Vector](#Vector),
normal is [Vector](#Vector),
majorRadius is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits),
minorRadius is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits))
returns [Ellipse](#Ellipse)

Returns a new `Ellipse` with the given parameters. `xDirection` and
`normal` must be perpendicular.

[tolerantEquals](#tolerantEquals-Ellipse-Ellipse)
(ellipse1 is [Ellipse](#Ellipse), ellipse2 is [Ellipse](#Ellipse))
predicate

Check that two `Ellipse`s are the same up to tolerance, including the
coordinate system.

[toString](#toString-Ellipse) (value is [Ellipse](#Ellipse))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

[KnotArray](#KnotArray) type

An array of non-decreasing numbers representing the knots of a spline

[canBeKnotArray](#canBeKnotArray-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for [`KnotArray`](#KnotArray)

[knotArray](#knotArray-array)
(value is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array))
returns [KnotArray](#KnotArray)

Cast an array on non-decreasing numbers to a KnotArray

[knotArrayIsCorrectSize](#knotArrayIsCorrectSize-KnotArray-number-number)
(knots is [KnotArray](#KnotArray),
degree is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
nControlPoints is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)) predicate

Assure that `size(knotArray)` is `1 + degree + nControlPoints`

[BSplineCurve](#BSplineCurve) type

The definition of a spline in 3D or 2D world space, or unitless 2D
parameter space.

See also

[`bSplineCurve(map)`](#bSplineCurve-map)

Value

Type

Description

`BSplineCurve`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `degree`

[`number`](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

The degree of the spline.

-   `dimension`

[`number`](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

The dimension of the spline. Must be 2 or 3.

-   `isRational`

[`boolean`](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)

Whether the spline is rational.

-   `isPeriodic`

[`boolean`](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)

Whether the spline is periodic.

-   `controlPoints`

[`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

An array of control points of the required dimension. Size should be at
least degree + 1. 2D spline control points can have world space units,
or be unitless if they are intended to represent locations in parameter
space (e.g. as a boundary input to
[`opCreateBSplineSurface`](#opCreateBSplineSurface-Context-Id-map)).

-   `weights`

[`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

*Required if `isRational` is `true`*

An array of unitless values with the same size as the control points
array.

-   `knots`

[`KnotArray`](#KnotArray)

An array of non-decreasing knots of size equal to
`1 + degree + size(controlPoints)`

[canBeBSplineCurve](#canBeBSplineCurve-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for [`BSplineCurve`](#BSplineCurve)

[bSplineCurve](#bSplineCurve-map)
(definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))
returns [BSplineCurve](#BSplineCurve)

Returns a new [`BSplineCurve`](#BSplineCurve), adding knot padding and
control point overlap as necessary.

EXAMPLE

> opCreateBSplineCurve(context, id + "bSplineCurve1", {
>                 "bSplineCurve" : bSplineCurve({
>                             "degree" : 2,
>                             "isPeriodic" : false,
>                             "controlPoints" : [
>                                     vector(0, 0, 0) * inch,
>                                     vector(1, 0, 0) * inch,
>                                     vector(1, 1, 0) * inch,
>                                     vector(0, 1, 0) * inch],
>                             "knots" : knotArray([0, .2, 1]) // Will be padded to [0, 0, 0, .2, 1, 1, 1]
>                         })
>             });
>
> Creates a spline starting at the origin, moving first quickly along
> the x-axis (toward the second point), and finishing on the y-axis.

EXAMPLE

> opCreateBSplineCurve(context, id + "bSplineCurve1", {
>                 "bSplineCurve" : bSplineCurve({
>                             "degree" : 3,
>                             "isPeriodic" : true,
>                             "controlPoints" : [
>                                     vector(0, 0, 0) * inch,
>                                     vector(1, 0, 0) * inch,
>                                     vector(1, 1, 0) * inch,
>                                     vector(0, 1, 0) * inch], // Will be overlapped by repeating the first 3 points
>                             "knots" : knotArray([0, .25, .5, .75, 1]) // Same as default when no knots provided. Will be padded to [-.75, -.5, -.25, 0, .25, .5, .75, 1, 1.25, 1.5, 1.75]
>                         })
>             });
>
> Creates a closed, curvature-continuous, symmetric curve between the
> four given points.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `degree`          | [`number`](/FsDoc/v   | The degree of the     |
|                       | ariables.html#number) | spline.               |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `isPeriodic`      | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | Whether the spline is |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) | periodic.             |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `controlPoints`   | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | An array of control   |
|                       | variables.html#array) | points. See           |
|                       |                       | [`BSplineC            |
|                       |                       | urve`](#BSplineCurve) |
|                       |                       | for specific detail.  |
|                       |                       | For periodic splines, |
|                       |                       | you may provide the   |
|                       |                       | necessary overlap, or |
|                       |                       | provide control       |
|                       |                       | points without any    |
|                       |                       | overlap. If no        |
|                       |                       | overlap is provided,  |
|                       |                       | `degree` overlapping  |
|                       |                       | control points        |
|                       |                       | (corresponding to the |
|                       |                       | first `degree`        |
|                       |                       | control points) will  |
|                       |                       | be added to the end   |
|                       |                       | of the control points |
|                       |                       | list (unless you      |
|                       |                       | provide a set of      |
|                       |                       | knots that show no    |
|                       |                       | overlap is            |
|                       |                       | necessary).           |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `[vector(-1, 1, 0)  |
|                       |                       |  * inch, vector(1, 1, |
|                       |                       |  0) * inch, vector(1, |
|                       |                       |  -1, 0) * inch, vecto |
|                       |                       | r(-1, -1, 0) * inch]` |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `weights`         | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | *Optional*            |
|                       | variables.html#array) |                       |
|                       |                       | An array of weights.  |
|                       |                       | See                   |
|                       |                       | [`BSplineC            |
|                       |                       | urve`](#BSplineCurve) |
|                       |                       | for specific detail.  |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `knots`           | [`Kn                  | *Optional*            |
|                       | otArray`](#KnotArray) |                       |
|                       |                       | An array of knots.    |
|                       |                       | See                   |
|                       |                       | [`BSplineC            |
|                       |                       | urve`](#BSplineCurve) |
|                       |                       | for specific detail.  |
|                       |                       | If knots are not      |
|                       |                       | provided a uniform    |
|                       |                       | parameterization wil  |
|                       |                       | be created such that  |
|                       |                       | the curve exists on   |
|                       |                       | the parameter range   |
|                       |                       | `[0, 1]`. For         |
|                       |                       | non-periodic curves   |
|                       |                       | with `n` control      |
|                       |                       | points, you may       |
|                       |                       | provide the full set  |
|                       |                       | of `n + degree + 1`   |
|                       |                       | knots, or you may     |
|                       |                       | provide               |
|                       |                       | `n - degree + 1`      |
|                       |                       | knots, and            |
|                       |                       | multiplicity will by  |
|                       |                       | padded onto the ends  |
|                       |                       | (which has the effect |
|                       |                       | of clamping the       |
|                       |                       | spline to its two     |
|                       |                       | endpoints). For       |
|                       |                       | periodic curves with  |
|                       |                       | `n` unique control    |
|                       |                       | points (and           |
|                       |                       | optionally an         |
|                       |                       | additional `degree`   |
|                       |                       | overlapping control   |
|                       |                       | points), you may      |
|                       |                       | provide the full set  |
|                       |                       | of                    |
|                       |                       | `n + 2 * degree + 1`  |
|                       |                       | knots, or you may     |
|                       |                       | provide `n + 1`       |
|                       |                       | knots, and the        |
|                       |                       | periodic knots will   |
|                       |                       | be padded onto the    |
|                       |                       | ends.                 |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[bSplineCurve](#bSplineCurve-number-boolean-array-KnotArray)
(degree is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
isPeriodic is [boolean](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean),
controlPoints is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array),
knots is [KnotArray](#KnotArray)) returns [BSplineCurve](#BSplineCurve)

**Deprecated:** *Use [`bSplineCurve(map)`](#bSplineCurve-map)*

[bSplineCurve](#bSplineCurve-number-boolean-array-array-KnotArray)
(degree is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
isPeriodic is [boolean](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean),
controlPoints is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array),
weights is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array),
knots is [KnotArray](#KnotArray)) returns [BSplineCurve](#BSplineCurve)

**Deprecated:** *Use [`bSplineCurve(map)`](#bSplineCurve-map)*

## [math](#module-math.fs) {#module-math.fs .file-header}

A module containing many elementary math functions.

Some math functions (such as [`sin`](#sin-ValueWithUnits) and
[`cos`](#cos-ValueWithUnits)) accept a
[`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits), rather than a
[number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number), and are defined in the
[units](/FsDoc/library.html#module-units.fs) module. There is no `pow`
function: exponentiation is done using the `^` operator.

When writing a FeatureScript module which uses only basic math
functionality, importing `mathUtils` (which imports this module along
with `matrix`, `transform`, and `vector`) is recommended.

[PI](#PI) const

The mathematical constant pi, to floating-point precision.

EXAMPLE

> `myAngle = (PI / 4) * radian`

In most cases, conversions using `PI` can be avoided if using
`ValueWithUnits` appropriately. Thus, you should never find yourself
writing a statement like `sin(myAngle * PI / 180)`, since `myAngle`
should already have correct units attached.

[tolerantEquals](#tolerantEquals-number-number)
(value1 is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
value2 is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)) predicate

Returns `true` if numbers are equal up to a computational tolerance. The
tolerance used is a specific number defined by `TOLERANCE.computational`
(set to `1e-13`) that is meant to adequately handle tolerances
introduced by numerical operations. However, this tolerance may be too
restrictive or generous for different situations, in which case
[`tolerantEquals(number, number, number)`](#tolerantEquals-number-number-number)
should be used with the appropriate tolerance specified.

EXAMPLE

> `tolerantEquals(1, 1)` returns `true`

EXAMPLE

> `tolerantEquals(1, 1 + 1e-14)` returns `true`

EXAMPLE

> `tolerantEquals(1, 1 / 900 * 9e100 / 1e98)` returns `true`

EXAMPLE

> `tolerantEquals(1, 1 / 9 / 9 / 9 / 9 * 9 * 9 * 9 * 9)` returns `true`

See also

[`tolerantEquals(number, number, number)`](#tolerantEquals-number-number-number)

[tolerantEquals](#tolerantEquals-number-number-number)
(value1 is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
value2 is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
tolerance is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)) predicate

Returns `true` if numbers are equal up to a specified tolerance.

EXAMPLE

> `tolerantEquals(1, 1, 0)` returns `true`

EXAMPLE

> `tolerantEquals(1, 1.01, 0.03)` returns `true`

EXAMPLE

> `tolerantEquals(1, 0.99, 0.03)` returns `true`

EXAMPLE

> `tolerantEquals(1, 1.01, 0.01)` returns `true`

EXAMPLE

> `tolerantEquals(1, 1.03, 0.01)` returns `false`

See also

[`tolerantEquals(number, number)`](#tolerantEquals-number-number)

[abs](#abs-) (value)

Absolute value.

EXAMPLE

> `abs(-1)` returns `1`

EXAMPLE

> `abs(-1.5 * inch)` equals `1.5 * inch`

[sqrt](#sqrt-number) (value is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))

Square root of a `number`.

EXAMPLE

> `sqrt(4)` returns `2`

EXAMPLE

> `sqrt(-4)` throws an error

[log](#log-number) (value is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))

Natural logarithm of a `number`.

EXAMPLE

> `log(exp(3))` returns `3`

EXAMPLE

> `log(0)` returns `-inf`

EXAMPLE

> `log(-1)` throws an error

[log10](#log10-number) (value is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))

Base 10 logarithm of a `number`.

EXAMPLE

> `log10(1000)` returns `3`

EXAMPLE

> `log10(0)` returns `-inf`

EXAMPLE

> `log10(-1)` throws an error

[sinh](#sinh-number) (value is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))

Hyperbolic sine.

[cosh](#cosh-number) (value is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))

Hyperbolic cosine.

[tanh](#tanh-number) (value is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))

Hyperbolic tangent.

[asinh](#asinh-number) (value is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))

Inverse hyperbolic sine.

[acosh](#acosh-number) (value is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))

Inverse hyperbolic cosine.

[atanh](#atanh-number) (value is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))

Inverse hyperbolic tangent.

[exp](#exp-number) (value is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))

`e` to the power of `value`.

EXAMPLE

> `exp(1)` returns `2.71828...`

EXAMPLE

> `exp(log(3))` returns `3`

[exp2](#exp2-number) (value is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))

`2` to the power of `value`.

EXAMPLE

> `exp2(10)` returns `1024`

[hypot](#hypot-number-number)
(a is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
b is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))

Hypotenuse function, as `sqrt(a^2 + b^2)`, but without any surprising
results due to finite numeric precision.

EXAMPLE

> `hypot(3, 4)` returns `5`

[floor](#floor-number) (value is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))

Round a `number` down to the nearest integer.

For values with units, first divide by a value with the same units.

EXAMPLE

> `floor(1.9)` returns `1`

EXAMPLE

> `floor(2.0)` returns `2`

EXAMPLE

> `floor(-3.3)` returns `-4`

EXAMPLE

> `var numberOfBricks is number = floor(wallLength / brickLength);`

[ceil](#ceil-number) (value is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))

Round a `number` up to the nearest integer.

For values with units, first divide by a value with the same units.

EXAMPLE

> `ceil(1.1)` returns `2`

EXAMPLE

> `ceil(1.0)` returns `1`

EXAMPLE

> `ceil(-3.3)` returns `-3`

EXAMPLE

> `var numberOfBricks is number = ceil(wallLength / brickLength)`

[round](#round-number) (value is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))

Round a `number` to the nearest integer.

EXAMPLE

> `round(1.4)` returns `1`

EXAMPLE

> `round(1.5)` returns `2`

EXAMPLE

> `round(-1.5)` returns `-1`

[roundToPrecision](#roundToPrecision-number-number)
(value is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
precision is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))

Round a `number` to a given number of decimal places.

EXAMPLE

> `roundToPrecision(0.12345, 3)` returns `0.123`

EXAMPLE

> `roundToPrecision(9.9995, 3)` returns `10`

EXAMPLE

> `roundToPrecision(123.45, -1)` returns `120`

For positive values of precision, this method is more accurate than
[round(value, multiple)](#round--). For instance,
`print(roundToPrecision(0.45682, 4))` prints `0.4568`, but
`round(0.45682, 0.0001)` prints `0.45680000000000004`. This is because
the floating point representation of `0.0001` is slightly imprecise, and
that imprecision is compounded inside the call to `round`. The floating
point value of `4`, on the other hand, is precise, so the result of
`roundToPrecision` will be the closest possible floating-point
representation of `0.4568`. Thus, `print` and other functions using
string conversion (`~`) will not print extraneous digits.

[min](#min--) (value1, value2)

Return the lesser of two values, which must be comparable with `<`.

EXAMPLE

> `min(0, 1)` returns `0`

EXAMPLE

> `min(1 * meter, 1 * inch)` equals `1 * inch`

[max](#max--) (value1, value2)

Return the greater of two values, which must be comparable with `<`.

EXAMPLE

> `max(0, 1)` returns `1`

EXAMPLE

> `max(1 * meter, 1 * inch)` equals `1 * meter`

[min](#min-array) (arr is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array))

Return the least of an array of values, as determined by operator `<`,
or undefined if the array is empty.

EXAMPLE

> `min([1, 2, 3])` returns `1`

EXAMPLE

> `min([1 * inch, 2 * inch, 3 * inch])` equals `1 * inch`

[max](#max-array) (arr is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array))

Return the greatest of an array of values, as determined by operator
`<`, or undefined if the array is empty.

EXAMPLE

> `max([1, 2, 3])` returns `3`

EXAMPLE

> `max([1 * inch, 2 * inch, 3 * inch])` equals `3 * inch`

[argMin](#argMin-array) (arr is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array))

Return the index of the smallest element of an array, as determined by
operator `<`, or undefined if the array is empty.

EXAMPLE

> `argMin([1 * inch, 2 * inch, 3 * inch])` returns `0`

[argMax](#argMax-array) (arr is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array))

Return the index of the largest element of an array, as determined by
the `>` operator, or undefined if the array is empty.

EXAMPLE

> `argMax([1 * inch, 2 * inch, 3 * inch])` returns `2`

[range](#range-number-number)
(from is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
to is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))

Return an array of numbers in a range. Only integers are allowed.

EXAMPLE

> `range(0, 3)` returns `[0, 1, 2, 3]`

[range](#range---) (from, to, count)

Return an array of numbers, (of type `number` or `ValueWithUnits`), in a
range. Note: before FeatureScript 372 this function received as input
the step size instead of the number of steps

EXAMPLE

> `range(0, 10, 6)` returns `[0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10]`

EXAMPLE

> `range(0, 4.5, 4)` returns `[0, 1.5, 3, 4.5]`

EXAMPLE

> `range(1 * inch, 1.4 * inch, 3)` returns
> `[1 * inch, 1.2 * inch, 1.4 * inch]`

[clamp](#clamp---) (value, lowerBound, higherBound)

Force a value into a range,

EXAMPLE

> `clamp(-1, 0, 20)` returns `0`,

EXAMPLE

> `clamp(10, 0, 20)` returns `10`

EXAMPLE

> `clamp(30, 0, 20)` returns `20`

EXAMPLE

> `clamp(30 * inch, 0 * inch, 20 * inch)` equals `20 * inch`

[isInteger](#isInteger-) (value) predicate

True if `value` is a finite integer.

Note that all numbers in FeatureScript represented as floating point
numbers, so an expression like `isInteger(hugeInteger + 0.1)` may still
return `true`.

Used in feature preconditions to define an integer input.

[isNonNegativeInteger](#isNonNegativeInteger-) (value) predicate

True if `value` is a finite integer greater than or equal to zero.

[isPositiveInteger](#isPositiveInteger-) (value) predicate

True if `value` is a finite integer greater than zero.

## [mathUtils](#module-mathUtils.fs) {#module-mathUtils.fs .file-header}

This module imports the `math`, `matrix`, `transform`, and `vector`
modules. It is designed to be imported instead of the `geometry` module
in Feature Studios where only math (not higher-level modeling
functionality) is needed.

## [matrix](#module-matrix.fs) {#module-matrix.fs .file-header}

[Matrix](#Matrix) type

A `Matrix` is an array of rows, all the same size, each of which is an
array of numbers

[canBeMatrix](#canBeMatrix-) (val) predicate

Typecheck for [`Matrix`](#Matrix)

[matrixSize](#matrixSize-Matrix) (matrix is [Matrix](#Matrix))
returns [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

Return a 2 element array containing the numbers of rows and columns of a
matrix.

[isSquare](#isSquare-Matrix) (matrix is [Matrix](#Matrix)) predicate

Check whether a matrix is square.

[matrix](#matrix-array) (value is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array))
returns [Matrix](#Matrix)

Cast a two-dimensional array to a matrix.

[identityMatrix](#identityMatrix-number)
(size is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))
returns [Matrix](#Matrix)

Construct an identity matrix of a given dimension.

[zeroMatrix](#zeroMatrix-number-number)
(rows is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
cols is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))
returns [Matrix](#Matrix)

Construct an all-zero matrix of a given dimension.

[diagonalMatrix](#diagonalMatrix-array)
(diagonalValues is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array))
returns [Matrix](#Matrix)

Given an array of `diagonalValues` of size `n`, construct an `n`x`n`
matrix which has those values along its main diagonal (starting in the
top-left), and `0` everywhere else.

[cwiseProduct](#cwiseProduct-Matrix-Matrix) (m1 is [Matrix](#Matrix),
m2 is [Matrix](#Matrix)) returns [Matrix](#Matrix)

Construct a matrix by multiplying corresponding elements of two matrices
(which must be the same size).

[transpose](#transpose-Matrix) (m is [Matrix](#Matrix))
returns [Matrix](#Matrix)

Return the transpose of a matrix.

[inverse](#inverse-Matrix) (m is [Matrix](#Matrix))
returns [Matrix](#Matrix)

Compute the inverse of a matrix. Throws an exception if the matrix is
not square. If the matrix is singular the resulting matrix will contain
infinities.

[squaredNorm](#squaredNorm-Matrix) (m is [Matrix](#Matrix))
returns [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

Return the sum of the squares of matrix elements.

[norm](#norm-Matrix) (m is [Matrix](#Matrix))
returns [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

Return the square root of the sum of the squares of matrix elements.

[svd](#svd-Matrix) (m is [Matrix](#Matrix))
returns [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

Compute the singular value decomposition of a matrix, i.e. `s`, `u`, and
`v`, where `m == u * s * transpose(v)` and s is a diagonal matrix of
singular values.

  Parameter   Type                  Additional Info
  ----------- --------------------- -------------------
  `m`         [`Matrix`](#Matrix)   an n-by-p matrix.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Return                | Type                  | Description           |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
|                       | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `u`               | [`Matrix`](#Matrix)   | An n-by-n unitary     |
|                       |                       | matrix                |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `s`               | [`Matrix`](#Matrix)   | An n-by-p diagonal    |
|                       |                       | matrix                |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `v`               | [`Matrix`](#Matrix)   | A p-by-p unitary      |
|                       |                       | matrix                |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[determinant](#determinant-Matrix) (m is [Matrix](#Matrix))
returns [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

Return the determinant of the matrix.

[toString](#toString-Matrix) (value is [Matrix](#Matrix))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

points array and a 1d weights array.

[combinePointsAndWeights](#combinePointsAndWeights-array-array)
(points is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array),
weights is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array))

weighted points array

## [persistentCoordSystem](#module-persistentCoordSystem.fs) {#module-persistentCoordSystem.fs .file-header}

[PersistentCoordSystem](#PersistentCoordSystem) type

A coordinate system that can persist as part of an attribute associated
with an entity. This coordinate system will be transformed along with
its parent entity as that entity undergoes transformations.

As with other attributes, the coordinate system will be propagated to
copied entities, such as instances in a pattern. These copied persistent
coordinate systems will take on the transforms of their new parents.

When [`getAttribute`](#getAttribute-Context-map) is used to retrieve a
previously-set persistent coordinate system, the value of coordSystem
will be in its transformed state for the current point in the feature
execution. If a transform is applied such that the coordinate system is
know longer right-handed, then the coordSystem value will be undefined.
For instance, this would happen in the case of a mirrored coordinate
system.

Value

Type

Description

`PersistentCoordSystem`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `coordSystem`

[`CoordSystem`](#CoordSystem)

The coordinate system to persist

-   `coordSystemId`

[`string`](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

An id to associate with the coordinate system. This id must be unique
within the context of the parent entity.

-   `forceRightHanded`

[`boolean`](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)

When true, ensures the coordinate system remains right-handed when
mirrored by inverting the Y-axis. Otherwise, mirroring the persistent
coordinate system destroys it.

[persistentCoordSystem](#persistentCoordSystem-CoordSystem-string-boolean)
(coordSystem is [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem),
coordSystemId is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
forceRightHanded is [boolean](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean))
returns [PersistentCoordSystem](#PersistentCoordSystem)

Creates a persistent coordinate system.

See also

[`PersistentCoordSystem`](#PersistentCoordSystem)

  Parameter            Type                                         Additional Info
  -------------------- -------------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `coordSystem`        [`CoordSystem`](#CoordSystem)                The coordinate system
  `coordSystemId`      [`string`](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)     An id with which to associate the coordinate system. This id must be unique within the context of the parent entity that an becomes associated with this persistent coordinate system through [`setAttribute`](#setAttribute-Context-map).
  `forceRightHanded`   [`boolean`](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)   When true, ensures the coordinate system remains right-handed when mirrored by inverting the Y-axis.

## [splineUtils](#module-splineUtils.fs) {#module-splineUtils.fs .file-header}

[ApproximationTarget](#ApproximationTarget) type

A set of points and optionally derivatives to approximate by a spline.

See also

[`approximateSpline(Context, map)`](#approximateSpline-Context-map)

Value

Type

Description

`ApproximationTarget`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `positions`

[`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

An ordered array of points for the spline to pass closely to.

-   `startDerivative`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

*Required if `start2ndDerivative` is provided*

The desired start derivative of the spline.

-   `start2ndDerivative`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

*Optional*

The desired start second derivative of the spline.

-   `endDerivative`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

*Required if `end2ndDerivative` is provided*

The desired end derivative of the spline.

-   `end2ndDerivative`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

*Optional*

The desired end second derivative of the spline.

[canBeApproximationTarget](#canBeApproximationTarget-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for ApproximationTarget

[approximationTarget](#approximationTarget-map)
(value is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))
returns [ApproximationTarget](#ApproximationTarget)

Construct an [`ApproximationTarget`](#ApproximationTarget)

[approximateSpline](#approximateSpline-Context-map)
(context is [Context](#Context),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))
returns [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

Compute a family of splines that approximates a family of
[`ApproximationTarget`](#ApproximationTarget)s to within a given
tolerance. The resulting splines are consistently parameterized, so
that, for example, lofting between them will match corresponding target
positions. Note: If `parameters` are not specified, the magnitude of
start and end derivatives in targets is ignored as well as the second
component parallel with the first derivative.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `degree`          | [`number`](/FsDoc/v   | The desired degree of |
|                       | ariables.html#number) | the curve. The output |
|                       |                       | may have a different  |
|                       |                       | degree, if for        |
|                       |                       | example there aren\'t |
|                       |                       | enough points.        |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `tolerance`       | [`ValueWithUni        | How far the output is |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | allowed to deviate    |
|                       |                       | from the input. Must  |
|                       |                       | be at least 1e-8      |
|                       |                       | meters.               |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `isPeriodic`      | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | Whether the output    |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) | spline is periodic.   |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `targets`         | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | An array of           |
|                       | variables.html#array) | [`App                 |
|                       |                       | roximationTarget`](#A |
|                       |                       | pproximationTarget)s. |
|                       |                       | All targets must have |
|                       |                       | the same number of    |
|                       |                       | positions and specify |
|                       |                       | corresponding         |
|                       |                       | derivative            |
|                       |                       | information.          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `parameters`      | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | *Optional*            |
|                       | variables.html#array) |                       |
|                       |                       | An array of numbers   |
|                       |                       | representing the      |
|                       |                       | parameters            |
|                       |                       | corresponding to the  |
|                       |                       | target points. Must   |
|                       |                       | be strictly           |
|                       |                       | increasing. If        |
|                       |                       | specified, the output |
|                       |                       | spline at those       |
|                       |                       | parameters will match |
|                       |                       | the target points. If |
|                       |                       | specified,            |
|                       |                       | derivatives in        |
|                       |                       | approximation targets |
|                       |                       | will not be rescaled. |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`number`](/FsDoc/v   | *Optional*            |
|    `maxControlPoints` | ariables.html#number) |                       |
|                       |                       | The maximum number of |
|                       |                       | control points that   |
|                       |                       | will be returned by   |
|                       |                       | this function\'s      |
|                       |                       | output. Tolerance     |
|                       |                       | will not be satisfied |
|                       |                       | if this limit is      |
|                       |                       | reached. Default is   |
|                       |                       | 10000.                |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | *Optional*            |
|  `interpolateIndices` | variables.html#array) |                       |
|                       |                       | An array of indices   |
|                       |                       | into target positions |
|                       |                       | that specifies which  |
|                       |                       | ones are to be        |
|                       |                       | interpolated exactly. |
|                       |                       | This is currently     |
|                       |                       | supported only for    |
|                       |                       | non-periodic splines. |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `suppres          | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
| sInterpolationNotice` | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | Don\'t report an info |
|                       |                       | if the result is      |
|                       |                       | fully interpolated.   |
|                       |                       | Default is false.     |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

  Return type                              Description
  ---------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------
  [`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)   An array of [`BSplineCurve`](#BSplineCurve)s, one for each target.

[evaluateSpline](#evaluateSpline-map)
(definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))
returns [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

Evaluate a 3D spline at several parameters, possibly with derivatives.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `spline`          | [`BSplineC            | The 3D spline to      |
|                       | urve`](#BSplineCurve) | evaluate.             |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `parameters`      | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | An array of numbers   |
|                       | variables.html#array) | in the range of the   |
|                       |                       | spline\'s knot        |
|                       |                       | vector.               |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `nDerivatives`    | [`number`](/FsDoc/v   | *Optional*            |
|                       | ariables.html#number) |                       |
|                       |                       | The number of         |
|                       |                       | derivatives to        |
|                       |                       | compute, in addition  |
|                       |                       | to the positions.     |
|                       |                       | Default is 0.         |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

  Return type                              Description
  ---------------------------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  [`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)   An array of arrays of points. If `result` is returned, `result[i][j]` is the `i`th derivative at `j`th parameter.

[elevateBezierDegree](#elevateBezierDegree-array-number)
(pointsIn is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array),
newDegree is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))
returns [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

Elevate the degree of a bezier curve defined by an array of control
points

  Parameter     Type                                       Additional Info
  ------------- ------------------------------------------ ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `pointsIn`    [`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)     The control points of the curve to be elevated. Must be non-empty.
  `newDegree`   [`number`](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)   The desired degree. If it is less than the number of control points, the control points will be returned unchanged.

  Return type                              Description
  ---------------------------------------- -------------------------------------------------
  [`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)   The control points of the degree-elevated curve

## [surfaceGeometry](#module-surfaceGeometry.fs) {#module-surfaceGeometry.fs .file-header}

This module contains methods for creating and working with primitive
surfaces: planes, cylinders, cones, spheres, and tori.

[XY_PLANE](#XY_PLANE) const

The world XY plane, equivalent to
`plane(vector(0, 0, 0) * meter, vector(0, 0, 1), vector(1, 0, 0))`

[YZ_PLANE](#YZ_PLANE) const

The world YZ plane, equivalent to
`plane(vector(0, 0, 0) * meter, vector(1, 0, 0), vector(0, 1, 0))`

[XZ_PLANE](#XZ_PLANE) const

The world XZ plane, equivalent to
`plane(vector(0, 0, 0) * meter, vector(0, 1, 0), vector(0, 0, 1))`

[Plane](#Plane) type

A `Plane` is a data type representing an origin, a normal vector, and an
X direction, perpendicular to the normal direction.

Value

Type

Description

`Plane`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `origin`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

A 3D point, in world space.

-   `normal`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

A 3D unit vector in world space.

-   `x`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

A 3D unit vector in world space. Must be perpendicular to `normal`.

[canBePlane](#canBePlane-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for [`Plane`](#Plane)

[plane](#plane-CoordSystem) (cSys is [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem))
returns [Plane](#Plane)

Create a [`Plane`](#Plane) on the XY plane of a specified coordinate
system.

[plane](#plane-Vector-Vector-Vector) (origin is [Vector](#Vector),
normal is [Vector](#Vector), x is [Vector](#Vector))
returns [Plane](#Plane)

Create a [`Plane`](#Plane) which fully specifies its orientation.

  Parameter   Type                  Additional Info
  ----------- --------------------- -----------------------------------------------------
  `origin`    [`Vector`](#Vector)   A 3D point in world space.
  `normal`    [`Vector`](#Vector)   A 3D vector in world space. Need not be normalized.
  `x`         [`Vector`](#Vector)   A 3D vector in world space. Need not be normalized.

[plane](#plane-Vector-Vector) (origin is [Vector](#Vector),
normal is [Vector](#Vector)) returns [Plane](#Plane)

Create a [`Plane`](#Plane) from a point and a normal.

The x-axis of this [`Plane`](#Plane)\'s coordinate system will be an
arbitrary vector perpendicular to the `normal`.

  Parameter   Type                  Additional Info
  ----------- --------------------- -----------------------------------------------------
  `origin`    [`Vector`](#Vector)   A 3D point in world space.
  `normal`    [`Vector`](#Vector)   A 3D vector in world space. Need not be normalized.

[alignCanonically](#alignCanonically-Context-Plane)
(context is [Context](#Context), plane is [Plane](#Plane))
returns [Plane](#Plane)

Returns the plane that would represent the coordinate system of a face
coplanar with the input plane. Used in plane transformation for
computing sketch patterns.

[yAxis](#yAxis-Plane) (plane is [Plane](#Plane))
returns [Vector](#Vector)

Returns the y-axis of the specified plane as a 3D Vector in world space.

[tolerantEquals](#tolerantEquals-Plane-Plane)
(plane1 is [Plane](#Plane), plane2 is [Plane](#Plane)) predicate

Check that two [`Plane`](#Plane)s are the same up to tolerance,
including checking that they have the same the origin and local
coordinate system.

To check if two [`Plane`](#Plane)s are equivalent (rather than equal),
use [`coplanarPlanes`](#coplanarPlanes-Plane-Plane).

[coplanarPlanes](#coplanarPlanes-Plane-Plane)
(plane1 is [Plane](#Plane), plane2 is [Plane](#Plane))
returns [boolean](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)

Returns `true` if the two planes are coplanar.

[planeToCSys](#planeToCSys-Plane) (plane is [Plane](#Plane))
returns [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem)

Create a coordinate system whose XY-plane is a specified plane, with its
origin at the plane\'s origin.

[coordSystem](#coordSystem-Plane) (plane is [Plane](#Plane))
returns [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem)

Create a coordinate system whose XY-plane is a specified plane, with its
origin at the plane\'s origin.

Alias for `planeToCSys`.

[toString](#toString-Plane) (value is [Plane](#Plane))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

[project](#project-Plane-Vector) (plane is [Plane](#Plane),
point is [Vector](#Vector)) returns [Vector](#Vector)

Projects a 3D `point` onto a [`Plane`](#Plane), returning a 3D point on
the plane.

[project](#project-Plane-Line) (plane is [Plane](#Plane),
line is [Line](#Line)) returns [Line](#Line)

Projects a [`Line`](#Line) onto a [`Plane`](#Plane), returning a
[`Line`](#Line) whose origin is on the [`Plane`](#Plane) and whose
direction is a normalized [`Vector`](#Vector) on the [`Plane`](#Plane)

Throws an error if the [`Line`](#Line) is in the same direction as the
normal of the [`Plane`](#Plane)

[planeToWorld](#planeToWorld-Plane-Vector) (plane is [Plane](#Plane),
planePoint is [Vector](#Vector)) returns [Vector](#Vector)

Transforms a 2D `point` in a [`Plane`](#Plane)\'s coordinates to its
corresponding 3D point in world coordinates.

[planeToWorld3D](#planeToWorld3D-Plane) (plane is [Plane](#Plane))
returns [Transform](#Transform)

Returns a [`Transform`](#Transform) which takes 3D points measured with
respect to a [`Plane`](#Plane) (such that points which lie on the plane
will have a z-coordinate of approximately `0`) and transforms them into
world coordinates.

[worldToPlane3D](#worldToPlane3D-Plane-Vector)
(plane is [Plane](#Plane), worldPoint is [Vector](#Vector))
returns [Vector](#Vector)

Transforms a 3D `point` in world coordinates into a 3D point measured in
a [`Plane`](#Plane)\'s coordinates. If the `point` lies on the
[`Plane`](#Plane), the result will have a z-coordinate of approximately
`0`.

[worldToPlane](#worldToPlane-Plane-Vector) (plane is [Plane](#Plane),
worldPoint is [Vector](#Vector)) returns [Vector](#Vector)

Transforms a 3D `worldPoint` in world coordinates into a 2D point
measured in a [`Plane`](#Plane)\'s (x,y) coordinates.

This is modified as of FeatureScript version 363.0. Older versions of
FeatureScript use `worldToPlane` to return 3D vectors composed of the
plane coordinate system baseis. This functionality is still available in
the `worldToPlane` function above.

[worldToPlane3D](#worldToPlane3D-Plane) (plane is [Plane](#Plane))
returns [Transform](#Transform)

Returns a [`Transform`](#Transform) which takes 3D points measured in
world coordinates and transforms them into 3D points measured in plane
coordinates (such that points which lie on the plane will have a
z-coordinate of approximately `0`).

[transform](#transform-Plane-Plane) (from is [Plane](#Plane),
to is [Plane](#Plane)) returns [Transform](#Transform)

Returns a [`Transform`](#Transform) which maps the plane `from` to the
plane `to`.

[mirrorAcross](#mirrorAcross-Plane) (plane is [Plane](#Plane))
returns [Transform](#Transform)

Returns a [`Transform`](#Transform) which takes points on one side of a
plane and transforms them to the other side. The resulting transform is
non-rigid, and using this transform in an
[`opTransform`](#opTransform-Context-Id-map) or similar operations will
invert the transformed bodies.

[intersection](#intersection-Plane-Plane) (plane1 is [Plane](#Plane),
plane2 is [Plane](#Plane))

Returns a [`Line`](#Line) at the intersection between the two
[`Plane`](#Plane)s. If the planes are parallel or coincident, returns
`undefined`.

[LinePlaneIntersection](#LinePlaneIntersection) type

Represents an intersection between a line and a plane. Depending on the
line and plane, this intersection may be a point, a line, or nothing.

Value

Type

Description

`LinePlaneIntersection`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `dim`

[`number`](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

Integer representing the dimension of the intersection.

EXAMPLE

> `0` indicates that `intersection` is a 3D length [`Vector`](#Vector).

EXAMPLE

> `1` indicates that `intersection` is a [`Line`](#Line).

EXAMPLE

> `-1` indicates that the intersection does not exist (i.e. the line and
> the plane are parallel).

-   `intersection`

`undefined` or [`Vector`](#Vector) or [`Line`](#Line) (depending on
`dim`) that represents the intersection.

[canBeLinePlaneIntersection](#canBeLinePlaneIntersection-) (value)
predicate

Typecheck for [`LinePlaneIntersection`](#LinePlaneIntersection)

[intersection](#intersection-Plane-Line) (plane is [Plane](#Plane),
line is [Line](#Line))
returns [LinePlaneIntersection](#LinePlaneIntersection)

Returns a [`LinePlaneIntersection`](#LinePlaneIntersection) representing
the intersection between `line` and `plane`.

[isPointOnPlane](#isPointOnPlane-Vector-Plane)
(point is [Vector](#Vector), plane is [Plane](#Plane))
returns [boolean](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)

Returns true if the point lies on the plane.

[flip](#flip-Plane) (plane is [Plane](#Plane)) returns [Plane](#Plane)

Returns a [`Plane`](#Plane) with the reversed normal vector.

[Cone](#Cone) type

Type representing a cone which extends infinitely down the positive
z-axis of its `coordSystem`.

Value

Type

Description

`Cone`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `coordSystem`

[`CoordSystem`](#CoordSystem)

The position and orientation of the cone.

-   `halfAngle`

[`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits)

The angle from z-axis of `coordSystem` to the surface of the cone.

[canBeCone](#canBeCone-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for [`Cone`](#Cone)

[cone](#cone-CoordSystem-ValueWithUnits)
(cSys is [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem),
halfAngle is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits)) returns [Cone](#Cone)

Constructs a `Cone` from a coordinate system and a half angle.

[tolerantEquals](#tolerantEquals-Cone-Cone) (cone1 is [Cone](#Cone),
cone2 is [Cone](#Cone)) predicate

Check that two `Cone`s are the same up to tolerance, including the local
coordinate system.

[toString](#toString-Cone) (value is [Cone](#Cone))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

[Cylinder](#Cylinder) type

Type representing a Cylinder which extends infinitely along the z-axis
of its `coordSystem`.

Value

Type

Description

`Cylinder`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `coordSystem`

[`CoordSystem`](#CoordSystem)

The position and orientation of the cylinder.

-   `radius`

[`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits)

The cylinder\'s radius.

[cylinder](#cylinder-CoordSystem-ValueWithUnits)
(cSys is [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem),
radius is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits))

Constructs a cylinder from a coordinate system and a radius.

[tolerantEquals](#tolerantEquals-Cylinder-Cylinder)
(cylinder1 is [Cylinder](#Cylinder), cylinder2 is [Cylinder](#Cylinder))
predicate

Check that two `Cylinder`s are the same up to tolerance, including the
local coordinate system.

[toString](#toString-Cylinder) (value is [Cylinder](#Cylinder))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

[Torus](#Torus) type

Type representing a torus, the shape of a circle revolved around a
coplanar axis.

The torus represented is revolved about the z-axis of the `coordSystem`
and centered in its xy-plane.

Value

Type

Description

`Torus`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `coordSystem`

[`CoordSystem`](#CoordSystem)

The position and orientation of the torus.

-   `radius`

[`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits)

The major radius, i.e. the distance from the center of the torus to the
center of the revolved circle.

-   `minorRadius`

[`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits)

The minor radius, i.e. the radius of the revolved circle.

[canBeTorus](#canBeTorus-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for [`Torus`](#Torus)

[torus](#torus-CoordSystem-ValueWithUnits-ValueWithUnits)
(cSys is [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem),
minorRadius is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits),
radius is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits)) returns [Torus](#Torus)

Constructs a torus from a coordinate system, the minor radius, and the
major radius.

[tolerantEquals](#tolerantEquals-Torus-Torus)
(torus1 is [Torus](#Torus), torus2 is [Torus](#Torus)) predicate

Check that two tori are the same up to tolerance, including the local
coordinate system.

[toString](#toString-Torus) (value is [Torus](#Torus))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

[Sphere](#Sphere) type

Type representing a sphere of a given radius centered around a 3D point.

Value

Type

Description

`Sphere`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `coordSystem`

[`CoordSystem`](#CoordSystem)

The position and orientation of the sphere.

-   `radius`

[`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits)

The sphere\'s radius.

[canBeSphere](#canBeSphere-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for [`Sphere`](#Sphere)

[sphere](#sphere-CoordSystem-ValueWithUnits)
(cSys is [CoordSystem](#CoordSystem),
radius is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits)) returns [Sphere](#Sphere)
