[addManipulators](#addManipulators-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
manipulators is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Add a manipulator to this feature, which will be visible and
interactable when a user edits the feature.

`addManipulators` should be called within the feature function, with the
offset on the added manipulator set to match the state of the
definition.

  Parameter        Type                                 Additional Info
  ---------------- ------------------------------------ -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `manipulators`   [`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)   A `map` whose keys will match the keys of `newManipulators` (passed into the Manipulator Change Function), and whose values are the `Manipulators` to be added.

## [path](#module-path.fs) {#module-path.fs .file-header}

[Path](#Path) type

Represents a series of connected edges which form a continuous path.

Value

Type

Description

`Path`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `edges`

[`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

The edges that form this Path, in the order of the Path.

-   `flipped`

[`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

An array of booleans corresponding to each edge in the path, set to
`true` to traverse the edge backwards.

-   `closed`

[`boolean`](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)

Whether the Path is a closed path.

-   `adjacentFaces`

[`Query`](#Query)

*Optional*

All adjacent faces on one side of the Path.

[canBePath](#canBePath-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for [`Path`](#Path)

[PathDistanceInformation](#PathDistanceInformation) type

Distance information returned by `constructPath` and
`evPathTangentLines` when either function is provided with
`referenceGeometry`

Value

Type

Description

`PathDistanceInformation`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `distance`

[`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits)

The distance between the the start of the [`Path`](#Path) and the center
of the bounding box of `referenceGeometry`, or infinity if
`referenceGeometry` is empty

-   `withinBoundingBox`

[`boolean`](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)

Whether the start of the [`Path`](#Path) is within the bounding box of
`referenceGeometry`, or `false` if `referenceGeometry` is empty

[canBePathDistanceInformation](#canBePathDistanceInformation-) (value)
predicate

Typecheck for [`PathDistanceInformation`](#PathDistanceInformation)

[defaultPathDistanceInformation](#defaultPathDistanceInformation) ()
returns [PathDistanceInformation](#PathDistanceInformation)

Returns a PathDistanceInformation with `distance` set to infinity and
`withinBoundingBox` set to false

[reverse](#reverse-Path) (path is [Path](#Path)) returns [Path](#Path)

Reverse the direction of a [`Path`](#Path)

  Parameter   Type              Additional Info
  ----------- ----------------- ---------------------------------
  `path`      [`Path`](#Path)   the [`Path`](#Path) to reverse.

[constructPath](#constructPath-Context-Query)
(context is [Context](#Context), edgesQuery is [Query](#Query))
returns [Path](#Path)

Construct a [`Path`](#Path) from a [`Query`](#Query) of edges, picking
the starting point of the path based on query evaluation order for
`edgesQuery`

  Parameter      Type                Additional Info
  -------------- ------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `edgesQuery`   [`Query`](#Query)   A [`Query`](#Query) of edges to form into a [`Path`](#Path). The edges are ordered with query evaluation order, so a [`qUnion`](#qUnion-array) should be used to ensure a stable ordering.

[constructPath](#constructPath-Context-Query-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), edgesQuery is [Query](#Query),
options is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))
returns [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

Construct a [`Path`](#Path) from a [`Query`](#Query) of edges,
optionally picking the starting point as the closest viable starting
point to the supplied `referenceGeometry`

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `edgesQuery`          | [`Query`](#Query)     | A [`Query`](#Query)   |
|                       |                       | of edges to form into |
|                       |                       | a [`Path`](#Path).    |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| `options`             | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     |                       | *Optional*            |
|   `referenceGeometry` |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | A geometry            |
|                       |                       | [`Query`](#Query) to  |
|                       |                       | determine the start   |
|                       |                       | of the                |
|                       |                       | [`Path`](#Path). If   |
|                       |                       | unspecified, (or the  |
|                       |                       | query is empty) the   |
|                       |                       | starting point of the |
|                       |                       | path will be based on |
|                       |                       | query evaluation      |
|                       |                       | order for             |
|                       |                       | `edgesQuery`.         |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `tolerance`       | [`ValueWithUni        | *Optional*            |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) |                       |
|                       |                       | Tolerance with length |
|                       |                       | units indicating how  |
|                       |                       | close endpoints need  |
|                       |                       | to be to be           |
|                       |                       | considered part of    |
|                       |                       | the same path.        |
|                       |                       | Default is            |
|                       |                       | `1e-8 * meter`        |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `1e-5 * meter`      |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Return                | Type                  | Description           |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
|                       | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `path`            | [`Path`](#Path)       | The resulting         |
|                       |                       | constructed           |
|                       |                       | [`Path`](#Path)       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `pat              | [`PathDista           | A map containing the  |
| hDistanceInformation` | nceInformation`](#Pat | distance from the     |
|                       | hDistanceInformation) | [`Path`](#Path) start |
|                       |                       | point to the center   |
|                       |                       | of the bounding box   |
|                       |                       | of                    |
|                       |                       | `referenceGeometry`   |
|                       |                       | and whether the       |
|                       |                       | [`Path`](#Path) start |
|                       |                       | point falls inside    |
|                       |                       | that bounding box.    |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[evPathLength](#evPathLength-Context-Path)
(context is [Context](#Context), path is [Path](#Path))
returns [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits)

  Return type                           Description
  ------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------
  [`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits)   The total length of the [`Path`](#Path).

[evPathTangentLines](#evPathTangentLines-Context-Path-array)
(context is [Context](#Context), path is [Path](#Path),
parameters is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array))
returns [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

Return tangent lines to a [`Path`](#Path) using arc length
parameterization.

  Parameter      Type                                     Additional Info
  -------------- ---------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `path`         [`Path`](#Path)                          The [`Path`](#Path) to use.
  `parameters`   [`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)   An array of numbers in the range 0..1 indicating where along the [`Path`](#Path) to evaluate tangents.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Return                | Type                  | Description           |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
|                       | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `tangentLines`    | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | The tangent           |
|                       | variables.html#array) | [`Line`](#Line)s      |
|                       |                       | corresponding to each |
|                       |                       | parameter             |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `edgeIndices`     | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | The indices of the    |
|                       | variables.html#array) | edges in the          |
|                       |                       | [`Path`](#Path)       |
|                       |                       | corresponding to each |
|                       |                       | parameter             |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[evPathTangentLines](#evPathTangentLines-Context-Path-array-)
(context is [Context](#Context), path is [Path](#Path),
parameters is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array), referenceGeometry)
returns [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

Return tangent lines to a [`Path`](#Path) using arc length
parameterization. By default, the 0 parameter of the [`Path`](#Path)
will be the start of the [`Path`](#Path). If evaluating a closed path,
providing reference geometry will alter the 0 parameter to be the
closest point on the [`Path`](#Path) to the reference geometry.
Providing reference geometry for a non-closed [`Path`](#Path) will not
change the parameterization of the [`Path`](#Path)

  Parameter             Type                                     Additional Info
  --------------------- ---------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `path`                [`Path`](#Path)                          The [`Path`](#Path) to use.
  `parameters`          [`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)   An array of numbers in the range 0..1 indicating where along the [`Path`](#Path) to evaluate tangents.
  `referenceGeometry`                                            A geometry [`Query`](#Query) to determine the 0 parameter of the [`Path`](#Path), or `undefined`. If an empty [`Query`](#Query) or `undefined` is specified, the tangents will be evaluated starting at the beginning of the path.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Return                | Type                  | Description           |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
|                       | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `tangentLines`    | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | The tangent           |
|                       | variables.html#array) | [`Line`](#Line)s      |
|                       |                       | corresponding to each |
|                       |                       | parameter             |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `edgeIndices`     | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | The indices of the    |
|                       | variables.html#array) | edges in the          |
|                       |                       | [`Path`](#Path)       |
|                       |                       | corresponding to each |
|                       |                       | parameter             |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `pat              | [`PathDista           | A map containing the  |
| hDistanceInformation` | nceInformation`](#Pat | distance from the     |
|                       | hDistanceInformation) | remapped 0 parameter  |
|                       |                       | to the center of the  |
|                       |                       | bounding box of       |
|                       |                       | `referenceGeometry`   |
|                       |                       | and whether the       |
|                       |                       | remapped 0 parameter  |
|                       |                       | falls inside that     |
|                       |                       | bounding box. Only    |
|                       |                       | valid if the path is  |
|                       |                       | closed.               |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[getPathEndVertices](#getPathEndVertices-Context-Path)
(context is [Context](#Context), path is [Path](#Path))
returns [Query](#Query)

Return query to end vertices of path if open or [`qNothing`](#qNothing)
if closed.

  Parameter   Type              Additional Info
  ----------- ----------------- -----------------------------
  `path`      [`Path`](#Path)   The [`Path`](#Path) to use.

## [patternCommon](#module-patternCommon.fs) {#module-patternCommon.fs .file-header}

[PatternType](#PatternType) enum

The type of pattern.

  Value       Description
  ----------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `PART`      Creates copies of bodies.
  `FEATURE`   Calls a feature function multiple times, first informing the `context` of the transform to be applied.
  `FACE`      Creates copies of faces and attempts to merge them with existing bodies.

[MirrorType](#MirrorType) enum

The type of mirror.

See also

[`PatternType`](#PatternType)

  Value       Description
  ----------- -------------
  `PART`      
  `FEATURE`   
  `FACE`

## [properties](#module-properties.fs) {#module-properties.fs .file-header}

Properties include name, appearance, material, and part number (see
[`PropertyType`](#PropertyType)). They can be set in FeatureScript, but
not read.

[setProperty](#setProperty-Context-map) (context is [Context](#Context),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Sets a property on a set of bodies and/or faces. The allowed properties
are listed in [`PropertyType`](#PropertyType). Only `APPEARANCE` and
`NAME` properties are supported for faces.

Note: Any properties set in this way will be overridden if they are set
directly in the Part Studio (via \"Rename\", \"Set appearance\", or the
properties dialog). In that case the property value provided in
FeatureScript will become shadowed. For example, if a part number is set
in a custom feature based on the configuration, manually editing the
part number from the properties dialog will override the custom
feature\'s part number for all configurations.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `entities`        | [`Query`](#Query)     | The bodies and/or     |
|                       |                       | faces to apply the    |
|                       |                       | property to.          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `propertyType`    | [`Property            | The property to set.  |
|                       | Type`](#PropertyType) |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `Pro                |
|                       |                       | pertyType.APPEARANCE` |
|                       |                       | > to change the part  |
|                       |                       | > appearance.         |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`string`](/FsDoc/v   | *Required if          |
|    `customPropertyId` | ariables.html#string) | `propertyType` is     |
|                       |                       | `CUSTOM`*             |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | The id of the custom  |
|                       |                       | property. The         |
|                       |                       | property id is        |
|                       |                       | available from your   |
|                       |                       | [company\'s custom    |
|                       |                       | properties            |
|                       |                       | page](ht              |
|                       |                       | tps://cad.onshape.com |
|                       |                       | /help/index.htm#cshid |
|                       |                       | =company_properties). |
|                       |                       | Note that this call   |
|                       |                       | performs no checks as |
|                       |                       | to whether the custom |
|                       |                       | property value is     |
|                       |                       | valid, so invalid     |
|                       |                       | property values may   |
|                       |                       | be recorded.          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `value`           |                       | A [`Color`](#Color)   |
|                       |                       | if the `propertyType` |
|                       |                       | is `APPEARANCE`, a    |
|                       |                       | [`                    |
|                       |                       | Material`](#Material) |
|                       |                       | if it is `MATERIAL`,  |
|                       |                       | a boolean if it is    |
|                       |                       | `EXCLUDE_FROM_BOM`, a |
|                       |                       | value with mass units |
|                       |                       | if it is              |
|                       |                       | `MASS_OVERRIDE`, and  |
|                       |                       | a string otherwise.   |
|                       |                       | The value should be a |
|                       |                       | string for a `CUSTOM` |
|                       |                       | property even if the  |
|                       |                       | property is of a      |
|                       |                       | non-string type.      |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `color(1, 0, 0)` to |
|                       |                       | > make the part red.  |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[getProperty](#getProperty-Context-map) (context is [Context](#Context),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

NOTE: This function cannot be called on the current context inside
custom features. It can only be called from [table
functions](/FsDoc/tables.html), [editing
logic](/FsDoc/uispec.html#editing-logic-function), [manipulator change
functions](/FsDoc/uispec.html#manipulator-change-function), and custom
features that refer to a different
[context](/FsDoc/library.html#Context). Getting properties in custom
features within the current context is not possible, since features are
regenerated before any user-set properties are applied.

Returns the value of a property of a single body, which can be either an
Onshape property (allowed properties listed on PropertyType) or a custom
property defined in company settings.

The returned value\'s type will correspond to the property type: A
[`Color`](#Color) if the `propertyType` is `APPEARANCE`, a
[`Material`](#Material) if it is `MATERIAL`, a boolean if it is
`EXCLUDE_FROM_BOM`, and a string otherwise. `CUSTOM` property\'s
returned value will always be a string, even if the custom property is
of a non-string type.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `entity`          | [`Query`](#Query)     | A single body the     |
|                       |                       | property applies to.  |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `propertyType`    | [`Property            | The property to get.  |
|                       | Type`](#PropertyType) |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `PropertyType.NAME` |
|                       |                       | > to get the body\'s  |
|                       |                       | > name                |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`string`](/FsDoc/v   | *Required if          |
|    `customPropertyId` | ariables.html#string) | `propertyType` is     |
|                       |                       | `CUSTOM`*             |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | The id of the custom  |
|                       |                       | property. The         |
|                       |                       | property id is        |
|                       |                       | available from your   |
|                       |                       | [company\'s custom    |
|                       |                       | properties            |
|                       |                       | page](ht              |
|                       |                       | tps://cad.onshape.com |
|                       |                       | /help/index.htm#cshid |
|                       |                       | =company_properties). |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[Color](#Color) type

Represents a color as red, green, blue, and alpha transparency
components, each between 0 and 1 (inclusive).

[canBeColor](#canBeColor-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for [`Color`](#Color)

[color](#color-number-number-number-number)
(red is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
green is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
blue is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
alpha is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)) returns [Color](#Color)

Create a [`Color`](#Color) from RGBA values, each between 0 and 1
(inclusive)

[color](#color-number-number-number)
(red is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
green is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
blue is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)) returns [Color](#Color)

Create an opaque [`Color`](#Color) from RGB values, each between 0 and 1
(inclusive)

[Material](#Material) type

Represents a material.

[canBeMaterial](#canBeMaterial-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for [`Material`](#Material)

[material](#material-string-ValueWithUnits)
(name is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
density is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits))

Constructs a material with a name and a density.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `name`                | [`string`](/FsDoc/v   | The displayed name of |
|                       | ariables.html#string) | the material.         |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| `density`             | [`ValueWithUni        | EXAMPLE               |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) |                       |
|                       |                       | > `19.3 * g           |
|                       |                       | ram / centimeter ^ 3` |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

## [string](#module-string.fs) {#module-string.fs .file-header}

Pretty printing and toString methods

[toString](#toString-) (value)
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

Return a string representation of any value.

Overloads of this method are found for many standard and custom types in
this and other modules. When overloaded, the `toString` method will be
called inside `print` and `println`, allowing users to change how custom
types are printed.

[toString](#toString-string)
(value is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

[toString](#toString-array)
(value is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

[toString](#toString-map) (value is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

[print](#print-) (value)

Print a message to the FeatureScript notices pane.

This has no effect on model state or rendering.

[println](#println-) (value)

Print a message to the FeatureScript notices pane, followed by a
newline.

This has no effect on model state or rendering.

[println](#println) ()

Print a newline to the FeatureScript notices pane.

[splitIntoCharacters](#splitIntoCharacters-string)
(s is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))
returns [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

Split a string into an array of characters, each represented as a
string.

[parseJson](#parseJson-string)
(s is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))

Parse a JSON string into either a map or array. Null values in the JSON
are returned as `undefined`. Throws if the string is not well-formed
JSON.

  Return type   Description
  ------------- ----------------------------------------------------
                A map or an array corresponding to the JSON value.

[REGEX_NUMBER](#REGEX_NUMBER) const

Matches a number in the string, with or without decimals or exponents.

[REGEX_NUMBER_CAPTURE](#REGEX_NUMBER_CAPTURE) const

Matches a number in the string, with or without decimals or exponents
and captures it.

[REGEX_ID_COMPONENT](#REGEX_ID_COMPONENT) const

Matches a single component of an [`Id`](#Id): an optional leading `*`
(unstable marker) followed by one or more characters from the set `a-z`,
`A-Z`, `0-9`, `_`, `.`, `+`, `/`, `-`.

[addCustomNumberMatching](#addCustomNumberMatching-string)
(regExp is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

Extends regular expression syntax by adding \\f to indicate a complete
number

[match](#match-string-string)
(s is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
regExp is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))
returns [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

Test if `s` matches `regExp` in its entirety.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Return                | Type                  | Description           |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
|                       | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `hasMatch`        | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | `true` if `regExp`    |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) | matches `s`           |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `captures`        | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | The first element is  |
|                       | variables.html#array) | always the input      |
|                       |                       | string `s`. The       |
|                       |                       | following elements    |
|                       |                       | are a list of all     |
|                       |                       | captured groups.      |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[replace](#replace-string-string-string)
(s is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
regExp is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
replacement is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

Returns a copy of `s` with every match of `regExp` replaced with the
string `replacement`.

EXAMPLE

> `replace("a~~aa~a", "a.", "X")` returns `X~X~a`

[stringToNumber](#stringToNumber-string)
(s is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))
returns [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

Convert a number in string form, into a FS number. Note that this
function will not accept trailing non numeric text, the entire string
must be a single valid number.

EXAMPLE

> `stringToNumber("1")` returns the number `1`

EXAMPLE

> `stringToNumber("1.0")` returns the number `1`

EXAMPLE

> `stringToNumber("1e2")` returns the number `100`

[length](#length-string) (s is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))
returns [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

Return the number of characters in a string.

[substring](#substring-string-number)
(s is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
startIndex is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

Return a substring of a string starting at the specified start index.

EXAMPLE

> `substring("refactoring", 7)` returns `"ring"`

[substring](#substring-string-number-number)
(s is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
startIndex is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number),
endIndex is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

Return a substring of a string starting at the specified start index and
ending at the specified end index.

EXAMPLE

> `substring("refactoring", 2, 6)` returns `"fact"`

[startsWith](#startsWith-string-string)
(s is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
prefix is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))
returns [boolean](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)

Return whether a string starts with the specified prefix.

[endsWith](#endsWith-string-string)
(s is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
suffix is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))
returns [boolean](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)

Return whether a string ends with the specified suffix.

[splitByRegexp](#splitByRegexp-string-string)
(s is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
separatorRegexp is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))
returns [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

Split a string into parts based on a regular expression separator.

EXAMPLE

> `splitByRegexp("this, truly, is a test.", "[,. ]+")` returns
> `[ "this" , "truly" , "is" , "a" , "test" ]`

EXAMPLE

> `splitByRegexp("fooooobazzoo", "oo")` returns `[ "f" , "" , "obazz" ]`

EXAMPLE

> `splitByRegex("foo", "")` returns `[ "" , "" , "" , "" ]`

[indexOf](#indexOf-string-string)
(s is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
substr is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))
returns [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

Return the index of a substring in a string, or -1 if the substring is
not found.

[indexOf](#indexOf-string-string-number)
(s is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
substr is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
startIndex is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))
returns [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

Return the index of a substring in a string starting the search at a
specified start index, or -1 if the substring is not found.

[indexOfRegexp](#indexOfRegexp-string-string)
(s is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
regexp is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))
returns [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

Return the first index of a regular expression match in a string, or -1
if not found.

[indexOfRegexp](#indexOfRegexp-string-string-number)
(s is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
regexp is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
startIndex is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))
returns [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

Return the first index of a regular expression match in a string
starting at the specified start index, or -1 if not found.

[repeatString](#repeatString-string-number)
(s is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
count is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

Return a string made by repeating the first argument a specified number
of times. For example: repeatString(\"foo\", 5) returns
\"foofoofoofoofoo\".

[isUndefinedOrEmptyString](#isUndefinedOrEmptyString-) (val) predicate

Is undefined or empty string.

[join](#join-array-string) (arr is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array),
separator is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

Concatenates all elements in an array into a string, delimited by a
separator string.

[join](#join-array) (arr is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

Concatenates all elements in an array into a string.

## [tabReferences](#module-tabReferences.fs) {#module-tabReferences.fs .file-header}

[PartStudioData](#PartStudioData) type

The value of a Part Studio reference parameter, specifying user-selected
parts or other bodies from another Part Studio. The bodies can be added
to the current context using an
[Instantiator](/FsDoc/library.html#module-instantiator).

Full documentation and examples uses can be found
[here](/FsDoc/imports.html#part-studio).

See also

[`addInstance(Instantiator, PartStudioData, map)`](#addInstance-Instantiator-PartStudioData-map)

Value

Type

Description

`PartStudioData`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `buildFunction`

[`BuildFunction`](#BuildFunction)

A function with one argument (a configuration map) which returns a new
context containing all parts of the referenced Part Studio.

-   `configuration`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

The user-input values for the configuration of the selected Part Studio.
The keys of this map are the configuration inputs\' [FeatureScript
ids](https://forum.onshape.com/discussion/9001/configurations-update-edit-featurescript-ids),
and the map can be passed (either as-is or modified) into the
buildFunction above, or to
[`addInstance`](#addInstance-Instantiator-function-map).

-   `partQuery`

[`Query`](#Query)

A query for the user-selected parts in the other context.

-   `configurationData`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

This maps configuration input FeatureScript ids to maps that have
information about the configuration inputs. Each of these maps has a
`defaultValue` field with the default value of the configuration input.
Enum configuration inputs (configuration lists) also have an `options`
field with the value of the [Enum](/FsDoc/type-tags.html#enumerations).

[ImageData](#ImageData) type

The value of an image reference parameter, which can be placed in a Part
Studio using [`skImage`](#skImage-Sketch-string-map). Outside of use
with skImage, color data for individual pixels is not accessable from
FeatureScript.

Full documentation and example uses can be found
[here](/FsDoc/imports.html#image).

Value

Type

Description

`ImageData`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `imageWidth`

[`number`](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

Width of the image, in pixels

-   `imageHeight`

[`number`](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

Height of the image, in pixels

-   `mediaType`

[`string`](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

MIME type of the uploaded image

[TableData](#TableData) type

The value of a CSV reference parameter, containing the file\'s tabular
data as an array of arrays.

Full documentation and example uses can be found
[here](/FsDoc/imports.html#csv).

Value

Type

Description

`TableData`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `csvData`

[`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

If the CSV contains a single rows, this value is a single array of
strings or numbers. If the value contains multiple rows, this value is
an array of arrays of strings or numbers. Individual cell values can be
accessed by indexing into these arrays
(`var row1column2 = definition.myTable.csvData[0][1]`), or by iterating
through them
(`for (var row in definition.myTable.csvData) { println(row); }`).

[JSONData](#JSONData) type

The value of a JSON reference parameter, containing the file\'s data.

Value

Type

Description

`JSONData`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `jsonData`

A value that represents the top-level entity of the imported JSON file:
this is a map if the JSON entity is an object, an array if the JSON
entity is an array, and likewise for the standard JSON types. Note that
JSON `null` values are imported as `undefined`.

[TextData](#TextData) type

The value of a Text reference parameter, containing the file\'s data.

Value

Type

Description

`TextData`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `textData`

[`string`](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

A value that represents the top-level entity of the imported text file.

[CADImportData](#CADImportData) type

The value of a CAD import reference parameter, which can be used by a
Part Studio import feature. The data is not accessible outside of an
import operation.

Full documentation and example can be found
[here](/FsDoc/imports.html#cad-import).

## [table](#module-table.fs) {#module-table.fs .file-header}

[defineTable](#defineTable-function)
(table is [function](/FsDoc/variables.html#function))
returns [function](/FsDoc/variables.html#function)

This function takes a table generation function and wraps it to define a
table. It is analogous to
[`defineFeature`](#defineFeature-function-map), except for tables. A
typical usage is something like:

    annotation { "Table Type Name" : "MyTable" } // This annotation is required for Onshape to recognize myTable as a table.
    export const myTable = defineTable(function(context is Context, definition is map) returns Table
        precondition
        {
            ... // Specify the parameters that this table takes, if any
        }
        {
            ... // Compute and return the table, using the context and the parameters
        });

For more information on writing tables, see `FeatureScript Tables`
(TODO) in the language guide.

  Parameter   Type                                           Additional Info
  ----------- ---------------------------------------------- -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `table`     [`function`](/FsDoc/variables.html#function)   A function that takes a `context` and a `definition` and returns a [`Table`](#Table) or a [`TableArray`](#TableArray)

[Table](#Table) type

A `Table` represents a read-only table, consisting of rows, columns, and
associated entities. Custom tables return a `Table` or an array of
`Table`s (tagged as a [`TableArray`](#TableArray)) as their output.

The user-visible strings in a table, like column headings and cell
values can be specified as either a string, a number, a
[`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits) or a
[`TemplateString`](#TemplateString). These are referred to as \"table
values\" and checked by the [`isTableValue`](#isTableValue-) predicate.

Value

Type

Description

`Table`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `title`

A table value specifying the title of the table

-   `columnDefinitions`

[`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

An array of [`TableColumnDefinition`](#TableColumnDefinition)s
specifying the columns.

-   `rows`

[`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

An array of [`TableRow`](#TableRow)s specifying the data in the table.

-   `entities`

[`Query`](#Query)

*Optional*

An optional [`Query`](#Query) specifying the entities associated with
the entire table.

[canBeTable](#canBeTable-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for [`Table`](#Table)

[table](#table--array-array) (title,
columnDefinitions is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array),
rows is [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)) returns [Table](#Table)

Constructs a [`Table`](#Table) given a title, column definitions, and
rows

[TableColumnDefinition](#TableColumnDefinition) type

A `TableColumnDefinition` represents a column in a [`Table`](#Table).

Value

Type

Description

`TableColumnDefinition`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `id`

[`string`](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

The internal id of the column. Referenced in [`TableRow`](#TableRow)s to
specify cell values.

-   `name`

A table value specifying the column name, which is displayed as the
heading

-   `alignment`

[`TableTextAlignment`](#TableTextAlignment)

*Optional*

How text is aligned in the column. Default is `LEFT`.

-   `entities`

[`Query`](#Query)

*Optional*

An optional [`Query`](#Query) specifying the entities associated with
the column.

[canBeTableColumnDefinition](#canBeTableColumnDefinition-) (value)
predicate

Typecheck for [`TableColumnDefinition`](#TableColumnDefinition)

[tableColumnDefinition](#tableColumnDefinition-string-)
(id is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string), name)
returns [TableColumnDefinition](#TableColumnDefinition)

Constructs a [`TableColumnDefinition`](#TableColumnDefinition) given an
id and a name.

[tableColumnDefinition](#tableColumnDefinition-string--TableTextAlignment)
(id is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string), name,
alignment is [TableTextAlignment](#TableTextAlignment))
returns [TableColumnDefinition](#TableColumnDefinition)

Constructs a [`TableColumnDefinition`](#TableColumnDefinition) given an
id, a name, and a [`TableTextAlignment`](#TableTextAlignment)
controlling how its cell content is aligned.

[tableColumnDefinition](#tableColumnDefinition-string--Query)
(id is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string), name,
entities is [Query](#Query))
returns [TableColumnDefinition](#TableColumnDefinition)

Constructs a [`TableColumnDefinition`](#TableColumnDefinition) given an
id, a name, and entities to cross-highlight when mousing over the
column.

[TableRow](#TableRow) type

A `TableRow` represents a row in a table, including the cells in that
row.

Value

Type

Description

`TableRow`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `columnIdToCell`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

The cell values. Keys are column ids, as specified in the table column
definitions. Values are table values or
[`TableCellError`](#TableCellError)s.

-   `entities`

[`Query`](#Query)

*Optional*

An optional [`Query`](#Query) specifying the entities associated with
the row.

(columnIdToCell is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))
returns [TableRow](#TableRow)

[tableRow](#tableRow-map-Query)
(columnIdToCell is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map),
entities is [Query](#Query)) returns [TableRow](#TableRow)

Constructs a [`TableRow`](#TableRow) given the cell values and entities.

Returns `true` if the input is a table value, that is a string, a

[TableCellWithInfo](#TableCellWithInfo) type

A `TableCellWithInfo` represents a table cell with both a value and an

value and an info message.

[allSolidsAndClosedComposites](#allSolidsAndClosedComposites-Context)
(context is [Context](#Context))
returns [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

Returns an array of maps for all modifiable non-mesh solids and closed
composites. Each map has a key `part`, which maps to a query for the
solid or composite and `bodies`, which maps to either the solid or all
constituent bodies. This is useful for iterating over what a user may
consider to be \"individual parts\" in a context.

EXAMPLE

> for (var partAndBodies in allSolidsAndClosedComposites(context))
>     {
>         var name = getProperty(context, { "entity" : partAndBodies.part, "propertyType" : PropertyType.NAME } );
>         var volume = evVolume(context, { "entities" : partAndBodies.bodies });
>     }

[toString](#toString-Table) (table is [Table](#Table))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

Tries to convert a table to string form. Because the actual table output
depends on user-chosen units and precision, the result of this function
may not match it.

[toString](#toString-TemplateString)
(value is [TemplateString](#TemplateString))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

## [templatestring](#module-templatestring.fs) {#module-templatestring.fs .file-header}

[ValueWithUnitsAndPrecision](#ValueWithUnitsAndPrecision) type

Represents a [`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits) which, when put in a
[`TemplateString`](#TemplateString) will render with a specified
precision override.

[canBeValueWithUnitsAndPrecision](#canBeValueWithUnitsAndPrecision-)
(value) predicate

Typecheck for
[`ValueWithUnitsAndPrecision`](#ValueWithUnitsAndPrecision)

[valueWithUnitsAndPrecision](#valueWithUnitsAndPrecision-ValueWithUnits-number)
(value is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits),
precision is [number](/FsDoc/variables.html#number))
returns [ValueWithUnitsAndPrecision](#ValueWithUnitsAndPrecision)

Constructs a [`ValueWithUnitsAndPrecision`](#ValueWithUnitsAndPrecision)
given the value and precision.

[TemplateString](#TemplateString) type

A `TemplateString` represents a value that will be formatted by template
substitution. It is useful when, for instance, a table cell needs to
display some text in combination with a length formatted in the document
length units.

The `TemplateString` is a map with a string field `template`. Other
fields represent parameters to substitute and may be strings, numbers or
[`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits).

Formatting happens as follows: Text in `template` that does not contain
the number sign `#` is unchanged. `#identifier` (where `identifier` is a
valid FeatureScript identifier) causes a substitution with the result of
looking up `identifier` in the map. `##` is changed to `#`. `# ` (The
number sign followed by a space) is removed, which can be useful for
separating a substitution from text.

EXAMPLE

> `{ 'template' : 'Length = #len', 'len' : foot }` gets formatted as
> `Length = 12 in` if document units are inches.

EXAMPLE

> `{ 'template' : '###var# bar', 'var' : 'foo' }` get formatted as
> `#foobar`.

[canBeTemplateString](#canBeTemplateString-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for [`TemplateString`](#TemplateString).

[templateString](#templateString-map)
(value is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))
returns [TemplateString](#TemplateString)

Constructor for [`TemplateString`](#TemplateString).

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `value`               | [`map`](/FsDo         | A map with a          |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) | \"template\" field    |
|                       |                       | and any number of     |
|                       |                       | other fields, which   |
|                       |                       | may be referenced in  |
|                       |                       | the template string   |
|                       |                       | as e.g. `#myValue`.   |
|                       |                       | Used in FeatureScript |
|                       |                       | tables. See           |
|                       |                       | [`TemplateStri        |
|                       |                       | ng`](#TemplateString) |
|                       |                       | docs for more info.   |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `{ "template        |
|                       |                       | " : "Value of #myValu |
|                       |                       | e", "myValue" : 42 }` |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

## [tolerance](#module-tolerance.fs) {#module-tolerance.fs .file-header}

[defineLengthTolerance](#defineLengthTolerance-map-string-string)
(definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map),
field is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
parentParameterName is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)) predicate

Creates a parameter group containing length tolerance controls for the
specified field in the specified definition.

[defineLengthToleranceExtended](#defineLengthToleranceExtended-map-string-string)
(definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map),
field is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
parentParameterName is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)) predicate

Creates a parameter group containing length fit tolerance controls for
the specified field in the specified definition.

[defineAngleTolerance](#defineAngleTolerance-map-string-string)
(definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map),
field is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
parentParameterName is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)) predicate

Creates a parameter group containing angle tolerance controls for the
specified field in the specified definition.

[getToleranceInfo](#getToleranceInfo-Context-Id-map-string-boolean)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map),
field is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
isAngle is [boolean](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean))
returns [ToleranceInfo](#ToleranceInfo)

[getToleranceInfo](#getToleranceInfo-map-string)
(definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map),
field is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))
returns [ToleranceInfo](#ToleranceInfo)

Extracts the [`ToleranceInfo`](#ToleranceInfo) associated with a given
field in the given feature definition. The
[`ToleranceInfo`](#ToleranceInfo) is gathered from parameters which are
created using either the
[`defineLengthTolerance`](#defineLengthTolerance-map-string-string) or
[`defineAngleTolerance`](#defineAngleTolerance-map-string-string)
predicates.

[updateFitToleranceFields](#updateFitToleranceFields-Context-Id-map-string)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map),
field is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))
returns [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

Updates the fit tolerance field information associated with a specified
field in the given feature definition. The tolerance information is
extracted from parameters created using the
[`defineLengthToleranceExtended`](#defineLengthToleranceExtended-map-string-string)
predicates.

  Parameter      Type                                       Additional Info
  -------------- ------------------------------------------ --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `context`      [`Context`](#Context)                      The target context.
  `id`           [`Id`](#Id)                                Identifier of the feature.
  `definition`   [`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)         The feature definition from which to extract and update tolerance information.
  `field`        [`string`](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)   The field name for which to update tolerance information.

  Return type                          Description
  ------------------------------------ --------------------------------------------------------------------
  [`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)   The updated feature definition with updated tolerance information.

[isToleranceSet](#isToleranceSet-ToleranceInfo)
(tolerance is [ToleranceInfo](#ToleranceInfo))
returns [boolean](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)

Determines whether or not a tolerance is set in a given
[`ToleranceInfo`](#ToleranceInfo). A tolerance is considered to be
\"set\" if either its tolerance type is set to a value other than
`NONE`, or if it has a precision override value set.

[isToleranceInfoOrUndefined](#isToleranceInfoOrUndefined-) (val)
predicate

Determines if a given value is either a
[`ToleranceInfo`](#ToleranceInfo) or `undefined`.

[getToleranceBounds](#getToleranceBounds-ValueWithUnits--map)
(nominal is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits),
tolerance is [ToleranceInfo](#ToleranceInfo),
options is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))
returns [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

Produces an array containing the upper and lower bounds of a
[`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits) given a specified
[`ToleranceInfo`](#ToleranceInfo) as well as a map of options.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `nominal`             | [`ValueWithUni        | the nominal value     |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| `tolerance`           | [`ToleranceI          | the tolerance info    |
|                       | nfo`](#ToleranceInfo) | for the given value   |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| `options`             | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `minimum`         | [`ValueWithUni        | the upper bound if    |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | the tolerance type is |
|                       |                       | maximum               |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `maximun`         | [`ValueWithUni        | the lower bound if    |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | the tolerance type is |
|                       |                       | minimum               |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | if false, uses the    |
| `useDrawingLimitsFix` | riables.html#boolean) | old bounds            |
|                       |                       | calculation of        |
|                       |                       | \\\[lowerLim,         |
|                       |                       | upperLim\\\]          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

## [toleranceTypes](#module-toleranceTypes.fs) {#module-toleranceTypes.fs .file-header}

[ToleranceType](#ToleranceType) enum

Defines the tolerance type of a hole feature\'s parameter.

  Value                  Description
  ---------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `NONE`                 Defines no tolerance.
  `SYMMETRICAL`          Defines a tolerance type where the allowed tolerance is a symmetrical deviation.
  `DEVIATION`            Defines a tolerance type where the allowed tolerance is an asymmetrical deviation.
  `LIMITS`               Defines a tolerance type where the allowed tolerance has defined upper and lower limits.
  `MIN`                  Defines a tolerance type where the parameter\'s value is considered the minimum allowed value.
  `MAX`                  Defines a tolerance type where the parameter\'s value is considered the maximum allowed value.
  `FIT`                  
  `FIT_WITH_TOLERANCE`   
  `FIT_TOLERANCE_ONLY`   
  `DEFAULT`              
  `BASIC`                

[ToleranceTypeExtended](#ToleranceTypeExtended) enum

Defines the tolerance type of a hole feature\'s parameter.

  Value                  Description
  ---------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `NONE`                 Defines no tolerance.
  `SYMMETRICAL`          Defines a tolerance type where the allowed tolerance is a symmetrical deviation.
  `DEVIATION`            Defines a tolerance type where the allowed tolerance is an asymmetrical deviation.
  `LIMITS`               Defines a tolerance type where the allowed tolerance has defined upper and lower limits.
  `MIN`                  Defines a tolerance type where the parameter\'s value is considered the minimum allowed value.
  `MAX`                  Defines a tolerance type where the parameter\'s value is considered the maximum allowed value.
  `FIT`                  Defines a tolerance type where the upper and lower limits are defined by the specified fit tolerance class.
  `FIT_WITH_TOLERANCE`   Defines a tolerance type where the upper and lower limits are defined by the specified fit tolerance class.
  `FIT_TOLERANCE_ONLY`   Defines a tolerance type where the upper and lower limits are defined by the specified fit tolerance class.

[ToleranceInfo](#ToleranceInfo) type

Stores tolerance-related info for a specific field in a feature. This
info includes an optional precision override, a tolerance type, and, if
applicable, an upper and lower bound associated with that tolerance
type.

Upper and lower tolerances are differences from the nominal value and
are both added to the nominal value.

EXAMPLE

> // +/- 1mm
>     {
>      toleranceType: ToleranceType.SYMMETRICAL,
>      upper: 1 * millimeter,
>      lower: -1 * millimeter
>     }

Tolerances need not be positive and negative. The only requirement is
that the upper tolerance is greater than the lower.

EXAMPLE

> // +0.1mm / +0.02mm
>     {
>      toleranceType: ToleranceType.DEVIATION,
>      upper: 0.1 * millimeter,
>      lower: 0.02 * millimeter
>     }

See also

[`setDimensionedEntities`](#setDimensionedEntities-Context-map)

Value

Type

Description

`ToleranceInfo`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `usePrecisionOverride`

[`boolean`](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)

*Optional*

Whether a precision override is defined

-   `precision`

[`number`](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

*Required if `usePrecisionOverride` is `true`*

The number of decimal places output for the parameter\'s nominal value
and tolerances

-   `toleranceType`

[`ToleranceType`](#ToleranceType)

-   `toleranceFitInfo`

[`ToleranceFitInfo`](#ToleranceFitInfo)

*Required if `toleranceType` is `FIT`, `FIT_WITH_TOLERANCE` or
`FIT_TOLERANCE_ONLY`*

Specifies the fit standard to be used for a hole or shaft

-   `upper`

[`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits)

*Required if `toleranceType` is `SYMMETRICAL`, `DEVIATION` or `LIMITS`*

Specifies the upper tolerance.

-   `lower`

[`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits)

*Required if `toleranceType` is `SYMMETRICAL`, `DEVIATION` or `LIMITS`*

Specifies the lower tolerance.

[canBeToleranceInfo](#canBeToleranceInfo-) (val) predicate

Type checking predicate for the [`ToleranceInfo`](#ToleranceInfo) type.

(info is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Constructor for the ToleranceInfo type.

[ToleranceFitInfo](#ToleranceFitInfo) type

Stores fit tolerance-related info for a specific field in a feature.

See also

[`setDimensionedEntities`](#setDimensionedEntities-Context-map) ,
[`ToleranceInfo`](#ToleranceInfo)

[PrecisionType](#PrecisionType) enum

Defines the precision of a tolerant quantity.

  Value                   Description
  ----------------------- ---------------------------------------
  `DEFAULT`               
  `ONES`                  Display precision up to \'1\'.
  `TENTHS`                Display precision up to \'0.1\'.
  `HUNDREDTHS`            Display precision up to \'0.01\'.
  `THOUSANDTHS`           Display precision up to \'0.001\'.
  `TEN_THOUSANDTHS`       Display precision up to \'0.0001\'.
  `HUNDRED_THOUSANDTHS`   Display precision up to \'0.00001\'.
  `MILLIONTHS`            Display precision up to \'0.000001\'.

Returns true if `edge` has two adjacent faces, false if `edge` is a
laminar or wire edge.

[isClosed](#isClosed-Context-Query) (context is [Context](#Context),
edge is [Query](#Query))
returns [boolean](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)

Returns true if `edge` is closed, false if `edge` is open

[dissolveWires](#dissolveWires-Query) (edgesAndWires is [Query](#Query))
returns [Query](#Query)

Returns the union of any edges from the input query and all the edges of
any body from the input query

[followWireEdgesToLaminarSource](#followWireEdgesToLaminarSource-Context-Query)
(context is [Context](#Context), query is [Query](#Query))
returns [Query](#Query)

If the query contains wire edges then this function will track the wire
edges back through creation history to find laminar edges that the edge
was copied from (or will return the original edge if none).

[extractDirection](#extractDirection-Context-Query)
(context is [Context](#Context), entity is [Query](#Query))

Extract a direction from an axis or a plane. Useful for processing query
parameters with the
[QueryFilterCompound.ALLOWS_DIRECTION](/FsDoc/library.html#QueryFilterCompound)
filter.

  Return type   Description
  ------------- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
                a 3D unit [`Vector`](#Vector) if a direction can be extracted, otherwise `undefined`.

[connectedComponents](#connectedComponents-Context-Query-AdjacencyType)
(context is [Context](#Context), entities is [Query](#Query),
adjacencyType is [AdjacencyType](#AdjacencyType))
returns [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

Find connected components in the topological graph of provided entities.
Each component is a group of topologically connected entities, and each
component is disjoint with (does not connect topologically with) any
other component. Connectivity is tested using
[`qAdjacent`](#qAdjacent-Query-AdjacencyType-EntityType) with the
specified `adjacencyType`.

Returns an array of components. Each component is an array of individual
queries. The queries in any component will respect the query evaluation
order of the supplied `entities` [`Query`](#Query). The components
themselves will also be ordered by query evaluation order, sorted by the
first entity in each component.

Unlike [`constructPath`](#constructPath-Context-Query), this function
operates on topological connections (underlying connections by a vertex
or edge). Distinct bodies are not topologically connected, so even if
two entities on distinct bodies are geometrically related by having a
coincident vertex or edge, the entities connected to these coincident
vertices or edges will fall into different components. Sketch edges are
each represented as a distinct wire body, and are not topologically
connected, so this method cannot be used for them.

[groupEntitiesByBody](#groupEntitiesByBody-Context-Query)
(context is [Context](#Context), entities is [Query](#Query))
returns [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

Group `entities` by their owner body.

  Return type                          Description
  ------------------------------------ -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  [`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)   a map from the transient query for an individual body to an array of transient queries for the individual entities which belong to that body.

[sweptAlong](#sweptAlong-Context-Query-Vector)
(context is [Context](#Context), face is [Query](#Query),
direction is [Vector](#Vector))
returns [boolean](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)

Check whether a face is swept along a specified direction.

[clusterBodies](#clusterBodies-Context-map)
(context is [Context](#Context),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))
returns [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

Groups bodies into clusters of identical topology and identical geometry
(up to a relative tolerance), allowing arbitrary 3D rotations (but not
reflection).

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `bodies`          | [`Query`](#Query)     | The bodies to cluster |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`number`](/FsDoc/v   | A tolerance,          |
|   `relativeTolerance` | ariables.html#number) | expressed as a        |
|                       |                       | decimal value, to     |
|                       |                       | compare bodies with.  |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `0.01` to cluster   |
|                       |                       | > bodies that have a  |
|                       |                       | > 1% similarity       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

  Return type                              Description
  ---------------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  [`array`](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)   an array of clusters, where each cluster is an array of indices into the result of evaluating `bodies`.

## [valueBounds](#module-valueBounds.fs) {#module-valueBounds.fs .file-header}

Value bounds are used to define minimum, maximum, and default values for
numbers and values with units. These bounds are in the feature dialog UI
for parameters with the given bounds.

In standard usage, a parameter can be specified with e.g. angle bounds
in a feature precondition as follows:

    isAngle(definition.myAngle, ANGLE_360_BOUNDS);

To change the bounds and defaults on the above declaration, a user may
replace ANGLE_360_BOUNDS with another `AngleBoundSpec` in this module,
or define their own. For instance, the following code creates a
parameter whose default value is 45 degrees (if the user\'s settings
have degrees as the default) or 1 radian (if the user\'s settings have
radians as the default).

    const MY_BOUNDS =
    {
        (degree) : [0, 45, 360],
        (radian) : 1
    } as AngleBoundSpec;
    ...
        isAngle(definition.myAngle, MY_BOUNDS);
    ...

[isLength](#isLength--LengthBoundSpec) (value,
boundSpec is [LengthBoundSpec](#LengthBoundSpec)) predicate

True for a value with length units which conforms to the given bounds.

Used in feature preconditions to specify a length parameter.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `boundSpec`           | [`LengthBoundSpe      | Specifies a min, a    |
|                       | c`](#LengthBoundSpec) | max, and a default    |
|                       |                       | value. These values   |
|                       |                       | are possibly          |
|                       |                       | different in          |
|                       |                       | different units for   |
|                       |                       | the sake of round     |
|                       |                       | numbers.              |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | To specify a          |
|                       |                       | parameter with        |
|                       |                       | different default     |
|                       |                       | value or different    |
|                       |                       | limits, use a         |
|                       |                       | different or custom   |
|                       |                       | `LengthBoundSpec`.    |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

to 90 degrees.

defaulting to 0 degrees.

[NONPOSITIVE_ZERO_DEFAULT_ANGLE_BOUNDS](#NONPOSITIVE_ZERO_DEFAULT_ANGLE_BOUNDS)
const

An`AngleBoundSpec` for an angle less than or equal to 0, with UI
defaults of 0.0 for all units.

[NONNEGATIVE_ZERO_DEFAULT_ANGLE_BOUNDS](#NONNEGATIVE_ZERO_DEFAULT_ANGLE_BOUNDS)
const

An `AngleBoundSpec` for an angle greater than or equal to 0, with UI
defaults of 0.0 for all units.

[POSITIVE_COUNT_BOUNDS](#POSITIVE_COUNT_BOUNDS) const

An `IntegerBoundSpec` for an integer strictly greater than zero,
defaulting to 2.

[POSITIVE_REAL_BOUNDS](#POSITIVE_REAL_BOUNDS) const

A `RealBoundSpec` for a number greater than or equal to zero, defaulting
to 1.

[SCALE_BOUNDS](#SCALE_BOUNDS) const

A `RealBoundSpec` for the positive or negative scale factor on a
transform, defaulting to `1`.

[LengthBoundSpec](#LengthBoundSpec) type

A spec to be used with the `isLength` predicate to define allowable
lengths and customize UI behaviors for feature dialog parameters that
take in a length.

A typical declaration looks like:

    const MY_LENGTH_BOUNDS =
    {
        (meter)      : [-500, 0.0025, 500],
        (centimeter) : .25,
        (millimeter) : 2.50,
        (inch)       : 0.1,
        (foot)       : 0.01,
        (yard)       : 0.0025
    } as LengthBoundSpec;

The values for `(meter)`, `(inch)`, etc. define the bounds and default
values for a feature parameter defined with `MY_LENGTH_BOUNDS`. The
default values will be different for users who have set different
default units.

Specifically, the first unit listed specified defines the minimum value,
default value, and the maximum value (in terms of that unit) and the
subsequent units define default values for those units, when a user with
those default units first opens the dialog. The default value for a unit
that is not listed is the default value of the first unit so
`{ (inch) : [0, 1, 1e4] } as LengthBoundSpec` will give a default of
`2.54 cm` in a Part Studio with centimeter units

[canBeLengthBoundSpec](#canBeLengthBoundSpec-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for LengthBoundSpec

[AngleBoundSpec](#AngleBoundSpec) type

A spec to be used with the `isAngle` predicate to define allowable
angles and customize UI behaviors for feature dialog parameters that
take in an angle.

A typical declaration looks like:

    const ANGLE_360_BOUNDS =
    {
        (degree) : [0, 30, 360],
        (radian) : 1
    } as AngleBoundSpec;

For more information on what the various fields signify, see
`LengthBoundSpec`.

[canBeAngleBoundSpec](#canBeAngleBoundSpec-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for AngleBoundSpec

[IntegerBoundSpec](#IntegerBoundSpec) type

A spec to be used with the `isInteger` predicate to define allowable
numbers and customize UI behaviors for feature dialog parameters that
take in a number.

A typical declaration looks like:

    const POSITIVE_COUNT_BOUNDS =
    {
        (unitless) : [1, 2, 1e5]
    } as IntegerBoundSpec;

For more information on what the various fields signify, see
`LengthBoundSpec`.

[canBeIntegerBoundSpec](#canBeIntegerBoundSpec-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for IntegerBoundSpec

[RealBoundSpec](#RealBoundSpec) type

A spec to be used with the `isReal` predicate to define allowable real
numbers and customize UI behaviors for feature dialog parameters that
take in a real number.

A typical declaration looks like:

    const POSITIVE_REAL_BOUNDS =
    {
        (unitless) : [0, 1, 1e5]
    } as RealBoundSpec;

For more information on what the various fields signify, see
`LengthBoundSpec`.

[canBeRealBoundSpec](#canBeRealBoundSpec-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for RealBoundSpec

[SWEEP_PITCH_BOUNDS](#SWEEP_PITCH_BOUNDS) const

A `LengthBoundSpec` for a sweep pitch. (`0.1 * inch`, etc.).

[SWEEP_TURNS_BOUNDS](#SWEEP_TURNS_BOUNDS) const

A `RealBoundSpec` for a sweep turns.

[SWEEP_ANGLE_BOUNDS](#SWEEP_ANGLE_BOUNDS) const

An `AngleBoundSpec` for sweep twist angles.

## [wrapSurface](#module-wrapSurface.fs) {#module-wrapSurface.fs .file-header}

[WrapSurface](#WrapSurface) type

Represents the `source` or `destination` surface for
[`opWrap`](#opWrap-Context-Id-map). Exactly one of `face`, `plane`,
`cylinder`, or `cone` must be defined.

(Formerly `RollSurface`)

Value

Type

Description

`WrapSurface`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `face`

[`Query`](#Query)

The face entity defining this `WrapSurface`.

-   `plane`

[`Plane`](#Plane)

The plane geometry defining this `WrapSurface`.

-   `cylinder`

[`Cylinder`](#Cylinder)

The cylinder geometry defining this `WrapSurface`.

-   `cone`

[`Cone`](#Cone)

The cone geometry defining this `WrapSurface`.

-   `anchorPoint`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

The anchor point of the `WrapSurface`, used to align the `source` and
`destination` `WrapSurface`s for [`opWrap`](#opWrap-Context-Id-map).
Must lie on the `WrapSurface`. If this condition is not met,
[`opWrap`](#opWrap-Context-Id-map) will fail.

-   `anchorDirection`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

The anchor direction of the `WrapSurface`, used to align the `source`
and `destination` `WrapSurface`s for [`opWrap`](#opWrap-Context-Id-map).
Must lie on the tangent plane of the `WrapSurface` at the `anchorPoint`.
If this condition is not met, [`opWrap`](#opWrap-Context-Id-map) will
fail.

[isWrapPlane](#isWrapPlane-Context-WrapSurface)
(context is [Context](#Context), val is [WrapSurface](#WrapSurface))
predicate

Returns whether the given [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) is a plane.

  Parameter   Type                            Additional Info
  ----------- ------------------------------- -----------------------------
  `val`       [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface)   The `WrapSurface` to check.
