(context is [Context](#Context), val is [WrapSurface](#WrapSurface))
predicate

Returns whether the given [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) is a cylinder.

  Parameter   Type                            Additional Info
  ----------- ------------------------------- -----------------------------
  `val`       [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface)   The `WrapSurface` to check.

[isWrapCone](#isWrapCone-Context-WrapSurface)
(context is [Context](#Context), val is [WrapSurface](#WrapSurface))
predicate

Returns whether the given [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) is a cone.

  Parameter   Type                            Additional Info
  ----------- ------------------------------- -----------------------------
  `val`       [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface)   The `WrapSurface` to check.

[makeWrapSurface](#makeWrapSurface-Context-Query)
(context is [Context](#Context), face is [Query](#Query))
returns [WrapSurface](#WrapSurface)

Make a [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) for
[`opWrap`](#opWrap-Context-Id-map) defined by a planar or cylindrical
face.

  Parameter   Type                Additional Info
  ----------- ------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `face`      [`Query`](#Query)   A face to use as the definition face for the `WrapSurface`. For a planar face: The plane `origin` will be used as the `anchorPoint` and the plane `x` direction will be used as the `anchorDirection`. For a cylindrical face: The point at which the cylinder\'s `coordSystem`\'s positive `xAxis` intersects the cylinder will be used as the `anchorPoint`. The cylinder\'s `yAxis` will be used as the `anchorDirection`. See [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) documentation for descriptions of `anchorPoint` and `anchorDirection`.

[makeWrapSurface](#makeWrapSurface-Context-Query-Vector-ValueWithUnits)
(context is [Context](#Context), face is [Query](#Query),
anchorPoint is [Vector](#Vector),
spin is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits))
returns [WrapSurface](#WrapSurface)

Make a [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) for
[`opWrap`](#opWrap-Context-Id-map) defined by a planar, cylindrical or
conical face.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `face`                | [`Query`](#Query)     | A face to use as the  |
|                       |                       | definition face for   |
|                       |                       | the `WrapSurface`.    |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| `anchorPoint`         | [`Vector`](#Vector)   | The anchor point of   |
|                       |                       | the `WrapSurface`.    |
|                       |                       | See `WrapSurface`     |
|                       |                       | documentation.        |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| `spin`                | [`ValueWithUni        | For a planar face:    |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | Angle of a            |
|                       |                       | counter-clockwise     |
|                       |                       | spin to apply to the  |
|                       |                       | `plane`\'s `x` axis   |
|                       |                       | about its positive    |
|                       |                       | `z` axis, which is    |
|                       |                       | then used as the      |
|                       |                       | `anchorDirection`.    |
|                       |                       | For a cylindrical     |
|                       |                       | face: Angle of a      |
|                       |                       | counter-clockwise     |
|                       |                       | spin to apply to the  |
|                       |                       | `cylinder`\'s         |
|                       |                       | canonical             |
|                       |                       | `anchorDirection` at  |
|                       |                       | the given             |
|                       |                       | `anchorPoint` about   |
|                       |                       | the direction vector  |
|                       |                       | from the cylinder     |
|                       |                       | axis to the           |
|                       |                       | `anchorPoint`. The    |
|                       |                       | canonical             |
|                       |                       | `anchorDirection` at  |
|                       |                       | the given             |
|                       |                       | `anchorPoint` is      |
|                       |                       | defined as the        |
|                       |                       | \"rightward\"         |
|                       |                       | direction when        |
|                       |                       | looking at the        |
|                       |                       | cylinder in the       |
|                       |                       | direction from the    |
|                       |                       | `anchorPoint` to the  |
|                       |                       | cylinder axis with    |
|                       |                       | the cylinder\'s       |
|                       |                       | `zAxis` up. In other  |
|                       |                       | words:                |
|                       |                       | `cross(cylinde        |
|                       |                       | r z axis, direction v |
|                       |                       | ector from cylinder a |
|                       |                       | xis to anchorPoint)`. |
|                       |                       | This spun direction   |
|                       |                       | is then used as the   |
|                       |                       | `anchorDirection`.    |
|                       |                       | For a conical face:   |
|                       |                       | Angle of a            |
|                       |                       | counter-clockwise     |
|                       |                       | spin to apply to the  |
|                       |                       | `cone`\'s canonical   |
|                       |                       | `anchorDirection` at  |
|                       |                       | the given             |
|                       |                       | `anchorPoint` about   |
|                       |                       | the normal at         |
|                       |                       | `anchorPoint`. The    |
|                       |                       | canonical             |
|                       |                       | `anchorDirection` at  |
|                       |                       | the given             |
|                       |                       | `anchorPoint` is      |
|                       |                       | defined as the        |
|                       |                       | \"rightward\"         |
|                       |                       | direction when        |
|                       |                       | looking at the cone   |
|                       |                       | from the normal.      |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | See                   |
|                       |                       | [`WrapSu              |
|                       |                       | rface`](#WrapSurface) |
|                       |                       | documentation for a   |
|                       |                       | description of        |
|                       |                       | `anchorDirection`.    |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[makeWrapSurface](#makeWrapSurface-Context-Query-Vector-Vector)
(context is [Context](#Context), face is [Query](#Query),
anchorPoint is [Vector](#Vector), anchorDirection is [Vector](#Vector))
returns [WrapSurface](#WrapSurface)

See [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) documentation for descriptions of `anchorPoint` and `anchorDirection`.

[makeWrapPlane](#makeWrapPlane-Plane-Vector-ValueWithUnits)
(plane is [Plane](#Plane), anchorPoint is [Vector](#Vector),
spin is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits))
returns [WrapSurface](#WrapSurface)

Make a [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) for
[`opWrap`](#opWrap-Context-Id-map) from a [`Plane`](#Plane).

  --------------- ------------------------------------- -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `plane`         [`Plane`](#Plane)                     The definition plane for the `WrapSurface`.
  `anchorPoint`   [`Vector`](#Vector)                   The anchor point of the `WrapSurface`. See [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) documentation.
  `spin`          [`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits)   Angle of a counter-clockwise spin to apply to the `plane`\'s `x` axis about its positive `z` axis, which is then used as the `anchorDirection`. See [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) documentation for a description of `anchorDirection`.

[flipWrapPlane](#flipWrapPlane-WrapSurface)
(wrapPlane is [WrapSurface](#WrapSurface))
returns [WrapSurface](#WrapSurface)

Flip the normal direction of the [`Plane`](#Plane) described by this
[`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface). Do not change the `anchorPoint` or
`anchorDirection`.

[makeWrapCylinder](#makeWrapCylinder-Cylinder)
(cylinder is [Cylinder](#Cylinder)) returns [WrapSurface](#WrapSurface)

Make a [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) for
[`opWrap`](#opWrap-Context-Id-map) from a [`Cylinder`](#Cylinder).

  Parameter    Type                      Additional Info
  ------------ ------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `cylinder`   [`Cylinder`](#Cylinder)   The definition cylinder for the `WrapSurface`. The point at which the cylinder\'s `coordSystem`\'s positive `xAxis` intersects the cylinder will be used as the `anchorPoint`. The cylinder\'s `yAxis` will be used as the `anchorDirection`. See [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) documentation for descriptions of `anchorPoint` and `anchorDirection`.

[makeWrapCylinder](#makeWrapCylinder-Cylinder-Vector-ValueWithUnits)
(cylinder is [Cylinder](#Cylinder), anchorPoint is [Vector](#Vector),
spin is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits))
returns [WrapSurface](#WrapSurface)

Make a [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) for
[`opWrap`](#opWrap-Context-Id-map) from a [`Cylinder`](#Cylinder).

  Parameter       Type                                  Additional Info
  --------------- ------------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `cylinder`      [`Cylinder`](#Cylinder)               The definition cylinder for the `WrapSurface`.
  `anchorPoint`   [`Vector`](#Vector)                   The anchor point of the `WrapSurface`. See [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) documentation.
  `spin`          [`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits)   Angle of a counter-clockwise spin to apply to the `cylinder`\'s canonical `anchorDirection` at the given `anchorPoint` about the direction vector from the cylinder axis to the `anchorPoint`. The canonical `anchorDirection` at the given `anchorPoint` is defined as the \"rightward\" direction when looking at the cylinder in the direction from the `anchorPoint` to the cylinder axis with the cylinder\'s `zAxis` up. In other words: `cross(cylinder z axis, direction vector from cylinder axis to anchorPoint)`. This spun direction is then used as the `anchorDirection`. See [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) documentation for a description of `anchorDirection`.

[makeWrapCylinder](#makeWrapCylinder-Cylinder-Vector-Vector)
(cylinder is [Cylinder](#Cylinder), anchorPoint is [Vector](#Vector),
anchorDirection is [Vector](#Vector))
returns [WrapSurface](#WrapSurface)

[`opWrap`](#opWrap-Context-Id-map) from a [`Cylinder`](#Cylinder).

  Parameter           Type                      Additional Info
  ------------------- ------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `cylinder`          [`Cylinder`](#Cylinder)   The definition cylinder for the `WrapSurface`.
  `anchorPoint`       [`Vector`](#Vector)       The anchor point of the `WrapSurface`. See [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) documentation.
  `anchorDirection`   [`Vector`](#Vector)       The anchor direction of the `WrapSurface`. See [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) documentation.

  `anchorDirection`   [`Vector`](#Vector)   The anchor direction of the `WrapSurface`. See [`WrapSurface`](#WrapSurface) documentation.

## Onshape features

## [bodyDraft](#module-bodyDraft.fs) {#module-bodyDraft.fs .file-header}

[bodyDraft](#bodyDraft-Context-Id-map) (context is [Context](#Context),
id is [Id](#Id), definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

An operation that performs an
[`opBodyDraft`](#opBodyDraft-Context-Id-map).

[bodyDraftEditLogic](#bodyDraftEditLogic-Context-Id-map-map-boolean-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
oldDefinition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map),
isCreating is [boolean](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean),
specifiedParameters is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

body draft editing logic

## [boolean](#module-boolean.fs) {#module-boolean.fs .file-header}

[booleanBodies](#booleanBodies-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

The boolean feature. Performs an
[`opBoolean`](#opBoolean-Context-Id-map) after a possible
[`opOffsetFace`](#opOffsetFace-Context-Id-map) if the operation is
subtraction.

[convertNewBodyOpToBoolOp](#convertNewBodyOpToBoolOp-NewBodyOperationType)
(operationType is [NewBodyOperationType](#NewBodyOperationType))
returns [BooleanOperationType](#BooleanOperationType)

Maps a [`NewBodyOperationType`](#NewBodyOperationType) (used in features
like [`extrude`](#extrude-Context-Id-map)) to its corresponding
[`BooleanOperationType`](#BooleanOperationType).

[booleanStepTypePredicate](#booleanStepTypePredicate-map)
(booleanDefinition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)) predicate

Predicate which specifies a field `operationType` of type
[`NewBodyOperationType`](#NewBodyOperationType). Used by body-creating
feature preconditions such as extrude, revolve, sweep or loft.

When used in a precondition,
[`NewBodyOperationType`](#NewBodyOperationType) creates UI like the
extrude feature, with a horizontal list of the words \"New\", \"Add\",
etc. When using this predicate in features, make sure to export an
import of `tool.fs` so that
[`NewBodyOperationType`](#NewBodyOperationType) is visible to the Part
Studios:

    export import(path : "onshape/std/tool.fs", version : "");

[booleanStepScopePredicate](#booleanStepScopePredicate-map)
(booleanDefinition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)) predicate

Used by body-creating feature preconditions to allow post-creation
booleans, specifying the merge scope (or \"Merge with all\") for that
boolean.

Designed to be used together with
[`booleanStepTypePredicate`](#booleanStepTypePredicate-map).

[booleanPatternScopePredicate](#booleanPatternScopePredicate-map)
(booleanDefinition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)) predicate

Used by body-creating pattern feature preconditions to allow
post-creation booleans with surfaces or solids, specifying the merge
scope (or \"Merge with all\") for that boolean.

[processNewBodyIfNeeded](#processNewBodyIfNeeded-Context-Id-map-function)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map),
reconstructOp is [function](/FsDoc/variables.html#function))

This function is designed to be used by body-creating features (like
[`extrude`](#extrude-Context-Id-map)) as a boolean post-processing step
with options from
[`booleanStepTypePredicate`](#booleanStepTypePredicate-map) and
[`booleanStepScopePredicate`](#booleanStepScopePredicate-map) in the
case where the preceding operations of the feature have created new
solid or surface bodies. On top of the regular boolean operation,
converts the `operationType` and creates error bodies on failure.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `id`                  | [`Id`](#Id)           | identifier of the     |
|                       |                       | main feature          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `operationType`   | [`New                 | EXAMPLE               |
|                       | BodyOperationType`](# |                       |
|                       | NewBodyOperationType) | > `NewB               |
|                       |                       | odyOperationType.ADD` |
|                       |                       | > performs a boolean  |
|                       |                       | > union               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `NewB               |
|                       |                       | odyOperationType.NEW` |
|                       |                       | > does nothing        |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `defaultScope`    | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `true` indicates    |
|                       |                       | > merge scope of      |
|                       |                       | > \"everything else\" |
|                       |                       | > (default)           |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `false` indicates   |
|                       |                       | > merge scope is      |
|                       |                       | > specified in        |
|                       |                       | > `booleanScope`      |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `booleanScope`    | [`Query`](#Query)     | targets to use if     |
|                       |                       | `defaultScope` is     |
|                       |                       | false                 |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `seed`            | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Optional*            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | If set, will be       |
|                       |                       | included in the tools |
|                       |                       | section of the        |
|                       |                       | boolean.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Optional*            |
| `mergeScopeExclusion` |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | If set, will be       |
|                       |                       | excluded from the     |
|                       |                       | targets section of    |
|                       |                       | the boolean.          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| `reconstructOp`       | [`                    | A function which      |
|                       | function`](/FsDoc/var | takes in an Id, and   |
|                       | iables.html#function) | reconstructs the      |
|                       |                       | input to show to the  |
|                       |                       | user as error         |
|                       |                       | geometry in case the  |
|                       |                       | input is problematic  |
|                       |                       | or the boolean itself |
|                       |                       | fails.                |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `function() {}` .   |
|                       |                       | > For a more          |
|                       |                       | > elaborate example   |
|                       |                       | > see the source code |
|                       |                       | > of revolve feature  |
|                       |                       | > in the Standard     |
|                       |                       | > Library.            |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[surfaceOperationTypePredicate](#surfaceOperationTypePredicate-map)
(surfaceDefinition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)) predicate

Predicate which specifies a field `surfaceOperationType` of type
[`NewSurfaceOperationType`](#NewSurfaceOperationType). Used by
surface-creating feature preconditions such as revolve, sweep or loft.

When used in a precondition,
[`NewSurfaceOperationType`](#NewSurfaceOperationType) creates UI like
the sweep feature, with a horizontal list of the words \"New\" and
\"Add\". When using this predicate in features, make sure to export an
import of `tool.fs` so that
[`NewSurfaceOperationType`](#NewSurfaceOperationType) is visible to the
Part Studios:

    export import(path : "onshape/std/tool.fs", version : "");

[surfaceJoinStepScopePredicate](#surfaceJoinStepScopePredicate-map)
(definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)) predicate

Used by surface-creating feature preconditions to allow post-creation
booleans, specifying the merge scope (or \"Merge with all\") for that
boolean.

Designed to be used together with
[`surfaceOperationTypePredicate`](#surfaceOperationTypePredicate-map).

[joinSurfaceBodiesWithAutoMatching](#joinSurfaceBodiesWithAutoMatching-Context-Id-map-boolean-function)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map),
makeSolid is [boolean](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean),
reconstructOp is [function](/FsDoc/variables.html#function))

This function is designed to be used by surface-body-creating features
(like [`extrude`](#extrude-Context-Id-map)) as a boolean post-processing
step with options from
[`surfaceOperationTypePredicate`](#surfaceOperationTypePredicate-map)
and
[`surfaceJoinStepScopePredicate`](#surfaceJoinStepScopePredicate-map).
It detects matching edges of adjacent bodies and joins surface bodies at
these edges.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `id`                  | [`Id`](#Id)           | identifier of the     |
|                       |                       | feature               |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
| `defaultSurfaceScope` | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `true` indicates    |
|                       |                       | > merge scope of all  |
|                       |                       | > the original and    |
|                       |                       | > related surfaces    |
|                       |                       | > used as input to    |
|                       |                       | > create this surface |
|                       |                       | > (default)           |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `false` indicates   |
|                       |                       | > merge scope is      |
|                       |                       | > specified in        |
|                       |                       | >                     |
|                       |                       | `booleanSurfaceScope` |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Optional*            |
| `booleanSurfaceScope` |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | targets to use if     |
|                       |                       | `defaultSurfaceScope` |
|                       |                       | is false Default is   |
|                       |                       | `qNothing()`          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `seed`            | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Optional*            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | Default is            |
|                       |                       | `qNothing()` If set,  |
|                       |                       | will be included in   |
|                       |                       | the tools section of  |
|                       |                       | the boolean.          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| `makeSolid`           | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | Tries to join the     |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) | surfaces into a solid |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| `reconstructOp`       | [`                    | A function which      |
|                       | function`](/FsDoc/var | takes in an Id, and   |
|                       | iables.html#function) | reconstructs the      |
|                       |                       | input to show to the  |
|                       |                       | user as error         |
|                       |                       | geometry in case the  |
|                       |                       | input is problematic  |
|                       |                       | or the join itself    |
|                       |                       | fails.                |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `function() {}` .   |
|                       |                       | > For a more          |
|                       |                       | > elaborate example   |
|                       |                       | > see the source code |
|                       |                       | > of revolve feature  |
|                       |                       | > in the Standard     |
|                       |                       | > Library.            |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[joinSurfaceBodiesWithAutoMatching](#joinSurfaceBodiesWithAutoMatching-Context-Id-map-function)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map),
reconstructOp is [function](/FsDoc/variables.html#function))

This function is designed to be used by surface-body-creating features
(like [`extrude`](#extrude-Context-Id-map)) as a boolean post-processing
step with options from
[`surfaceOperationTypePredicate`](#surfaceOperationTypePredicate-map)
and
[`surfaceJoinStepScopePredicate`](#surfaceJoinStepScopePredicate-map).
It detects matching edges of adjacent bodies and joins surface bodies at
these edges.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `id`                  | [`Id`](#Id)           | identifier of the     |
|                       |                       | feature               |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
| `defaultSurfaceScope` | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `true` indicates    |
|                       |                       | > merge scope of all  |
|                       |                       | > the original and    |
|                       |                       | > related surfaces    |
|                       |                       | > used as input to    |
|                       |                       | > create this surface |
|                       |                       | > (default)           |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `false` indicates   |
|                       |                       | > merge scope is      |
|                       |                       | > specified in        |
|                       |                       | >                     |
|                       |                       | `booleanSurfaceScope` |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Optional*            |
| `booleanSurfaceScope` |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | targets to use if     |
|                       |                       | `defaultSurfaceScope` |
|                       |                       | is false Default is   |
|                       |                       | `qNothing()`          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `seed`            | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Optional*            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | Default is            |
|                       |                       | `qNothing()` If set,  |
|                       |                       | will be included in   |
|                       |                       | the tools section of  |
|                       |                       | the boolean.          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `makeSolid`       | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | Tries to join the     |
|                       |                       | surfaces into a solid |
|                       |                       | Default is false      |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
| `eraseImprintedEdges` | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | Merge all mergeable   |
|                       |                       | imprinted edges       |
|                       |                       | created by the        |
|                       |                       | boolean operation     |
|                       |                       | Default is true       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| `reconstructOp`       | [`                    | A function which      |
|                       | function`](/FsDoc/var | takes in an Id, and   |
|                       | iables.html#function) | reconstructs the      |
|                       |                       | input to show to the  |
|                       |                       | user as error         |
|                       |                       | geometry in case the  |
|                       |                       | input is problematic  |
|                       |                       | or the join itself    |
|                       |                       | fails.                |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `function() {}` .   |
|                       |                       | > For a more          |
|                       |                       | > elaborate example   |
|                       |                       | > see the source code |
|                       |                       | > of revolve feature  |
|                       |                       | > in the Standard     |
|                       |                       | > Library.            |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

## [bridgingCurve](#module-bridgingCurve.fs) {#module-bridgingCurve.fs .file-header}

[BridgingCurveMatchType](#BridgingCurveMatchType) enum

Specifies how the bridging curve will match the vertex or edge at each
side

  Value         Description
  ------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `POSITION`    The bridging curve will end at the provided vertex. Direction of the curve is unspecified
  `TANGENCY`    The bridging curve will end at the vertex and the curve will be tangent to the edge
  `CURVATURE`   The bridging curve will end at the vertex and the curve will have same curvature as the edge at the vertex
  `G3`          

[BIAS_BOUNDS](#BIAS_BOUNDS) const

A `RealBoundSpec` for bias of a tangency/tangency bridge, defaulting to
`0.5`.

[bridgingCurve](#bridgingCurve-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Creates a curve between two points, optionally with matching of tangency
or curvature to other curves at that point

[SideQueries](#SideQueries) type

Data type for side queries

Value

Type

Description

`SideQueries`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `vertex`

[`Query`](#Query)

*Optional*

The vertex element on a side query

-   `edge`

[`Query`](#Query)

*Optional*

The edge element on a side query

-   `face`

[`Query`](#Query)

*Optional*

The face element on a side query

[SideData](#SideData) type

Data type for precomputed side data

Value

Type

Description

`SideData`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `point`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

The position

-   `frame`

[`EdgeCurvatureResult`](#EdgeCurvatureResult)

*Optional*

The coordSystem and curvature at the given position

[TwoSidesData](#TwoSidesData) type

Aggregation of both sides SideData

Value

Type

Description

`TwoSidesData`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `side1`

[`SideData`](#SideData)

SideData map for start side

-   `side2`

[`SideData`](#SideData)

SideData map for end side

[BridgingSideData](#BridgingSideData) type

Data type for unified control points computation

Value

Type

Description

`BridgingSideData`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `degree`

[`number`](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

0 for positional continuity (G0), 1 for tangent continuity (G1), 2 for
curvature continuity (G2)

-   `position`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

The position

-   `tangent`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

*Required if `degree` \>= 1*

The tangent direction vector

-   `speedScale`

[`number`](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

*Optional*

How much to scale the default speed

-   `curvatureDirection`

[`Vector`](#Vector)

*Required if `degree` == 2*

The curvature direction vector

-   `curvature`

[`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits)

*Required if `degree` == 2*

The curvature magnitude (inverse length units)

-   `curvatureOffsetScale`

[`number`](/FsDoc/variables.html#number)

*Optional*

How much to scale the default third control point offset

[computeBridgingControlPoints](#computeBridgingControlPoints-Context-BridgingSideData-BridgingSideData)
(context is [Context](#Context),
side1 is [BridgingSideData](#BridgingSideData),
side2 is [BridgingSideData](#BridgingSideData))
returns [array](/FsDoc/variables.html#array)

Returns an array of control points for a bridigng bezier curve given two
side constraints

## [circularPattern](#module-circularPattern.fs) {#module-circularPattern.fs .file-header}

[circularPattern](#circularPattern-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Performs a body, face, or feature circular pattern. Internally, performs
an [`applyPattern`](#applyPattern-Context-Id-map-Transform), which in
turn performs an [`opPattern`](#opPattern-Context-Id-map) or, for a
feature pattern, calls the feature function.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `patternType`     | [`Patter              | *Optional*            |
|                       | nType`](#PatternType) |                       |
|                       |                       | Specifies a `PART`,   |
|                       |                       | `FEATURE`, or `FACE`  |
|                       |                       | pattern. Default is   |
|                       |                       | `PART`.               |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `entities`        | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Required if          |
|                       |                       | `patternType` is      |
|                       |                       | `PART`*               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | The parts to pattern. |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `qCr                |
|                       |                       | eatedBy(id + "extrude |
|                       |                       | 1", EntityType.BODY)` |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `faces`           | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Required if          |
|                       |                       | `patternType` is      |
|                       |                       | `FACE`*               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | The faces to pattern. |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`Featur              | *Required if          |
|    `instanceFunction` | eList`](#FeatureList) | `patternType` is      |
|                       |                       | `FEATURE`*            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | The                   |
|                       |                       | [`Featur              |
|                       |                       | eList`](#FeatureList) |
|                       |                       | of the features to    |
|                       |                       | pattern.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `axis`            | [`Query`](#Query)     | The axis of the       |
|                       |                       | pattern.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `angle`           | [`ValueWithUni        | The angle between     |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | each pattern          |
|                       |                       | instance, or the      |
|                       |                       | total angle spanned   |
|                       |                       | by the pattern if     |
|                       |                       | `equalSpace` is       |
|                       |                       | `true`.               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `360 * degree`      |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `instanceCount`   | [`number`](/FsDoc/v   | The resulting number  |
|                       | ariables.html#number) | of pattern entities,  |
|                       |                       | unless `isCentered`   |
|                       |                       | is `true`.            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `4` to have 4       |
|                       |                       | > resulting pattern   |
|                       |                       | > entities (including |
|                       |                       | > the seed).          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|   `oppositeDirection` | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `true` to switch    |
|                       |                       | > the direction of    |
|                       |                       | > the pattern around  |
|                       |                       | > the axis.           |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `equalSpace`      | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `true` for the      |
|                       |                       | > entire pattern to   |
|                       |                       | > lie within `angle`  |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `false` for there   |
|                       |                       | > to be `angle`       |
|                       |                       | > between each        |
|                       |                       | > pattern instance    |
|                       |                       | > (default)           |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `isCentered`      | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | Whether to center the |
|                       |                       | pattern on the seed.  |
|                       |                       | When set to `true`,   |
|                       |                       | `instanceCount - 1`   |
|                       |                       | pattern entities are  |
|                       |                       | created in each       |
|                       |                       | direction around the  |
|                       |                       | axis. Default is      |
|                       |                       | `false`.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `operationType`   | [`New                 | *Optional*            |
|                       | BodyOperationType`](# |                       |
|                       | NewBodyOperationType) | Specifies how the     |
|                       |                       | newly created body    |
|                       |                       | will be merged with   |
|                       |                       | existing bodies.      |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `defaultScope`    | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `true` to merge     |
|                       |                       | > with all other      |
|                       |                       | > bodies              |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `false` to merge    |
|                       |                       | > with `booleanScope` |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `booleanScope`    | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Required if          |
|                       |                       | `defaultScope` is     |
|                       |                       | `false`*              |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | The specified bodies  |
|                       |                       | to merge with.        |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `skipInstances`   | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | Whether to exclude    |
|                       |                       | certain instances of  |
|                       |                       | the pattern.          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | *Required if          |
|    `skippedInstances` | variables.html#array) | `skipInstances` is    |
|                       |                       | `true`*               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | Which instances of    |
|                       |                       | the pattern to skip.  |
|                       |                       | Each is denoted by a  |
|                       |                       | single index, which   |
|                       |                       | may be negative if    |
|                       |                       | `isCentered` is       |
|                       |                       | `true`.               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `[{                 |
|                       |                       |  index: -3 }, { index |
|                       |                       | : 2 }, { index: 5 }]` |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

## [compositeCurve](#module-compositeCurve.fs) {#module-compositeCurve.fs .file-header}

## [constrainedSurface](#module-constrainedSurface.fs) {#module-constrainedSurface.fs .file-header}

[ConstrainedSurfaceType](#ConstrainedSurfaceType) enum

Constrained surface input type

  Value      Description
  ---------- -------------
  `POINTS`   
  `MESH`     

[OptimizationMethod](#OptimizationMethod) enum

Constrained surface optimization type

  Value      Description
  ---------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `PERF`     Faster performance that may produce lower-quality surfaces.
  `SMOOTH`   Typically produces higher-quality surfaces with lower curvatures, but with slower performance and more control points.

[constrainedSurface](#constrainedSurface-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Constrained surface feature. Takes an arbitrary number of vertices and
optional normal directions or meshes and creates a surface passing
through the vertices/mesh points within a provided tolerance.

    constrainedSurface(context, id + "FG2MuQGCNLssvyp_123", {
         "csType" : ConstrainedSurfaceType.MESH,
         "vertices" : [],
         "meshes" : qUnion([iLEjUaBPUqVOpC_query]),
         "tolerance" : { 'value' : try(1 * inch), 'expression' : "1 in" }.value,
         "optimize" : OptimizationMethod.PERF
    });

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `csType`          | [`Constra             | Determines if the     |
|                       | inedSurfaceType`](#Co | input is a mesh or a  |
|                       | nstrainedSurfaceType) | collection of points. |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `vertices`        | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | *Required if `csType` |
|                       | variables.html#array) | is                    |
|                       |                       | `Constraine           |
|                       |                       | dSurfaceType.POINTS`* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | An array of elements, |
|                       |                       | each with a `vertex`  |
|                       |                       | on the surface to     |
|                       |                       | include. Optionally,  |
|                       |                       | specify a directional |
|                       |                       | object to set the     |
|                       |                       | normal in the         |
|                       |                       | `normal` field, and   |
|                       |                       | set `flipNormal` to   |
|                       |                       | `true` to flip the    |
|                       |                       | normal.               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > [                   |
|                       |                       | >                     |
|                       |                       | {"vertex": qVertex1}, |
|                       |                       | >                     |
|                       |                       | {"vertex": qVertex2}, |
|                       |                       | >                     |
|                       |                       |  {"vertex": qVertex3, |
|                       |                       |  "normal": qNormal1}, |
|                       |                       | >                     |
|                       |                       |    {"vertex": qVertex |
|                       |                       | 4, "normal": qNormal2 |
|                       |                       | , "flipNormal": true} |
|                       |                       | >     ]               |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `meshes`          | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Required if `csType` |
|                       |                       | is                    |
|                       |                       | `Constrai             |
|                       |                       | nedSurfaceType.MESH`* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | A query of mesh faces |
|                       |                       | or bodies. Each mesh  |
|                       |                       | vertex of the query   |
|                       |                       | elements is on the    |
|                       |                       | constrained surface.  |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `                   |
|                       |                       | "meshes" : qUnion([iL |
|                       |                       | EjUaBPUqVOpC_query])` |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `tolerance`       | [`ValueWithUni        | Length to define how  |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | far from each vertex  |
|                       |                       | the constrained       |
|                       |                       | surface can be.       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `optimize`        | [                     | Whether to optimize   |
|                       | `OptimizationMethod`] | for performance       |
|                       | (#OptimizationMethod) | (`Opti                |
|                       |                       | mizationMethod.PERF`) |
|                       |                       | or smoothness         |
|                       |                       | (`Optimiz             |
|                       |                       | ationMethod.SMOOTH`). |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

## [curvePattern](#module-curvePattern.fs) {#module-curvePattern.fs .file-header}

[CurvePatternSpacingType](#CurvePatternSpacingType) enum

Specifies the type of spacing between pattern instances.

  Value        Description
  ------------ --------------------------------------------------
  `EQUAL`      Equal-spaced instances along the length of curve
  `DISTANCE`   Instances spaced by custom distance

[curvePattern](#curvePattern-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Performs a body, face, or feature curve pattern. Internally, performs an
[`applyPattern`](#applyPattern-Context-Id-map-Transform), which in turn
performs an [`opPattern`](#opPattern-Context-Id-map) or, for a feature
pattern, calls the feature function.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `patternType`     | [`Patter              | *Optional*            |
|                       | nType`](#PatternType) |                       |
|                       |                       | Specifies a `PART`,   |
|                       |                       | `FEATURE`, or `FACE`  |
|                       |                       | pattern. Default is   |
|                       |                       | `PART`.               |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `entities`        | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Required if          |
|                       |                       | `patternType` is      |
|                       |                       | `PART`*               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | The parts to pattern. |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `qCr                |
|                       |                       | eatedBy(id + "extrude |
|                       |                       | 1", EntityType.BODY)` |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `faces`           | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Required if          |
|                       |                       | `patternType` is      |
|                       |                       | `FACE`*               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | The faces to pattern. |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`Featur              | *Required if          |
|    `instanceFunction` | eList`](#FeatureList) | `patternType` is      |
|                       |                       | `FEATURE`*            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | The                   |
|                       |                       | [`Featur              |
|                       |                       | eList`](#FeatureList) |
|                       |                       | of the features to    |
|                       |                       | pattern.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `edges`           | [`Query`](#Query)     | A [`Query`](#Query)   |
|                       |                       | for a set of edges to |
|                       |                       | pattern along. The    |
|                       |                       | edges must form a     |
|                       |                       | continuous path.      |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `spacingType`     | [`CurvePatt           | Specifies the type of |
|                       | ernSpacingType`](#Cur | spacing between       |
|                       | vePatternSpacingType) | pattern entities.     |
|                       |                       | Default is `EQUAL`.   |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `distance`        | [`ValueWithUni        | *Required if          |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | `spacingType` is      |
|                       |                       | `DISTANCE`*           |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | The distance between  |
|                       |                       | each pattern entity.  |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `1.0 * inch` to     |
|                       |                       | > space the pattern   |
|                       |                       | > entities 1 inch     |
|                       |                       | > apart.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `instanceCount`   | [`number`](/FsDoc/v   | The resulting number  |
|                       | ariables.html#number) | of pattern entities.  |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `2` to have 2       |
|                       |                       | > resulting pattern   |
|                       |                       | > entities (including |
|                       |                       | > the seed).          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `keepOrientation` | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `true` for the      |
|                       |                       | > pattern entities to |
|                       |                       | > keep the            |
|                       |                       | > orientation of the  |
|                       |                       | > seed                |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `false` for the     |
|                       |                       | > pattern entities to |
|                       |                       | > reorient along the  |
|                       |                       | > path (default)      |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `operationType`   | [`New                 | *Optional*            |
|                       | BodyOperationType`](# |                       |
|                       | NewBodyOperationType) | Specifies how the     |
|                       |                       | newly created body    |
|                       |                       | will be merged with   |
|                       |                       | existing bodies.      |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `defaultScope`    | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `true` to merge     |
|                       |                       | > with all other      |
|                       |                       | > bodies              |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `false` to merge    |
|                       |                       | > with `booleanScope` |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `booleanScope`    | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Required if          |
|                       |                       | `defaultScope` is     |
|                       |                       | `false`*              |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | The specified bodies  |
|                       |                       | to merge with.        |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `skipInstances`   | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | Whether to exclude    |
|                       |                       | certain instances of  |
|                       |                       | the pattern.          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | *Required if          |
|    `skippedInstances` | variables.html#array) | `skipInstances` is    |
|                       |                       | `true`*               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | Which instances of    |
|                       |                       | the pattern to skip.  |
|                       |                       | Each is denoted by a  |
|                       |                       | single positive       |
|                       |                       | index.                |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `[{ index           |
|                       |                       | : 2 }, { index: 5 }]` |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
