sheet. Returns information about arc ids and arc end position.

[getDefaultBendReliefWidth](#getDefaultBendReliefWidth-Context-Query)
(context is [Context](#Context), model is [Query](#Query))
returns [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits)

Returns the width of a default bend relief for a sheet metal model. In
the case of a tear, returns the minimal clearance.

## [shell](#module-shell.fs) {#module-shell.fs .file-header}

[shell](#shell-Context-Id-map) (context is [Context](#Context),
id is [Id](#Id), definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Feature performing an [`opShell`](#opShell-Context-Id-map).

## [splitpart](#module-splitpart.fs) {#module-splitpart.fs .file-header}

[SplitType](#SplitType) enum

Defines whether a `split` should split whole parts, or just faces.

  Value    Description
  -------- -------------
  `PART`   
  `FACE`   

[splitPart](#splitPart-Context-Id-map) (context is [Context](#Context),
id is [Id](#Id), definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Feature performing an [`opSplitPart`](#opSplitPart-Context-Id-map).

## [sweep](#module-sweep.fs) {#module-sweep.fs .file-header}

[sweep](#sweep-Context-Id-map) (context is [Context](#Context),
id is [Id](#Id), definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Feature performing an [`opSweep`](#opSweep-Context-Id-map), followed by
an [`opBoolean`](#opBoolean-Context-Id-map). For simple sweeps, prefer
using [`opSweep`](#opSweep-Context-Id-map) directly.

## [tag](#module-tag.fs) {#module-tag.fs .file-header}

[TagPurpose](#TagPurpose) enum

Defines the kind of entity being tagged in the feature.

  Value         Description
  ------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `FRAME`       Tag a frame profile with metadata. The metadata will be displayed in the cut list for frames derived from the tagged profile.
  `FORM`        Tag a form with metadata. The metadata will be used for formed features derived from the form.
  `PCB_HOLES`   Tag holes or cutouts with ECAD metadata. The metadata will be used for importing to PCB Studio.

[PcbEntityType](#PcbEntityType) enum

Type of PCB entities being tagged by a Tag feature.

  Value      Description
  ---------- -----------------------------------------------------
  `HOLE`     Entities will be imported as holes in PCB Studio.
  `CUTOUT`   Entities will be imported as cutouts in PCB Studio.

[PcbPlatedHolePurpose](#PcbPlatedHolePurpose) enum

Predefined purpose values for a plated PCB hole tagged by a Tag feature.

  Value           Description
  --------------- -------------------------------------------
  `NONE`          No purpose specified.
  `VIA`           Hole is associated with a conductive via.
  `THERMAL_VIA`   Hole is associated with a thermal via.
  `PIN`           Hole is associated with a component pin.
  `CUSTOM`        User-defined purpose.

[PcbNonPlatedHolePurpose](#PcbNonPlatedHolePurpose) enum

Predefined purpose values for a non-plated PCB hole tagged by a Tag
feature.

  Value        Description
  ------------ -------------------------------------
  `NONE`       No purpose specified.
  `MOUNTING`   Hole is used for mounting purposes.
  `TOOLING`    Hole is used for tooling purposes.
  `CUSTOM`     User-defined purpose.

[PcbHoleOwner](#PcbHoleOwner) enum

Owner of a PCB hole tagged by a Tag feature.

  Value       Description
  ----------- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `UNOWNED`   Hole can be modified in either system.
  `ECAD`      Hole is owned by the Electrical system and should not be modified in the Mechanical system.
  `MCAD`      Hole is owned by the Mechanical system and should not be modified in the Electrical system.

[tag](#tag-Context-Id-map) (context is [Context](#Context),
id is [Id](#Id), definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Tag an entity with metadata. The metadata will be used for formed and
frame features or imported to PCB Studio.

## [thicken](#module-thicken.fs) {#module-thicken.fs .file-header}

[thicken](#thicken-Context-Id-map) (context is [Context](#Context),
id is [Id](#Id), definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Feature performing an [`opThicken`](#opThicken-Context-Id-map), followed
by an [`opBoolean`](#opBoolean-Context-Id-map). For simple thickens,
prefer using [`opThicken`](#opThicken-Context-Id-map) directly.

## [transformCopy](#module-transformCopy.fs) {#module-transformCopy.fs .file-header}

[TransformType](#TransformType) enum

Defines how a the transform for a `transform` feature should be
specified. SCALE_UNIFORMLY actually also supports non-uniform scaling.

  Value                         Description
  ----------------------------- -------------
  `TRANSLATION_ENTITY`          
  `TRANSLATION_DISTANCE`        
  `TRANSLATION_3D`              
  `TRANSFORM_MATE_CONNECTORS`   
  `ROTATION`                    
  `COPY`                        
  `SCALE_UNIFORMLY`             

[transform](#transform-Context-Id-map) (context is [Context](#Context),
id is [Id](#Id), definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Move and/or rotate bodies or mate connectors with a single
[`Transform`](#Transform), constructed with input according to the
selected [`TransformType`](#TransformType).

Internally, performs an [`opTransform`](#opTransform-Context-Id-map)
when not copying, and an [`opPattern`](#opPattern-Context-Id-map) when
copying. For simple transforms, prefer calling
[`opTransform`](#opTransform-Context-Id-map) or
[`opPattern`](#opPattern-Context-Id-map) directly.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `entities`        | [`Query`](#Query)     | The bodies or mate    |
|                       |                       | connectors to         |
|                       |                       | transform. An error   |
|                       |                       | is thrown if anything |
|                       |                       | else is included.     |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `qCr                |
|                       |                       | eatedBy(id + "extrude |
|                       |                       | 1", EntityType.BODY)` |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `transformType`   | [`TransformT          | Defines how the       |
|                       | ype`](#TransformType) | transform type should |
|                       |                       | be specified.         |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `Transfor           |
|                       |                       | mType.TRANSLATION_3D` |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `dx`              | [`ValueWithUni        | *Required if          |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `Transform            |
|                       |                       | Type.TRANSLATION_3D`* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | A value with length   |
|                       |                       | units specifying the  |
|                       |                       | distance to move in   |
|                       |                       | the world `x`         |
|                       |                       | direction.            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `1 * inch`          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `dy`              | [`ValueWithUni        | *Required if          |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `Transform            |
|                       |                       | Type.TRANSLATION_3D`* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | A value with length   |
|                       |                       | units specifying the  |
|                       |                       | distance to move in   |
|                       |                       | the world `y`         |
|                       |                       | direction.            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `1 * inch`          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `dz`              | [`ValueWithUni        | *Required if          |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `Transform            |
|                       |                       | Type.TRANSLATION_3D`* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | A value with length   |
|                       |                       | units specifying the  |
|                       |                       | distance to move in   |
|                       |                       | the world `z`         |
|                       |                       | direction.            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `1 * inch`          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `transformLine`   | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Required if          |
|                       |                       | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `TransformType        |
|                       |                       | .TRANSLATION_ENTITY`* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | A `Query` for either  |
|                       |                       | a single line or a    |
|                       |                       | pair of points,       |
|                       |                       | specifying the        |
|                       |                       | direction and         |
|                       |                       | distance to           |
|                       |                       | transform.            |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `opp              | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Required if          |
| ositeDirectionEntity` | riables.html#boolean) | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `TransformType        |
|                       |                       | .TRANSLATION_ENTITY`* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `true` to flip the  |
|                       |                       | > transform           |
|                       |                       | > direction.          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `transformAxis`   | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Required if          |
|                       |                       | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `Tra                  |
|                       |                       | nsformType.ROTATION`* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | A `Query` for a line, |
|                       |                       | cylinder, etc. to     |
|                       |                       | specify the transform |
|                       |                       | direction.            |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `angle`           | [`ValueWithUni        | *Required if          |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `Tra                  |
|                       |                       | nsformType.ROTATION`* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | A value with angle    |
|                       |                       | units specifying the  |
|                       |                       | angle to rotate.      |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Required if          |
|  `transformDirection` |                       | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `TransformType.T      |
|                       |                       | RANSLATION_DISTANCE`* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | A `Query` for either  |
|                       |                       | a single line or a    |
|                       |                       | pair of points,       |
|                       |                       | specifying the        |
|                       |                       | direction to          |
|                       |                       | transform.            |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `distance`        | [`ValueWithUni        | *Required if          |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `TransformType.T      |
|                       |                       | RANSLATION_DISTANCE`* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | A value with length   |
|                       |                       | units specifying the  |
|                       |                       | distance to move.     |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Required if          |
|   `oppositeDirection` | riables.html#boolean) | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `TransformType.       |
|                       |                       | TRANSLATION_DISTANCE` |
|                       |                       | or                    |
|                       |                       | `Tra                  |
|                       |                       | nsformType.ROTATION`* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `true` to transform |
|                       |                       | > in the opposite     |
|                       |                       | > direction.          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `uniform`         | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | True if the scale is  |
|                       |                       | uniform in all        |
|                       |                       | directions (default)  |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `scale`           | [`number`](/FsDoc/v   | *Required if          |
|                       | ariables.html#number) | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `Transform            |
|                       |                       | Type.SCALE_UNIFORMLY` |
|                       |                       | and `uniform` is      |
|                       |                       | true*                 |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | A positive real       |
|                       |                       | number specifying the |
|                       |                       | scale factor.         |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `scalePoint`      | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Required if          |
|                       |                       | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `TransformT           |
|                       |                       | ype.SCALE_UNIFORMLY`* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | A point specifying    |
|                       |                       | the center of the     |
|                       |                       | scale or a mate       |
|                       |                       | connector specifying  |
|                       |                       | the coordinate system |
|                       |                       | for a non-uniform     |
|                       |                       | scale.                |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `scaleX`          | [`number`](/FsDoc/v   | *Required if          |
|                       | ariables.html#number) | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `Transform            |
|                       |                       | Type.SCALE_UNIFORMLY` |
|                       |                       | and `uniform` is      |
|                       |                       | false*                |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `scaleY`          | [`number`](/FsDoc/v   | *Required if          |
|                       | ariables.html#number) | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `Transform            |
|                       |                       | Type.SCALE_UNIFORMLY` |
|                       |                       | and `uniform` is      |
|                       |                       | false*                |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `scaleZ`          | [`number`](/FsDoc/v   | *Required if          |
|                       | ariables.html#number) | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `Transform            |
|                       |                       | Type.SCALE_UNIFORMLY` |
|                       |                       | and `uniform` is      |
|                       |                       | false*                |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `baseConnector`   | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Required if          |
|                       |                       | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `TransformType.TRANSF |
|                       |                       | ORM_MATE_CONNECTORS`* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | The mate connector to |
|                       |                       | transform from.       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `                 | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Required if          |
| destinationConnector` |                       | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `TransformType.TRANSF |
|                       |                       | ORM_MATE_CONNECTORS`* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | The mate connector to |
|                       |                       | transform to.         |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `oppos            | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Required if          |
| iteDirectionMateAxis` | riables.html#boolean) | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | `TransformType.TRANSF |
|                       |                       | ORM_MATE_CONNECTORS`* |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`MateC               | *Required if          |
|   `secondaryAxisType` | onnectorAxisType`](#M | `transformType` is    |
|                       | ateConnectorAxisType) | `TransformType.TRANSF |
|                       |                       | ORM_MATE_CONNECTORS`* |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `makeCopy`        | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Required if          |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) | `transformType` is    |
|                       |                       | not                   |
|                       |                       | `TransformType.COPY`* |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `true` to keep the  |
|                       |                       | > original bodies.    |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[copyPart](#copyPart-Context-Id-map) (context is [Context](#Context),

## [variable](#module-variable.fs) {#module-variable.fs .file-header}

[VariableMode](#VariableMode) enum

Whether the variable is measured, assigned or from table.

  Value        Description
  ------------ -------------
  `ASSIGNED`   
  `MEASURED`   
  `TABLE`      

[VariableMeasurementMode](#VariableMeasurementMode) enum

Whether to measure distance or length.

  Value        Description
  ------------ -------------
  `DISTANCE`   
  `LENGTH`     
  `DIAMETER`   

[AxisWithCustom](#AxisWithCustom) enum

Axis selection

  Value        Description
  ------------ -------------
  `DISTANCE`   
  `X`          
  `Y`          
  `Z`          
  `CUSTOM`     

[VariableMinMaxSelection](#VariableMinMaxSelection) enum

Min or max selection

  Value       Description
  ----------- -------------
  `MINIMUM`   
  `MAXIMUM`   

[AccessType](#AccessType) enum

Whether the accessType is by index, by index range, by label or all
values

  Value           Description
  --------------- -------------
  `INDEX`         
  `INDEX_RANGE`   
  `LABEL`         
  `ALL`           

[ResultType](#ResultType) enum

Whether result type is single value, array or map

  Value      Description
  ---------- -------------
  `SINGLE`   
  `ARRAY`    
  `MAP`      

[TABLE_INDEX_BOUNDS](#TABLE_INDEX_BOUNDS) const

table index bounds

[assignVariable](#assignVariable-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Feature performing a `setVariable` allowing a user to assign a
FeatureScript value to a context variable. This variable may be
retrieved within a feature by calling `getVariable`, or in a Part Studio
using `#` syntax (e.g. `#foo`) inside any parameter which allows an
expression (including the `value` parameter of another variable!)

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `mode`            | [`Variable            | Whether the variable  |
|                       | Mode`](#VariableMode) | is measured, assigned |
|                       |                       | or from import.       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `name`            | [`string`](/FsDoc/v   | Must be an            |
|                       | ariables.html#string) | identifier.           |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `description`     | [`string`](/FsDoc/v   | Description of the    |
|                       | ariables.html#string) | variable. Maximum     |
|                       |                       | length of 256         |
|                       |                       | characters.           |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `variableType`    | [`Variable            | The type of variable. |
|                       | Type`](#VariableType) | If it is not ANY, the |
|                       |                       | value is restricted   |
|                       |                       | to be a length,       |
|                       |                       | angle, or number and  |
|                       |                       | is passed through the |
|                       |                       | `lengthValue`,        |
|                       |                       | `angleValue`, or      |
|                       |                       | `numberValue` field,  |
|                       |                       | respectively.         |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `lengthValue`     | [`ValueWithUni        | Used if               |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | `variableType` is     |
|                       |                       | `LENGTH`.             |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `angleValue`      | [`ValueWithUni        | Used if               |
|                       | ts`](#ValueWithUnits) | `variableType` is     |
|                       |                       | `ANGLE`.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `numberValue`     | [`number`](/FsDoc/v   | Used if               |
|                       | ariables.html#number) | `variableType` is     |
|                       |                       | `NUMBER`.             |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `anyValue`        |                       | Used if               |
|                       |                       | `variableType` is     |
|                       |                       | `ANY`. Can be any     |
|                       |                       | immutable             |
|                       |                       | FeatureScript value,  |
|                       |                       | including a length,   |
|                       |                       | an array, or a        |
|                       |                       | function.             |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `measurementMode` | [`VariableM           | Whether to measure    |
|                       | easurementMode`](#Var | distance, length, or  |
|                       | iableMeasurementMode) | diameter.             |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `entityCouple`    | [`Query`](#Query)     | Query for distance    |
|                       |                       | mode, containing two  |
|                       |                       | entities to measure   |
|                       |                       | the distance between. |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `minmax`          | [`VariableM           | Whether to measure    |
|                       | inMaxSelection`](#Var | the minimum or        |
|                       | iableMinMaxSelection) | maximum distance.     |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `axis`            | [`AxisWithCust        | Axis to measure       |
|                       | om`](#AxisWithCustom) | distance along.       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `extendEntities`  | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | Extend selected       |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) | planes and lines out  |
|                       |                       | to infinity.          |
|                       |                       | Incompatible with     |
|                       |                       | maximum.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `measureFromAxis` | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | Measure from the axis |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) | of geometry with an   |
|                       |                       | axis, rather than     |
|                       |                       | from the edge.        |
|                       |                       | Incompatible with     |
|                       |                       | maximum.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`AxisWithCust        | Select an axis to     |
|   `componentSelector` | om`](#AxisWithCustom) | measure along.        |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `customDirection` | [`Query`](#Query)     | If componentSelector  |
|                       |                       | is CUSTOM, a query    |
|                       |                       | containing geometry   |
|                       |                       | with a directon to    |
|                       |                       | measure along.        |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `lengthEntities`  | [`Query`](#Query)     | Query for length      |
|                       |                       | mode, containing      |
|                       |                       | entities to measure   |
|                       |                       | the length of.        |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `radius`          | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | Whether to measure    |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) | the radius rather     |
|                       |                       | than the diameter.    |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `diameterEntity`  | [`Query`](#Query)     | Query for diameter    |
|                       |                       | mode, containing an   |
|                       |                       | entity to measure the |
|                       |                       | diameter of.          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[verifyVariableNameIsValid](#verifyVariableNameIsValid-string-string)
(name is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
faultyParameter is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))

Throws an error if `name` is not a valid identifier.

[verifyVariableName](#verifyVariableName-Context-string-string)
(context is [Context](#Context),
name is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
faultyParameter is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))

Throws an error if `name` is not a valid identifier or is used by a
query variable.

[makeLookupFunction](#makeLookupFunction-Context-Id)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id))
returns [function](/FsDoc/variables.html#function)

Make a function to look up variables from the given context. Used in
generated part studio code.

[measureDistance](#measureDistance-Context-map)
(context is [Context](#Context),
arg is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))
returns [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

Measure the distance between two entities.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `arg`                 | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `entities`        | [`Query`](#Query)     | a query containing    |
|                       |                       | two entities to       |
|                       |                       | measure the distance  |
|                       |                       | between.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `measureFromAxis` | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | measure from the axis |
|                       |                       | of the selected       |
|                       |                       | geometry, if an axis  |
|                       |                       | is available.         |
|                       |                       | Incompatible with the |
|                       |                       | `maximum` option.     |
|                       |                       | Defaults to `false`.  |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `customDirection` | [`Query`](#Query)     | *Optional*            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | measure the distance  |
|                       |                       | between two entities  |
|                       |                       | along a direction     |
|                       |                       | selected by this      |
|                       |                       | query.                |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `extendEntities`  | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | extend the entities   |
|                       |                       | out to infinity.      |
|                       |                       | Incompatible with the |
|                       |                       | `maximum` option.     |
|                       |                       | Defaults to `false`.  |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `maximum`         | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | measure the maximum   |
|                       |                       | distance between the  |
|                       |                       | selected entities,    |
|                       |                       | instead of the        |
|                       |                       | minimum by default.   |
|                       |                       | Defaults to `false`.  |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[selectDistanceComponent](#selectDistanceComponent-map-AxisWithCustom)
(distanceMap is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map),
componentSelector is [AxisWithCustom](#AxisWithCustom))

Returns appropriate entry in distanceMap returned from measureDistance
given the selected AxisWithCustom.

[measureDiameter](#measureDiameter-Context-map)
(context is [Context](#Context),
arg is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Measure the diameter (or radius) of the selected entity.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `arg`                 | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `entity`          | [`Query`](#Query)     | a query containing an |
|                       |                       | entity of             |
|                       |                       | GeometryType.CIRCLE,  |
|                       |                       | GeometryType.ARC,     |
|                       |                       | G                     |
|                       |                       | eometryType.CYLINDER, |
|                       |                       | or                    |
|                       |                       | GeometryType.SPHERE.  |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `radius`          | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | *Optional*            |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) |                       |
|                       |                       | measure the radius    |
|                       |                       | instead of the        |
|                       |                       | diameter. Defaults to |
|                       |                       | `false`.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

## [wrap](#module-wrap.fs) {#module-wrap.fs .file-header}

[WrapResultType](#WrapResultType) enum

Defines what type of output the Wrap feature should produce.

  Value       Description
  ----------- ------------------------------------------------------------------
  `SOLID`     The wrap operation will produce thickened solid bodies.
  `SURFACE`   The wrap operation will produce surface bodies.
  `IMPRINT`   The wrap operation will imprint edges onto the destination face.

[wrap](#wrap-Context-Id-map) (context is [Context](#Context),
id is [Id](#Id), definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Feature performing an [`opWrap`](#opWrap-Context-Id-map).

  `UP_TO_BODY`      Extrude up to the specified solid body.
  `UP_TO_VERTEX`    Extrude up to the specified vertex.
  `THROUGH_ALL`     Extrude an unspecified distance, guaranteed to be further in the extrude direction than any other solid or surface in the context.

  `INTERFERE`              Bounding topologies interfere.

  `ABUT_TOOL_OUT_TARGET`   Bounding tool abuts bounding target on outside.
  `TARGET_IN_TOOL`         Target completely inside tool.
  `TOOL_IN_TARGET`         Tool completely inside target.

## [edgeconvexitytype](#module-edgeconvexitytype.gen.fs) {#module-edgeconvexitytype.gen.fs .file-header}

[EdgeConvexityType](#EdgeConvexityType) enum

Specifies how two faces join at a given edge. This is a function of the
interior angle between the adjoining surfaces, measured inside the owner
body.

  Value        Description
  ------------ ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  `CONVEX`     The interior angle is less than 180 degrees along the whole edge.
  `CONCAVE`    The interior angle is greater than 180 degrees along the whole edge.
  `SMOOTH`     The interior angle is equal to 180 degrees along the whole edge.
  `VARIABLE`   None of the above, i.e. the edge convexity varies along the edge.

## [edgetopology](#module-edgetopology.gen.fs) {#module-edgetopology.gen.fs .file-header}

[EdgeTopology](#EdgeTopology) enum

Specifies the topology of an edge entity.

Can be used in a filter on a query parameter to only allow certain
selections:

     annotation { "Name" : "Surface edges", "Filter" : EntityType.EDGE && EdgeTopology.ONE_SIDED } definition.edges is Query; 

See also

[`qEdgeTopologyFilter`](#qEdgeTopologyFilter-Query-EdgeTopology)

  Value         Description
  ------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------
  `WIRE`        edge without adjacent faces, belonging to a wire body.
  `ONE_SIDED`   An edge adjacent to one face (e.g. the edge of a surface extrude).
  `TWO_SIDED`   An edge which joins two faces (e.g. the edge of a cube).

## [entityinferencetype](#module-entityinferencetype.gen.fs) {#module-entityinferencetype.gen.fs .file-header}

[EntityInferenceType](#EntityInferenceType) enum

Specifies how a mate connector\'s coordinate system is defined with
respect to an entity.

See also

[`mateConnector`](#mateConnector-Context-Id-map)

  Value                 Description
  --------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `POINT`               Place a mate connector at a vertex with the world coordinate system, or at the tip of a conical face with its Z-axis along the cone axis.
  `CENTROID`            Place a mate connector at the centroid of a face, with its Z-axis along the face normal at that point.
  `CENTER`              Place a mate connector at the center of a circular or elliptical edge with its Z-axis along the normal of the plane which the edge lies on, or at the center of a sphere with the world coordinate system.
  `MID_POINT`           Place a mate connector at the midpoint of an edge, with its Z-axis along the edge.
  `TOP_AXIS_POINT`      Place a mate connector at the projection of the top extreme of a cylindrical or other revolved face onto the central axis of that face, with its Z-axis along the central axis.
  `MID_AXIS_POINT`      Place a mate connector at the projection of the point midway betwen the top and bottom extremes of a cylindrical or other revolved face onto the central axis of that face, with its Z-axis along the central axis.
  `BOTTOM_AXIS_POINT`   Place a mate connector at the projection of the bottom extreme of a cylindrical or other revolved face onto the central axis of that face, with
  `LOOP_CENTER`         Place a mate connector at the center of a loop of planar edges, with its Z-axis along the normal of the plane. Only one edge of the loop needs to be selected for this inference.
  `VIRTUAL_SHARP`       Place a mate connector at locations along a loop of planar edges where a single nonlinear edge sits between two nonparallel lines and is tangent to both. The mate origin will be the projected intersection of the two lines, with its Z-axis along the normal of the plane.

## [fittolerancetables](#module-fittolerancetables.gen.fs) {#module-fittolerancetables.gen.fs .file-header}

[getFitToleranceLimits](#getFitToleranceLimits-ValueWithUnits-string-string-boolean)
(nominalSize is [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits),
standard is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
toleranceClass is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string),
isHoleBasis is [boolean](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean))
returns [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

Retrieves fit tolerance limits for a given nominal size, tolerance
class, and basis.

  Parameter          Type                                         Additional Info
  ------------------ -------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `nominalSize`      [`ValueWithUnits`](#ValueWithUnits)          The nominal size with units for which the fit tolerance limits are required.
  `standard`         [`string`](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)     The standard of the fit tolerance table (\"ANSI\" or \"ISO\").
  `toleranceClass`   [`string`](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)     The tolerance class as a string.
  `isHoleBasis`      [`boolean`](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)   Indicates whether the basis of the tolerance class is hole or shaft.

  Return type                          Description
  ------------------------------------ ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  [`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)   A map containing the fit tolerance limits. Returns an empty map if no suitable tolerance limits are found.

## [holepositionreference](#module-holepositionreference.gen.fs) {#module-holepositionreference.gen.fs .file-header}

[HolePositionReference](#HolePositionReference) enum

For a [`HoleProfile`](#HoleProfile), a reference point along the hole
axis to which the `position` of the profile is relative. To calculate
these references, the cylinder of the hole is intersected with the
`targets` of the hole. If the hole cylinder encounters a slanted or
otherwise irregular face at the intersection with the target, the
reference may by a range rather than a single point. Users may specify
that they want the end of this range by using a
[`holeProfile`](#holeProfile-HolePositionReference-ValueWithUnits-ValueWithUnits-map),
or the beginning of this range by using a
[`holeProfileBeforeReference`](#holeProfileBeforeReference-HolePositionReference-ValueWithUnits-ValueWithUnits-map);
or they may control this behavior directly by setting the
`beforeReference` flag of the `HoleProfile`.

Typically, the beginning of the reference range should be used for the
first profile of the hole, and the end of the reference range should be
used for the rest of the profiles of the hole. Using the beginning of
the reference range for the first profile ensures that when the hole
tool is cut from a target with a slanted or otherwise irregular entrance
face, the first profile is far back enough to ensure that no undesirable
overhang is left behind on the target, preventing the fastener from
entering the hole. Using the end of the reference range for the rest of
the profiles ensures that any nominal distances are measured from where
the hole cylinder fully enters the part. As an example of both of these
concepts, set the first two profiles of a
[`HoleDefinition`](#HoleDefinition) as a
[`holeProfileBeforeReference`](#holeProfileBeforeReference-HolePositionReference-ValueWithUnits-ValueWithUnits-map)
referencing `TARGET_START` with a `position` of 0 inches, and a
[`holeProfile`](#holeProfile-HolePositionReference-ValueWithUnits-ValueWithUnits-map)
referencing `TARGET_START` with a `position` of 2 inches, respectively.
If this layout of profiles is used against a target that has a slanted
entrance face, the first profile will be placed right where the cylinder
first intersects the part, ensuring that there is no overhang when the
hole tool is subtracted from the target. The second profile will be
placed 2 inches from where the hole cylinder fully enters the target,
such that the request for the hole to be 2 inches deep is understood as
the hole having a full two inches of depth in the target, exluding the
area where the hole is only partially cut into the target. Notably, to
accomplish this the first and second profiles are placed more than two
inches apart.

When calculating references, any intersection which is fully behind the
`origin` of the axis [`Line`](#Line) (i.e. the axis point) is always
ignored. Range intersections where the end of the range is ahead of the
axis point but the start of the range is behind the axis point are not
ignored.

  Value                          Description
  ------------------------------ ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `AXIS_POINT`                   When calling [`opHole`](#opHole-Context-Id-map), the user will supply one or more `axes` defining where the holes are to be placed. When using this reference, the `position` of the profile will be in reference to the `origin` of the axis [`Line`](#Line) of the hole.
  `TARGET_START`                 When using this reference, the `position` of the profile will be in reference to the closest intersection of the hole cylinder with any target.
  `LAST_TARGET_START`            For each target, find the first intersection of the hole cylinder into the target. When using this reference, the `position` of the profile will be in reference to the furthest of those intersections. Notably, this means that if certain targets are geometrically constructed such that the hole cylinder enters and exits the target multiple times, only the first of the entrances into the target is considered.
  `LAST_TARGET_END`              When using this reference, the `position` of the profile will be in reference to the furthest exit of the hole cylinder from any target.
  `UP_TO_ENTITY`                 
  `UP_TO_NEXT`                   
  `LAST_TARGET_START_IN_DEPTH`   For each target, find the first intersection of the hole cylinder into the target. When using this reference, the `position` of the profile will be in reference to the furthest of those intersections within the hole depth.

  `Y_LINEAR_LIMIT_MAX`          Maximum Y linear limit.

  `Z_LINEAR_LIMIT_MAX`          Maximum Z linear limit.

  `Y_ROTATION_LIMIT_MIN`        Minimum Y rotation limit.
  `Y_ROTATION_LIMIT_MAX`        Maximum Y rotation limit.
  `Z_ROTATION_LIMIT_MIN`        Minimum Z rotation limit.
  `Z_ROTATION_LIMIT_MAX`        Maximum Z rotation limit.
  `CONE_ANGLE_LIMIT`            Cone angle limit.
  `HOLE_DIAMETER`               Hole diameter.
  `HOLE_DEPTH`                  Hole depth.
  `HOLE_DRILL_ANGLE`            Hole drill angle.
  `HOLE_COUNTERBORE_DIAMETER`   Hole counterbore diameter.
  `HOLE_COUNTERBORE_DEPTH`      Hole counterbore depth.
  `HOLE_COUNTERSINK_DIAMETER`   Hole countersink diameter.
  `HOLE_TAP_DIAMETER`           Hole tap diameter.
  `HOLE_COUNTERSINK_ANGLE`      Hole countersink angle.
  `HOLE_TAPPED_DEPTH`           Hole tapped depth.
  `HOLE_TAP_CLEARANCE`          Hole tap clearance.
  `ALONG_X`                     Along the X-axis.
  `ALONG_Y`                     Along the Y-axis.
  `ALONG_Z`                     Along the Z-axis.
  `DOF_TRANSLATION`             Degrees of freedom translation.
  `DOF_ROTATION`                Degrees of freedom rotation.
  `DIHEDRAL_LOW`                Dihedral low.
  `DIHEDRAL_HIGH`               Dihedral high.

## [lofttopology](#module-lofttopology.gen.fs) {#module-lofttopology.gen.fs .file-header}

[LoftTopology](#LoftTopology) enum

Specifies topology option for opLoft.

  Value       Description
  ----------- ----------------------------------------------------------------
  `MINIMAL`   Minimal number of faces created.
  `COLUMNS`   One face is created for each matching set of profile segments.
  `GRID`      Faces created for COLUMNS option are split at each profile.

## [lookupTablePath](#module-lookupTablePath.fs) {#module-lookupTablePath.fs .file-header}

[LookupTablePath](#LookupTablePath) type

A `LookupTablePath` identifies a map of keys into a multi-level table.

The fields on a LookupTablePath depend on its related LookupTable.

Value

Type

Description

`LookupTablePath`

[`map`](/FsDoc/variables.html#map)

-   `entityType`

[`EntityType`](#EntityType)

*Optional*

[canBeLookupTablePath](#canBeLookupTablePath-) (value) predicate

Typecheck for [`LookupTablePath`](#LookupTablePath)

[lookupTablePath](#lookupTablePath-map)
(source is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))
returns [LookupTablePath](#LookupTablePath)

Creates a `lookupTablePath`.

[lookupTableEvaluate](#lookupTableEvaluate-string)
(expression is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))
returns [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits)

Convert a table expression in string form into a ValueWithUnits.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `expression`          | [`string`](/FsDoc/v   | Currently the         |
|                       | ariables.html#string) | following forms are   |
|                       |                       | supported:            |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `                   |
|                       |                       | {number} <*> {units}` |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `{number}/          |
|                       |                       | {number} <*> {units}` |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | > `{                  |
|                       |                       | number} <+> {number}/ |
|                       |                       | {number} <*> {units}` |
|                       |                       | > Where               |
|                       |                       | > `{n                 |
|                       |                       | umber} is <-><.>nnn>` |
|                       |                       | > or \<-\>inf         |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | EXAMPLE               |
|                       |                       |                       |
|                       |                       | >                     |
|                       |                       |  `{} indicate an enti |
|                       |                       | ty, <> indicate the c |
|                       |                       | ontents are optional` |
|                       |                       | > spaces between      |
|                       |                       | > entities are        |
|                       |                       | > optional unless     |
|                       |                       | > they are required   |
|                       |                       | > to separate         |
|                       |                       | > entities.           |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[lookupTableFixExpression](#lookupTableFixExpression-string)
(expression is [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string))
returns [string](/FsDoc/variables.html#string)

insert plus sign and parenthesis as needed to make a valid expression

[getLookupTable](#getLookupTable-map-LookupTablePath)
(table is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map),
path is [LookupTablePath](#LookupTablePath))

Find a terminal/content table from a path and convert into
expression/value form

[lookupTableGetValue](#lookupTableGetValue-) (value)
returns [ValueWithUnits](#ValueWithUnits)

Extract the value portion of expression/value maps or evaluate
expressions. value maybe an expression or a value with units

[isLookupTableViolated](#isLookupTableViolated-map-map)
(definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map),
table is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))
returns [boolean](/FsDoc/variables.html#boolean)

Test if the current definition violates the table.

## [manipulatordragtypeenum](#module-manipulatordragtypeenum.gen.fs) {#module-manipulatordragtypeenum.gen.fs .file-header}

[ManipulatorDragTypeEnum](#ManipulatorDragTypeEnum) enum

Specifies the type of manipulator being dragged.

  Value          Description
  -------------- -------------
  `NONE`         
  `LINEAR_X`     
  `LINEAR_Y`     
  `LINEAR_Z`     
  `PLANAR_XY`    
  `PLANAR_XZ`    
  `PLANAR_YZ`    
  `ROTATION_X`   
  `ROTATION_Y`   
  `ROTATION_Z`   
  `REPOSITION`   

## [manipulatorstyleenum](#module-manipulatorstyleenum.gen.fs) {#module-manipulatorstyleenum.gen.fs .file-header}

[ManipulatorStyleEnum](#ManipulatorStyleEnum) enum

Specifies the style of a manipulator intended to look unique.

See also

[`addManipulators`](#addManipulators-Context-Id-map)

  Value          Description
  -------------- -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  `DEFAULT`      the standard display of the manipulator. \*
  `SECONDARY`    the angular or linear manipulator has two arrow heads. \*
  `SIMPLE`       the display of the angular or linear manipulator is simpler. \*
  `TANGENTIAL`   the linear manipulator has two smaller arrows around a circular base.

## [manipulatortype](#module-manipulatortype.gen.fs) {#module-manipulatortype.gen.fs .file-header}

[ManipulatorType](#ManipulatorType) enum

Specifies a specific type of interactive manipulator.

  Value             Description
  ----------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `LINEAR_1D`       A single arrow which can move along a single axis. See `extrude` for an example.
  `LINEAR_3D`       A triad of perpendicular arrows which specify a 3D position. See `transformCopy` for an example.
  `ANGULAR`         A curved arrow, with two radii, which can move along a circumference to specify an angle. See `revolve` for an example.
  `FLIP`            A static arrow which can be clicked to toggle a flip direction. See `extrude` (with BoundingType.THROUGH_ALL) for an example.
  `POINTS`          A series of points which can be selected one at a time.
  `TOGGLE_POINTS`   A series of points which can each be selected individually.
  `TRIAD_FULL`      A triad of perpendicular arrows, planar and angular manipulators.

## [mateconnectoraxistype](#module-mateconnectoraxistype.gen.fs) {#module-mateconnectoraxistype.gen.fs .file-header}

[MateConnectorAxisType](#MateConnectorAxisType) enum

Defines what direction the X-axis of a mate connector should face,
relative to the reference defined. Thus, a mate connector defined with
`PLUS_Y` will differ from the selected coordinate system by a 90-degree
rotation about that coordinate system\'s Z-axis.

  Value       Description
  ----------- -------------
  `PLUS_X`    
  `PLUS_Y`    
  `MINUS_X`   
  `MINUS_Y`   

## [matedoftype](#module-matedoftype.gen.fs) {#module-matedoftype.gen.fs .file-header}

[MateDOFType](#MateDOFType) enum

Specifies a single degree of freedom of a mate.

  Value   Description
  ------- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `Tx`    Translation along the X axis.
  `Ty`    Translation along the Y axis.
  `Tz`    Translation along the Z axis.
  `Rz`    Rotation around the Z axis.
  `Ryp`   Rotation around the transformed Y axis from previous transform sequence.
  `Rzp`   Rotation around the transformed z axis from previous transform sequence .

[MoveCurveBoundaryType](#MoveCurveBoundaryType) enum

Specifies whether to trim or extend the curve.

  Value      Description
  ---------- -------------
  `TRIM`     
  `EXTEND`   

## [offsetcurvetype](#module-offsetcurvetype.gen.fs) {#module-offsetcurvetype.gen.fs .file-header}

[OffsetCurveType](#OffsetCurveType) enum

Defines what kind of offset distance is used in offset curve on face.

  Value         Description
  ------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `GEODESIC`    The distance is calculated along the faces of the body in which the selected edges lie in.
  `EUCLIDEAN`   The distance is the distance in world coordinates.

## [origincreationtype](#module-origincreationtype.gen.fs) {#module-origincreationtype.gen.fs .file-header}

[OriginCreationType](#OriginCreationType) enum

define it.

  `BETWEEN_ENTITIES`   

## [partstudioitemtype](#module-partstudioitemtype.gen.fs) {#module-partstudioitemtype.gen.fs .file-header}

[PartStudioItemType](#PartStudioItemType) enum

Enumeration of the types of items that can be selected inside a [Part
Studio reference parameter](/FsDoc/imports.html#part-studio). By default
this parameter allows selecting all of the item types below. It can be
filtered by specifying a union of any number of PartStudioItemTypes
(e.g.
`"Filter" : PartStudioItemType.SOLID || PartStudioItemType.ENTIRE_PART_STUDIO`)
in the parameter\'s annotation.

  Value                     Description
  ------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `SOLID`                   A body with `BodyType.SOLID` (i.e. a part in the parts list)
  `SURFACE`                 A non-sketch body with `BodyType.SURFACE` (i.e. a surface in the parts list)
  `WIRE`                    A non-sketch body with `BodyType.WIRE` (i.e. a curve in the parts list)
  `MESH`                    An imported mesh (i.e. a mesh in the parts list)
  `SKETCH`                  An entire sketch feature (i.e. all `POINT`, `WIRE`, and `SURFACE` bodies created by the sketch)
  `FLATTENED_SHEET_METAL`   A flattened sheet metal part (available from any Part Studio with sheet metal features)
  `ENTIRE_PART_STUDIO`      The entire Part Studio. Setting this option allows clicking the top item (with the full Part Studio\'s tumbnail and name), which sets the `partQuery` to `qEverything(EntityType.BODY)`.
  `CONSTRUCTION_PLANE`      A plane created with Plane feature or with `opPlane`
  `COMPOSITE_PART`          

## [profilecontrolmode](#module-profilecontrolmode.gen.fs) {#module-profilecontrolmode.gen.fs .file-header}

[ProfileControlMode](#ProfileControlMode) enum

Specifies how a profile should be controlled while sweeping.

  Value                Description
  -------------------- ---------------------------------------------
  `NONE`               No additional profile control.
  `KEEP_ORIENTATION`   Keep the orientation constant.
  `LOCK_FACES`         Lock the sweep operation to a set of faces.
  `LOCK_DIRECTION`     Lock the sweep operation to a direction.:

## [ruledsurfacecornertype](#module-ruledsurfacecornertype.gen.fs) {#module-ruledsurfacecornertype.gen.fs .file-header}

[RuledSurfaceCornerType](#RuledSurfaceCornerType) enum

Describes how a Ruled Surface creates corners.

  Value         Description
  ------------- ----------------------------------------------------------
  `SPUN`        Conical corners are created by spinning an edge.
  `EXTENDED`    Ruled surfaces are altered so that they meet at corners.
  `LOFTED`      Curvature continuous lofts are created patching corners.
  `NO_CORNER`   Leave corners open.

## [ruledsurfacetype](#module-ruledsurfacetype.gen.fs) {#module-ruledsurfacetype.gen.fs .file-header}

[RuledSurfaceType](#RuledSurfaceType) enum

Describes how a Ruled Surface is defined.

  Value                   Description
  ----------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `ANGLE_FROM_FACE`       Ruled surface is perpendicular to reference faces.
  `ALIGNED_WITH_VECTOR`   Ruled surface is in direction of reference direction.
  `ANGLE_FROM_VECTOR`     Ruled surface will meet a reference direction at an angle when viewed from the path tangent direction.

## [sectionpart](#module-sectionpart.fs) {#module-sectionpart.fs .file-header}

[sectionPart](#sectionPart-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Feature creating a section of a part behind a plane. Internally,
performs an [`opSplitPart`](#opSplitPart-Context-Id-map) followed by an
[`opDeleteBodies`](#opDeleteBodies-Context-Id-map).

Not exposed in the Part Studio UI.

[planeSectionPart](#planeSectionPart-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Split a set of parts with a plane and delete all bodies in front of the
face. Unlike sectionPart, bodies which are entirely behind the split
plane are retained. Any bodies not included in the target query are
deleted.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `target`          | [`Query`](#Query)     | Bodies to be split.   |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `plane`           | [`Plane`](#Plane)     | Plane that splits the |
|                       |                       | bodies. Everything on |
|                       |                       | the positive z side   |
|                       |                       | of the plane will be  |
|                       |                       | removed.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[multiplePlaneSectionPart](#multiplePlaneSectionPart-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Split a set of parts with multiple planes and delete all bodies in front
of the faces.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `target`          | [`Query`](#Query)     | Bodies to be split.   |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `sketchPlanes`    | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | Planes that splits    |
|                       | variables.html#array) | the bodies.           |
|                       |                       | Everything on the     |
|                       |                       | positive z side of    |
|                       |                       | the planes will be    |
|                       |                       | removed.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[multiplePlanesSectionTransformedParts](#multiplePlanesSectionTransformedParts-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

method for processing all part studio parts for assembly for multiple
planes section

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `targets`         | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | Bodies to be split.   |
|                       | variables.html#array) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `planes`          | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | Planes that splits    |
|                       | variables.html#array) | the bodies.           |
|                       |                       | Everything on the     |
|                       |                       | positive z side of    |
|                       |                       | the planes will be    |
|                       |                       | removed.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

[jogSectionPart](#jogSectionPart-Context-Id-map)
(context is [Context](#Context), id is [Id](#Id),
definition is [map](/FsDoc/variables.html#map))

Split a part down a jogged section line and delete all back bodies. Used
by drawings. Needs to be a feature so that drawings created by queries
can resolve. Any bodies not included in the target query are deleted.

+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| Parameter             | Type                  | Additional Info       |
+=======================+=======================+=======================+
| `definition`          | [`map`](/FsDo         |                       |
|                       | c/variables.html#map) |                       |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `target`          | [`Query`](#Query)     | Bodies to be split.   |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `sketchPlane`     | [`Plane`](#Plane)     | Plane that the jog    |
|                       |                       | line will be drawn in |
|                       |                       | and extruded normal   |
|                       |                       | to. Everything on the |
|                       |                       | positive x side of    |
|                       |                       | the jog line will be  |
|                       |                       | removed.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `jogPoints`       | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | Points that the       |
|                       | variables.html#array) | cutting line goes     |
|                       |                       | through in world      |
|                       |                       | coordinates.          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | Whether or not it is  |
|    `isPartialSection` | riables.html#boolean) | a partial section     |
|                       |                       | cut.                  |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `keepSketches`    | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | Whether or not        |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) | sketches will be kept |
|                       |                       | in the section cut    |
|                       |                       | results.              |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `isBrokenOut`     | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | Whether or not it is  |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) | a broken-out section  |
|                       |                       | cut.                  |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `isCropView`      | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | Whether or not it is  |
|                       | riables.html#boolean) | a crop section cut.   |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `b                | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | Array of the number   |
| rokenOutPointNumbers` | variables.html#array) | of spline points of   |
|                       |                       | each broken-out       |
|                       |                       | section cut.          |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `br               | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | Array of end          |
| okenOutEndConditions` | variables.html#array) | conditions of each    |
|                       |                       | broken-out section    |
|                       |                       | cut.                  |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -   `offsetPoints`    | [`array`](/FsDoc/     | Array of points for   |
|                       | variables.html#array) | offsetting the        |
|                       |                       | section lines.        |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+
| -                     | [`boolean`](/FsDoc/va | Whether or not it is  |
|    `isAlignedSection` | riables.html#boolean) | an aligned section    |
|                       |                       | cut.                  |
+-----------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------+

## [smcornertype](#module-smcornertype.gen.fs) {#module-smcornertype.gen.fs .file-header}

[SMCornerType](#SMCornerType) enum

Specifies the type of corner at a vertex of a sheet metal model.

  Value             Description
  ----------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `OPEN_CORNER`     The corner has more than two bends or, when folded the edges of the metal do not meet.
  `CLOSED_CORNER`   The corner has two bends and when folded the edge of the metal meet.
  `BEND_END`        The \'corner\' is the end of a bend and there may be bend relief.
  `NOT_A_CORNER`    The vertex is not associated with a corner

  `KEEP_FRONT`   
  `KEEP_BACK`

## [tessellatedloftreturnstatus](#module-tessellatedloftreturnstatus.gen.fs) {#module-tessellatedloftreturnstatus.gen.fs .file-header}

[TessellatedLoftReturnStatus](#TessellatedLoftReturnStatus) enum

Enumerates failure modes for tessellated loft.

  Value                                 Description
  ------------------------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  `OK`                                  No error.
  `CRISS_CROSSED_MATCHES`               At least two matches cross each other.
  `UNDERNOURISHED_MATCHES`              At least one match consists of fewer than 2 match points.
  `NON_CONSECUTIVE_MATCHES`             A match jumps across a profile without specifying a match point.
  `MATCHING_CYLINDER_CREATION_FAILED`   Most likely due to two profiles touching.
  `NON_CURVE_INPUT`                     Only curves are allowed within profiles.
  `UNIDENTIFIED`                        Some unknown error occurred.
  `INVALID_MATCH_POINT_INDEX`           At least one of the match indices points to a non-existent match point.
  `INVALID_MATCH_POINT`                 At least one match point is specified on non-profile topology.
